## PAGE 00001: (2026x Refresh1) Accessing and modifying model element properties

- page_id: `303989441`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989441/2026x+Refresh1+Accessing+and+modifying+model+element+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:37.906+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with UML model
- labels: ['container']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Element properties can be accessed with simple setters and getters. For example *NamedElement.name* property:

```java

```

##### Container Properties

Our modeling tools use a composite structure of the model.

Every model element is a container and contains its own children and knows about its own parent.

A model element parent can be accessed with the *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element.getOwner()***method. Owned children can be received with the *Element.getOwnedElement()*method. Different types of children are stored in separate container properties.

You can access these container properties by names that are described in the UML specification. The *getOwnedElement()*method collects all children from all inner container properties.

The container properties modification and iteration is straightforward using the *java.util.Collection*interface. Property change events are fired automatically when container properties are modified.

Containers implement subsets and unions constraints from the UML metamodel specification. This explains how the modification of one container can affect other containers. Make sure you understand subsets and unions in the UML metamodel. If you want to add some inner Element to the union collection, you need to add it into a specific subset of *union.*

Some containers are read-only. This is true for the most of DERIVED UML metamodel properties.

Some derived properties are editable. For example, *Element.ownedElement*is editable.

It is enough to set one UML meta-association property value and an opposite property will be set too. For example, adding a *Class*into a *Package*can be done in two ways:

```java

```

or

```java

```

##### Accessing elements in container properties

The following example illustrates retrieving children of model elements:

```java

```

##### Modifying elements in container

Use standard *java.util.Collection or *java.util.List**methods:

```java
().add(child);
    modelElement.get().remove(child);]]>
```

##### Navigable opposite references

References between elements are defined in 'pairs' - for each primary navigable reference there is an opposite reference. Majority of those opposite references are not navigable (are not exposed in opposite metaclass).

For example *TypedElement* references it's *Type*by reference*type*, but *Type* does not expose *TypedElements* typed by it. Such approach is valid semantically, but makes model traversing challenging.

Our UML metamodel implementation does not follow this restriction and exposes all opposite references.

Some opposite references have 'friendly' names defined by UML specification.

For example *NamedElement.clientDependency* opposite reference name is *Dependency.client*.

Other opposite reference names are constructed using a pattern *_<primaryReferenceDefiningMetaclassName>Of<primaryReferenceName>*. Such naming pattern provides different names for these opposite references.

For example, there is a primary reference *type* defined in *TypedElement*. The opposite reference is defined in *Type* and has the name *_typedElementOfType.*

*[IMAGE alt='' src='']*

Setters and getters are provided for each opposite reference. For example:

```java

```

## PAGE 00002: (2026x Refresh1) Accessing dependency matrix data

- page_id: `303989015`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989015/2026x+Refresh1+Accessing+dependency+matrix+data
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:12.789+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Working with Dependency Matrix
- labels: ['matrix-data-helper', 'dependency-extractor']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Internal data of a dependency matrix can be used outside the dependency matrix component. You can export the data to any required format.

Use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.MatrixDataHelper* class to get the data component of a dependency matrix. You will get all row and column elements and access all cell information. You can also check, if the dependency matrix is empty.

The *MatrixDataHelper* class has three methods that are to be used to access data of the matrix. First of all you must check, if the matrix is already built (building the matrix means collecting all the needed data from the model). In some cases, when settings are changed or a lot of model changes occur, you have to rebuild the matrix to get the up-to-date information. Thus the best pattern to use is as follows:

```java

```

****

The duration of the build process depends on both the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.DependencyExtractor* implementation and the size of the matrix.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

You can export the data to any required format.</p><p>Use the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.MatrixDataHelper</em> class to get the data component of a dependency matrix. You will get all row and column elements and access all cell information. You can also check, if the dependency matrix is empty.</p><p>The <em>MatrixDataHelper</em> class has three methods that are to be used to access data of the matrix. First of all you must check, if the matrix is already built (building the matrix means collecting all the needed data from the model). In some cases, when settings are changed or a lot of model changes occur, you have to rebuild the matrix to get the up-to-date information. Thus the best pattern to use is as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23aa6fb2-b457-4ac4-be52-13cb70e60717"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    MatrixData matrixData;
    if (MatrixDataHelper.isRebuildNeeded(matrix))
    {
        matrixData = MatrixDataHelper.buildMatrix(matrix);
    }
    else
    {
        matrixData = MatrixDataHelper.getMatrixData(matrix);
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span><strong><br /></strong></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="249c2222-0a33-416c-9360-e744c28bfc11"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The duration of the build process depends on both the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.DependencyExtractor</em> implementation and the size of the matrix.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989038 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00003: (2026x Refresh1) Action API

- page_id: `303989038`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989038/2026x+Refresh1+Action+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:14.475+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 > (2026x Refresh1) Alf Plugin Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *AlfActionUtil* class also includes a set of static methods that perform operations over the entire active Project. These are:

- parse (with no argument), which parses the Alf bodies of all Elements in the active Project that are annotated as needing recompilation.
- parseAll, which parses all Alf text in the active Project, but does not do any mapping to UML.
- build, which does a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Building and cleaning' space='MAA'] of the Alf bodies of all elements in the active project that are annotated as needing recompilation and, optionally, also elements annotated with compilation errors (depending on its includeErrorAnnotated argument).
- clean , which deletes all auxiliary template bindings and does a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Building and cleaning' space='MAA'] of all Alf text in the active project.

In all cases, dependencies are registered for all parsed Elements and any errors are recorded as error Annotations on relevant Elements (see also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='The Alf editor' space='MAA'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Management' space='MAA']).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <em>AlfActionUtil</em> class also includes a set of static methods that perform operations over the entire active Project. These are:</p><ul><li><em>parse</em> (with no argument), which parses the Alf bodies of all Elements in the active Project that are annotated as needing recompilation.</li><li><em>parseAll,</em> which parses all Alf text in the active Project, but does not do any mapping to UML.</li><li><em>build,</em> which does a <ac:link ac:anchor="build-project"><ri:page ri:space-key="MAA" ri:content-title="Building and cleaning" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[normal build]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the Alf bodies of all elements in the active project that are annotated as needing recompilation and, optionally, also elements annotated with compilation errors (depending on its <em>includeErrorAnnotated</em> argument).</li><li><em>clean</em><em>,</em> which deletes all auxiliary template bindings and does a <ac:link ac:anchor="clean-project"><ri:page ri:space-key="MAA" ri:content-title="Building and cleaning" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[clean build]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of all Alf text in the active project.</li></ul><p>In all cases, dependencies are registered for all parsed Elements and any errors are recorded as error Annotations on relevant Elements (see also <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MAA" ri:content-title="The Alf editor" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MAA" ri:content-title="Dependency Management" /></ac:link>).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989555 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00004: (2026x Refresh1) Actions Utility Class

- page_id: `303989555`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989555/2026x+Refresh1+Actions+Utility+Class
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:41.721+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 > (2026x Refresh1) Working With Model > (2026x Refresh1) Utilities and Helpers
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The `[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.Actions](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/Actions.html)` utility class provides a unified API for **identifying, classifying, and working with SysML v2 actions**.

It supports both:

- `ActionDefinition` — reusable action definitions
- `ActionUsage` — concrete usages of actions within behaviors or structures

and their common subtypes such as:

- If actions
- Loop actions (for-loop, while-loop)
- Assignment actions
- Accept / Send actions
- Terminate actions

In addition, the class provides convenience methods for:

- retrieving actions from types
- identifying special semantic actions (e.g., start , done )
- working with input parameters of [ActionUsage](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/ActionUsage.html)

#### **Understanding Actions in SysML v2**

In SysML v2, actions are not isolated elements. They are:

- features owned by types (definitions or usages)
- part of behavioral structures
- often connected through parameters and flows

Because of this, working with actions typically involves:

- identifying their exact subtype
- navigating ownership (owned vs inherited actions)
- handling parameters explicitly

The `[Actions](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/Actions.html)` utility class centralizes these recurring patterns.

#### **Identifying Special Actions**

Some actions have special execution semantics (e.g., control flow or system interaction). 
Use `[isSpecialAction](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/Actions.html#isSpecialAction(org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass))()` to detect them.

##### Includes:

- [ControlNode](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/ControlNode.html)
- [IfActionUsage](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/IfActionUsage.html)
- [LoopActionUsage](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/LoopActionUsage.html)
- [AssignmentActionUsage](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/AssignmentActionUsage.html)
- [AcceptActionUsage](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/AcceptActionUsage.html)
- [SendActionUsage](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/SendActionUsage.html)
- [TerminateActionUsage](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/TerminateActionUsage.html)

```java

```

Use this when:

- building generic processors for actions
- filtering out control-flow or system-level actions

#### **If/Loop Action Identification**

These helpers allow precise classification of conditional and iterative actions.

##### Available methods:

- [isIfOrLoopActionUsage](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/Actions.html#isIfOrLoopActionUsage(org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass))(eClass)
- [isIfActionUsage](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/Actions.html#isIfActionUsage(org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass))(eClass)
- [isForLoopActionUsage](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/Actions.html#isForLoopActionUsage(org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass))(eClass)
- [isWhileLoopActionUsage](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/Actions.html#isWhileLoopActionUsage(org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass))(eClass)

```java

```

This is especially useful when:

- analyzing behavioral logic
- transforming or exporting models
- building validation rules

#### **Identifying Action Usages and Definitions**

Actions exist in two primary forms:

- `ActionDefinition`
- `ActionUsage`

Use the following helpers to detect either:

```java

```

This is useful for:

- generic model traversal
- tooling that should work for both definitions and usages

#### **Retrieving Actions from Types**

Actions are typically owned by types (definitions or usages).

##### All actions (including inherited)

```java
actions = Actions.getActions(type);]]>
```

Returns:

- all `ActionUsage` elements
- including inherited and referenced ones

##### Owned actions only

```java
ownedActions = Actions.getOwnedActions(type);]]>
```

Behavior:

- For `Definition` → returns ownedAction
- For `Usage` → returns nestedAction

Use this when:

- you want only locally defined behavior
- you need to avoid inherited elements

#### **Start/Done Action Identification**

Some actions represent lifecycle semantics (e.g., *start*, *done*). 
These are resolved using the **standard Actions library** and redefinition logic.

```java

```

These checks:

- rely on [ActionsLibrary](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/libraries/standard/ActionsLibrary.html)
- support redefinition (not just exact matches)

Use this for:

- execution modeling
- lifecycle interpretation
- simulation or analysis tooling

#### **Working with Input Parameters**

Action inputs are represented as `[Feature](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Feature.html)` elements and accessed by index.

##### Get or create parameter

```java

```

- Creates the parameter if it does not exist
- Ensures consistent parameter structure

##### Get existing parameter only

```java

```

- Returns null if not present
- Does not modify the model

#### **Key Takeaways**

- Actions in SysML v2 are typed, structured elements , not simple nodes
- The [Actions](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/Actions.html) utility class helps you:
  - classify action types
  - navigate ownership
  - detect semantic actions (start/done)
  - manage parameters safely
- It significantly reduces boilerplate and error-prone type checks

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/Actions.html">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.Actions</a></code> utility class provides a unified API for <strong>identifying, classifying, and working with SysML v2 actions</strong>.</p><p>It supports both:</p><ul><li data-uuid="d5af9f12-a21b-4244-95c6-2dbe5da0bbee"><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/ActionDefinition.html"><code>ActionDefinition</code> </a>— reusable action definitions</li><li data-uuid="171ca9df-188e-4966-b5b1-3e06543755e1"><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/ActionUsage.html"><code>ActionUsage</code> </a>— concrete usages of actions within behaviors or structures</li></ul><p>and their common subtypes such as:</p><ul><li data-uuid="4ced9593-3938-4ade-a4e7-b5f24b8c1ea2">If actions</li><li data-uuid="2cdf30c3-3d56-4d95-a2f3-78a85c43cf0d">Loop actions (for-loop, while-loop)</li><li data-uuid="aa639d85-2b68-46bd-83df-b5ee927b7daa">Assignment actions</li><li data-uuid="9bbee67a-aae6-4070-9805-6aa8fc47bca5">Accept / Send actions</li><li data-uuid="303bab06-4096-4e7c-a0d2-350ea6413f08">Terminate actions</li></ul><p>In addition, the class provides convenience methods for:</p><ul><li data-uuid="67fac345-fb56-4f37-89a9-d7878f6ad19f">retrieving actions from types</li><li data-uuid="289facd6-0daf-426d-83f7-a4c3adcf45b6">identifying special semantic actions (e.g., <em>start</em>, <em>done</em>)</li><li data-uuid="d747ad9a-579b-49e1-b13a-f9b91456b6a6">working with input parameters of <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/ActionUsage.html">ActionUsage</a></code></li></ul><h2><strong>Understanding Actions in SysML v2</strong></h2><p>In SysML v2, actions are not isolated elements. They are:</p><ul><li data-uuid="6ff835ca-ed27-452d-b1a4-09fc75e06e7e"><strong>features owned by types</strong> (definitions or usages)</li><li data-uuid="d2eea23e-c0e5-4db2-80fa-90d1189f19ad">part of <strong>behavioral structures</strong></li><li data-uuid="ff1cf4cb-2874-4a66-ac94-78383356a64a">often connected through <strong>parameters and flows</strong></li></ul><p>Because of this, working with actions typically involves:</p><ul><li data-uuid="f6feffc6-9b34-4ae5-bdde-f061ccba5783">identifying their exact subtype</li><li data-uuid="6dd0afb9-e38c-4356-a00e-948bfc6cee66">navigating ownership (owned vs inherited actions)</li><li data-uuid="4206f7e8-400a-447f-a29a-dbaef87826d6">handling parameters explicitly</li></ul><p>The <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/Actions.html">Actions</a></code> utility class centralizes these recurring patterns.</p><h2><strong>Identifying Special Actions </strong></h2><p>Some actions have special execution semantics (e.g., control flow or system interaction).<br />Use <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/Actions.html#isSpecialAction(org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass)">isSpecialAction</a>()</code> to detect them.</p><h3>Includes:</h3><ul><li data-uuid="55b35cba-d494-443a-9db5-ca2941e2ed83"><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/ControlNode.html">ControlNode</a></code></li><li data-uuid="3d04d2b2-6d6e-4735-92bd-5c989156554f"><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/IfActionUsage.html">IfActionUsage</a></code></li><li data-uuid="52f9ad24-ce3b-49c0-b26c-e4fc0258f16d"><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/LoopActionUsage.html">LoopActionUsage</a></code></li><li data-uuid="bab7ad2f-ef26-44be-922e-4541c5a7334b"><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/AssignmentActionUsage.html">AssignmentActionUsage</a></code></li><li data-uuid="73ddfb20-000a-457d-ae23-dce83ffe3313"><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/AcceptActionUsage.html">AcceptActionUsage</a></code></li><li data-uuid="aeb3feae-8abe-486c-9b51-4cd639f0fb6d"><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/SendActionUsage.html">SendActionUsage</a></code></li><li data-uuid="29138d5d-51c4-493f-b000-66145958c23c"><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/TerminateActionUsage.html">TerminateActionUsage</a></code></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="96666544-c579-448c-ade5-af8908ae1540"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.Actions;
import org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass;

EClass myEClass = ...;

boolean isSpecial = Actions.isSpecialAction(myEClass);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Use this when:</p><ul><li data-uuid="d25b9fbf-0d1d-41c9-a7b5-55a81a54d5d3">building generic processors for actions</li><li data-uuid="be067172-b765-4e7f-9086-d710add2de34">filtering out control-flow or system-level actions</li></ul><h2><strong>If/Loop Action Identification</strong></h2><p>These helpers allow precise classification of conditional and iterative actions.</p><h3>Available methods:</h3><ul><li data-uuid="3a201e36-6efa-44c6-92e7-a3e0d061b6f4"><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/Actions.html#isIfOrLoopActionUsage(org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass)">isIfOrLoopActionUsage</a>(eClass)</code></li><li data-uuid="2f65694f-5575-4181-8e6c-18f2e49cb944"><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/Actions.html#isIfActionUsage(org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass)">isIfActionUsage</a>(eClass)</code></li><li data-uuid="49585ece-443b-4e8d-885b-f07775b5c3a5"><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/Actions.html#isForLoopActionUsage(org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass)">isForLoopActionUsage</a>(eClass)</code></li><li data-uuid="dbf0d348-5433-4518-afac-90bbcc20d96e"><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/Actions.html#isWhileLoopActionUsage(org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass)">isWhileLoopActionUsage</a>(eClass)</code></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60b18287-ff11-452f-bdad-519cbc3c1828"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.Actions;
import org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass;

Eclass myEClass = ...;

boolean isConditional = Actions.isIfOrLoopActionUsage(myEClass);
boolean isIf = Actions.isIfActionUsage(myEClass);
boolean isFor = Actions.isForLoopActionUsage(myEClass);
boolean isWhile = Actions.isWhileLoopActionUsage(myEClass);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This is especially useful when:</p><ul><li data-uuid="d86e0d75-06f8-48a9-a551-12b6de48a2f1">analyzing behavioral logic</li><li data-uuid="bf4146a1-6b80-44de-8595-b2b363b228db">transforming or exporting models</li><li data-uuid="80c7b003-6ef8-42f8-b3d7-5749f0f97a5c">building validation rules</li></ul><h2><strong>Identifying Action Usages and Definitions</strong></h2><p>Actions exist in two primary forms:</p><ul><li data-uuid="523c5ed5-f64a-49ea-87c0-31af9129f76a"><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/ActionDefinition.html"><code>ActionDefinition</code></a></li><li data-uuid="a9550a86-9813-4577-97ef-e795f0aa50f5"><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/ActionUsage.html"><code>ActionUsage</code></a></li></ul><p>Use the following helpers to detect either:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67447350-b806-4ea7-b915-6d0e6c5817c4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element;
import org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass;

Element element = ...;
EClass eClass = ...;

boolean isActionElement = Actions.isActionUsageOrDefinition(element);
boolean isActionClass = Actions.isActionUsageOrDefinition(eClass);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This is useful for:</p><ul><li data-uuid="bb3f576e-82e8-4b1b-b0dc-67ef86d27c6d">generic model traversal</li><li data-uuid="7afc6bcf-fe18-4b01-bbd3-2470463a3e8d">tooling that should work for both definitions and usages</li></ul><h2><strong>Retrieving Actions from Types</strong></h2><p>Actions are typically owned by types (definitions or usages).</p><h3>All actions (including inherited)</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b1172a7-a767-4504-bef2-16eef04d799d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type;

Type type = ...;

List<ActionUsage> actions = Actions.getActions(type);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Returns:</p><ul><li data-uuid="cc463247-03df-4eb3-9be4-a88f84b9f0d7">all <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/ActionUsage.html"><code>ActionUsage</code></a> elements</li><li data-uuid="3942a2f3-1b08-4c28-ab55-abe309620982">including inherited and referenced ones</li></ul><h3>Owned actions only</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3c7548fe-6844-4b5f-80b5-537e7e4e52fb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[List<ActionUsage> ownedActions = Actions.getOwnedActions(type);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Behavior:</p><ul><li data-uuid="d68bc1e9-af01-4ef1-b667-a703f2169763">For <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/Definition.html"><code>Definition</code> </a>→ returns <code>ownedAction</code></li><li data-uuid="0bc9cf4e-706b-4dcf-b6d7-a1f41396e69f">For <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/sysml/Usage.html"><code>Usage</code> </a>→ returns <code>nestedAction</code></li></ul><p>Use this when:</p><ul><li data-uuid="11a0bff7-a1a1-4b2a-8298-80ff596ebe4d">you want only locally defined behavior</li><li data-uuid="ceca0ae7-2d38-4721-bd31-5638c1615f18">you need to avoid inherited elements</li></ul><h2><strong>Start/Done Action Identification</strong></h2><p>Some actions represent lifecycle semantics (e.g., <em>start</em>, <em>done</em>).<br />These are resolved using the <strong>standard Actions library</strong> and redefinition logic.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="70fa5235-8ae5-4be1-b1a9-4178420e3d8c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element;

Element element = ...;

boolean isStart = Actions.isStartActionUsage(element);
boolean isDone = Actions.isDoneActionUsage(element);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>These checks:</p><ul><li data-uuid="326d6931-206e-4dc1-b50c-f19c00bcd9e8">rely on <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/libraries/standard/ActionsLibrary.html">ActionsLibrary</a></code></li><li data-uuid="2241034a-3b25-4901-8334-19fcc593a096">support <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Redefinition.html"> redefinition </a>(not just exact matches)</li></ul><p>Use this for:</p><ul><li data-uuid="6406c67b-8934-419f-bdc3-572b269c1fd9">execution modeling</li><li data-uuid="2a7eb185-547d-4655-bff5-d3fc3489dea3">lifecycle interpretation</li><li data-uuid="85d832c5-35a4-43cb-86ca-9daf3c9d5f05">simulation or analysis tooling</li></ul><h2><strong>Working with Input Parameters</strong></h2><p>Action inputs are represented as <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Feature.html">Feature</a></code> elements and accessed by index.</p><h3>Get or create parameter</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b2cd35d-dba5-4999-a471-a2e9e4bbc488"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.ActionUsage;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Feature;

ActionUsage action = ...;

Feature param = Actions.getOrCreateInputParameter(action, 0);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li data-uuid="6ece0094-1adb-4ffa-bff9-45b039042ff4">Creates the parameter if it does not exist</li><li data-uuid="c55f7af1-51d4-414b-940a-55bd5664bbc4">Ensures consistent parameter structure</li></ul><h3>Get existing parameter only</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="87edcad3-5e12-4f5d-8ea3-ef32e75dbb7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Feature param = Actions.getOwnedInputParameter(action, 1);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li data-uuid="419c5cdd-186c-418b-8d96-5e7c6434fc78">Returns <code>null</code> if not present</li><li data-uuid="4e63ee08-4aa4-4aa8-8160-307398f893e9">Does not modify the model</li></ul><h2><strong>Key Takeaways</strong></h2><ul><li data-uuid="2a38fa92-dff2-4849-82f9-7dda83c8f3d9">Actions in SysML v2 are <strong>typed, structured elements</strong>, not simple nodes</li><li data-uuid="a5d3b74c-ec90-44ad-9f17-8047515b78a0">The <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/Actions.html">Actions</a></code> utility class helps you:<ul><li>classify action types</li><li>navigate ownership</li><li>detect semantic actions (start/done)</li><li>manage parameters safely</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="5c6d1a07-8e1d-4a19-a285-b4327f8b7e77">It significantly reduces <strong>boilerplate and error-prone type checks</strong></li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989414 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00006: (2026x Refresh1) Activating and deactivating an element

- page_id: `303989414`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989414/2026x+Refresh1+Activating+and+deactivating+an+element
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:36.835+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 > (2026x Refresh1) Alf Plugin Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x Refresh1) Java APIs > (2026x Refresh1) Engine
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An execution engine can activate a specific element with values by calling an activateElement(Element element, Collection values)) of the ExecutionEngine. To deactivate the element, it will call a deactivateElement(Element element, Collection values)) of the same class.

These two methods are mostly called in the ExecutionEngine.execute(element). For example, if an Activity element on an Activity diagram is passed from the ExecutionEngine.execute(element), we use the activateElement() and deactivateElement() methods to activate and deactivate the Actions in that particular Activity respectively.

If activateElement() or deactivateElement() is called to a specific element, it will trigger all registered engine listeners including both predefined and user-defined listeners. For example, an AnimationListener is a predefined listener that is the Subclass of an EngineListener. It is used to display a specific element animation (in highlight) on a diagram in MagicDraw.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An execution engine can activate a specific element with values by calling an <span style="text-decoration: underline;">activateElement</span>(Element element, Collection values)) of the ExecutionEngine. To deactivate the element, it will call a deactivateElement(Element element, Collection values)) of the same class.</p><p>These two methods are mostly called in the ExecutionEngine.execute(element). For example, if an Activity element on an Activity diagram is passed from the ExecutionEngine.execute(element), we use the activateElement() and deactivateElement() methods to activate and deactivate the Actions in that particular Activity respectively.</p><p>If activateElement() or deactivateElement() is called to a specific element, it will trigger all registered engine listeners including both predefined and user-defined listeners. For example, an AnimationListener is a predefined listener that is the Subclass of an EngineListener. It is used to display a specific element animation (in highlight) on a diagram in MagicDraw.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989380 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00007: (2026x Refresh1) Adding custom panel or tree into browser

- page_id: `303989380`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989380/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+custom+panel+or+tree+into+browser
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:34.476+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Browser
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use*com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.Browser.addBrowserInitializer(Browser.BrowserInitializer[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/browser/Browser.html#addBrowserInitializer-com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.Browser.BrowserInitializer-)*to register a custom browser initializer and add your custom panel or tree into a browser.

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\browserpanel

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Use<em> com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.Browser.addBrowserInitializer(Browser.BrowserInitializer<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/browser/Browser.html#addBrowserInitializer-com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.Browser.BrowserInitializer-">)</a> </em>to register a custom browser initializer and add your custom panel or tree into a browser.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\browserpanel</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989364 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00008: (2026x Refresh1) Adding custom project window

- page_id: `303989364`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989364/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+custom+project+window
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:33.216+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Using and extending UI components
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

MagicDraw allows to open internal windows for the project and display some project related information in these windows. For example, Validation Result, Used By, Depend on, and etc. It is possible to add a custom internal window using API.

- Use com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.ProjectWindowsManager.ConfiguratorRegistry.addConfigurator( ProjectWindowsConfigurator) to register project window configurator.
- Implement com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.ProjectWindowsConfigurator and add window using ProjectWindowsManager.addWindow(ProjectWindow[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/ProjectWindowsManager.html#addWindow-com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.ProjectWindow-).

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>/openapi/examples/projectwindow*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>MagicDraw allows to open internal windows for the project and display some project related information in these windows. For example, Validation Result, Used By, Depend on, and etc. It is possible to add a custom internal window using API.</p><ul><li>Use<em> </em><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.ProjectWindowsManager.ConfiguratorRegistry.addConfigurator(</em><em>ProjectWindowsConfigurator)</em> to register project window configurator.</li><li>Implement <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.ProjectWindowsConfigurator</em> and add window using <em>ProjectWindowsManager.addWindow(ProjectWindow<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/ProjectWindowsManager.html#addWindow-com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.ProjectWindow-">)</a>.</em><br /><em><br /></em></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;/openapi/examples/projectwindow</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989090 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00009: (2026x Refresh1) Adding new diagram types

- page_id: `303989090`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989090/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+new+diagram+types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:18.020+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Diagram types
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Adding new diagram types to the modeling tool consists of two steps:

1. Overriding the abstract *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.DiagramDescriptor* class
2. 

###### **overrideSte****p #1. Override the abstract *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.DiagramDescriptor*class**

Override the abstract class *DiagramDescriptor* and implement the following abstract methods:

- getDiagramTypeId() – this method must return a diagram type id, which is used to identify the diagram
- getSingularDiagramTypeHumanName() – the method returns the diagram type human name
- getPluralDiagramTypeHumanName() – the method returns the diagram type human name in a plural form
- getSuperType() – this method must return a super type (super diagram) of this diagram type
- isCreatable() – returns the flag indicating if this diagram type is creatable
- getSVGIcon() – returns a large icon for this type of the diagram (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Adding new functionality' space='']Adding New Functionality )
- getSmallIconURL() – returns a small icon URL for this type of the diagram (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Adding new functionality' space='']Adding New Functionality )
- getDiagramActions() – returns the manager of actions used in the diagram
- getDiagramToolbarConfigurator() – returns the action manager configurator which configures the described diagram toolbar (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Adding new functionality' space='']Adding New Functionality )
- getDiagramShortcutsConfigurator() – returns the action manager configurator which configures described diagram shortcuts (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Adding new functionality' space='']Adding New Functionality )
- getDiagramContextConfigurator() – returns the action manager configurator which configures described diagram shortcut menu actions (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Adding new functionality' space=''] ).

###### **The diagram descriptor example**

For the full source code, see the Open API examples in *<program installation directory>\openapi\examples.*

```java

```

**registerSte****p #2. Register a new diagram type**

The new diagram descriptor should be registered in a modeling tool using the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.addNewDiagramType(**DiagramDescriptor)***method of the modeling tool you are using. This method can be invoked when a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Plugins' space=''] is initialized.

###### **The diagram descriptor registration example**

For the full source code, see the Open API examples in *<program installation directory>\openapi\examples\specificdiagram.*

```java

```

URL for this type of the diagram (see </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding new functionality" /><ac:link-body>Adding New <span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </span>Functionality</ac:link-body></ac:link><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">)</span></li><li><em>getDiagramActions()</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> – returns the manager of actions used in the diagram</span></li><li><em>getDiagramToolbarConfigurator()</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> – returns the action manager configurator which configures the described diagram toolbar (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding new functionality" /><ac:link-body>Adding New <span> </span>Functionality</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">)</span></li><li><em>getDiagramShortcutsConfigurator()</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> – returns the action manager configurator which configures described diagram shortcuts (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding new functionality" /><ac:link-body>Adding New <span> </span>Functionality</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">)</span></li><li><em>getDiagramContextConfigurator()</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> – returns the action manager configurator which configures described diagram shortcut menu actions (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding new functionality" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Adding New Functionality]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">).</span></li></ul></div><div class="WordSection2"><p><br /></p><h4><strong>The diagram descriptor example</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3eae444-3eb0-41c0-9fbf-4ffa202ef7ed"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For the full source code, see the Open API examples in <em>&lt;program installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="961dd4e3-b080-4883-8e31-be3e8009358c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    /**
````html
    * Descriptor of a specific diagram.
    */
    public class SpecificDiagramDescriptor extends DiagramDescriptor
    {
         public static final String SPECIFIC_DIAGRAM = "Specific Diagram";
 
        /**
        * Let this diagram type be a sub type of a class diagram type.
        */
        public String getSuperType()
        {
            return DiagramType.UML_CLASS_DIAGRAM;
        }
 
        /**
        * This is a creatable diagram.
        */
        public boolean isCreatable()
        {
            return true;
        }
 
        /**
        * Actions used in this diagram.
        */
        public MDActionsManager getDiagramActions()
        {
            return SpecificDiagramActions.ACTIONS;
        }
 
        /**
        * A configurator for a diagram toolbar.
        */
        public AMConfigurator getDiagramToolbarConfigurator()
        {
            return new SpecificDiagramToolbarConfigurator();
        }
 
        /**
        * A configurator for diagram shortcuts.
        */
        public AMConfigurator getDiagramShortcutsConfigurator()
        {
            return new ClassDiagramShortcutsConfigurator();
        }
 
        /**
        * A configurator for a diagram shortcut menu.
        */
        public DiagramContextAMConfigurator getDiagramContextConfigurator()
        {
            return new BaseDiagramContextAMConfigurator();
        }
 
        /**
        * Id of the diagram type.
        */
        public String getDiagramTypeId()
        {
            return SPECIFIC_DIAGRAM;
        }
 
        /**
        * A diagram type human name.
        */
        public String getSingularDiagramTypeHumanName()
        {
            return "Specific Diagram";
        }
 
        /**
        * A diagram type human name in a plural form.
        */
        public String getPluralDiagramTypeHumanName()
        {
            return "Specific Diagrams";
        }
 
        /**
        * Resizable svg icon for diagram.
        */
        public ResizableIcon getSVGIcon()
		{
   			ResizableIcon icon = null;
   			try
			{
      			icon = IconsFactory.getSvgIcon(new File("icons/specificdiagram.svg").toURI().toURL());
   			}
   			catch(Exception e){e.printStackTrace();}
   			return icon;
		}
 
        /**
        * URL to a small icon for a diagram.
        */
        public URL getSmallIconURL()
        {
             return getClass().getResource("icons/specificdiagram.svg");
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40564597-ffce-43be-b83e-9848b692e065"><ac:parameter ac:name="">register</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Ste</strong><strong>p #2. Register a new diagram type</strong></p><div class="WordSection1"><p>The new diagram descriptor should be registered in a modeling tool using the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.addNewDiagramType(</em><em>DiagramDescriptor)</em><em> </em>method of the modeling tool you are using. This method can be invoked when a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Plugins" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is initialized.</p><h4><strong>The diagram descriptor registration example</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8fd30d68-73a8-4e2b-8b2c-43b3e24c78bc"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For the full source code, see the Open API examples in <em>&lt;program installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\specificdiagram.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea87ecad-9037-47ab-a3ce-7c801987074c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	class SpecificDiagramPlugin extends Plugin{
        /**
        * Initializing the plugin.
        */
        public void init()
        {
            // Registering a new diagram type
            Application.getInstance().addNewDiagramType(new SpecificDiagramDescriptor());
        }
        /**
        * Always returns true, because this plugin does not have any close specific actions.
        */
        public boolean close()
        {
            return true;
        }
        /**
        * Always returns true, because this plugin does not have any specific suportability conditions.
        */
        public boolean isSupported()
        {
            return true;
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00010: (2026x Refresh1) Adding new functionality

- page_id: `303988936`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303988936/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+new+functionality
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:09.437+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General
- labels: ['menu-command', 'button', 'command', 'shortcut-menu-command']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A program actions mechanism enables to add new functionality to the modeling tool and the way to invoke it through GUI. In this case, all menu commands and buttons are considered as actions.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using and extending other UI components' space=''][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using and extending other UI components' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A program actions mechanism enables to add new functionality to the modeling tool and the way to invoke it through GUI. In this case, all menu commands and buttons are considered as actions.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="480c08b7-4c55-41b6-9165-291917dc8362"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="71d29685-16b3-4762-9684-067bac30d19f"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p> </p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using and extending other UI components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Using and extending other UI component]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="248c8d01-fca8-41a3-b981-6c8d55fab471"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using and extending other UI components" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989542 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00011: (2026x Refresh1) Adding, Moving, and Removing Elements

- page_id: `303989542`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989542/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+Moving+and+Removing+Elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:40.993+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 > (2026x Refresh1) Working With Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Add or move an element by setting its owner directly

You can add an element to a container, or move it to a different one, by calling `[setOwner](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html#setOwner(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element))()` on the element.

This is the simplest way to establish containment. If the element already belongs to another owner, calling `[setOwner](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html#setOwner(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element))()` moves it to the new one.

```java

```

##### Add or move an element with the `Elements` utility

The `[Elements](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html)` utility class provides helper methods for working with ownership relationships explicitly.

Use `[Elements.setOwningMembership](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#setOwningMembership(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Namespace,org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass))()` when you want to create or update the owning relationship in a consistent and explicit way. This is especially useful when working with APIs that represent model structure through relationship elements.

If the element already has an owner, it is moved to the new one.

```java

```

##### Remove an element with `dispose()` or `selfDispose()`

To remove an element from the model, call `[dispose](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/model/ModelElement.html#dispose())()` on it.

`[dispose](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/model/ModelElement.html#dispose())()` deletes the element from the model and also removes related ownership and other connected relationships.

```java

```

Use `[selfDispose](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/model/ModelElement.html#selfDispose())()` when you only want to delete the element itself together with its owning relationship.

```java

```

##### Remove an element with the `Elements` utility

The `[Elements](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html)` utility class also provides a convenient way to delete one or more elements.

Use `[Elements.dispose](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#dispose(java.util.Collection))()` when you want a consistent utility-based way to remove elements from the model.

```java

```

#### **Notes**

`[setOwner](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html#setOwner(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element))()` is the most direct way to add or move an element.

`[Elements.setOwningMembership](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#setOwningMembership(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Namespace,org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass))()` is more explicit and better reflects the relationship-based structure of the SysML v2 / KerML model.

`[dispose](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/model/ModelElement.html#dispose())()` removes the element more fully, while `[selfDispose](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/model/ModelElement.html#selfDispose())``()` is a lighter-weight deletion that removes only the element and its owning relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Add or move an element by setting its owner directly</h3><p>You can add an element to a container, or move it to a different one, by calling <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html#setOwner(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element)">setOwner</a>()</code> on the element.</p><p>This is the simplest way to establish containment. If the element already belongs to another owner, calling <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html#setOwner(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element)">setOwner</a>()</code> moves it to the new one.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1e1093a-098a-4251-9417-f6c41397f150"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Package;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.ElementsFactory;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.PartDefinition;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project;

Project project = ...;
ElementsFactory elementsFactory = ElementsFactory.get(project);

Package pkg = elementsFactory.createPackage();
PartDefinition drone = elementsFactory.createPartDefinition();

// Add or move the PartDefinition into the Package
drone.setOwner(pkg);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span>Add or move an element with the <code>Elements</code> utility</span></h3><p>The <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html">Elements</a></code> utility class provides helper methods for working with ownership relationships explicitly.</p><p>Use <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#setOwningMembership(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Namespace,org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass)">Elements.setOwningMembership</a>()</code> when you want to create or update the owning relationship in a consistent and explicit way. This is especially useful when working with APIs that represent model structure through relationship elements.</p><p>If the element already has an owner, it is moved to the new one.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="90ad06ef-dac6-4b00-be6f-94d4eba22e33"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Elements;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.KerMLPackage;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Package;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.ElementsFactory;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.PartDefinition;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project;

Project project = ...;
ElementsFactory elementsFactory = ElementsFactory.get(project);

Package pkg = elementsFactory.createPackage();
PartDefinition drone = elementsFactory.createPartDefinition();

// Add or move the PartDefinition into the Package
Elements.setOwningMembership(drone, pkg, KerMLPackage.eINSTANCE.getOwningMembership());]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span>Remove an element with <code>dispose()</code> or <code>selfDispose()</code></span></h3><p>To remove an element from the model, call <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/model/ModelElement.html#dispose()">dispose</a>()</code> on it.</p><p><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/model/ModelElement.html#dispose()">dispose</a>()</code> deletes the element from the model and also removes related ownership and other connected relationships.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e504bf65-7086-4891-97f7-89c048fabe9a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Element element = ...;
element.dispose();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Use <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/model/ModelElement.html#selfDispose()">selfDispose</a>()</code> when you only want to delete the element itself together with its owning relationship.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4997beb2-cad9-40ca-981b-6f2b433e2ae7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Element element = ...;
element.selfDispose();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span>Remove an element with the <code>Elements</code> utility</span></h3><p>The <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html">Elements</a></code> utility class also provides a convenient way to delete one or more elements.</p><p>Use <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#dispose(java.util.Collection)">Elements.dispose</a>()</code> when you want a consistent utility-based way to remove elements from the model.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="403b7ba8-5930-4908-80ad-7a20de365751"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Elements;
import java.util.List;

com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Element element = ...;
Elements.dispose(List.of(element));]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Notes</strong></h2><p><code><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html#setOwner(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element)">setOwner</a></code>()</code> is the most direct way to add or move an element.</p><p><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Elements.html#setOwningMembership(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Namespace,org.eclipse.emf.ecore.EClass)">Elements.setOwningMembership</a>()</code> is more explicit and better reflects the relationship-based structure of the SysML v2 / KerML model.</p><p><code><code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/model/ModelElement.html#dispose()">dispose</a></code>()</code> removes the element more fully, while <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/model/ModelElement.html#selfDispose()">selfDispose</a></code><code>()</code> is a lighter-weight deletion that removes only the element and its owning relationship.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989424 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00012: (2026x Refresh1) Adding, moving, deleting model elements

- page_id: `303989424`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989424/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+moving+deleting+model+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:37.608+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are two ways to add/move/delete model elements:

- Use com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.ModelElementsManager
- Call direct functions on Elements

The *ModelElementsManager* is the singleton utility class which checks *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element* editing permissions and the session existence before executing the function. Also, it performs a check, if an element can be added to a parent. If the*ModelElementsManager*is not used, a programmer must perform these checks in the code explicitly.

com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.NamedElement

Element

(s)

##### Adding element to owner with *ModelElementsManager*

For adding a new model *Element*into a model, use the *addElement(**Element, Element)*method provided by the *ModelElementsManager*.

```java

```

java.lang.IllegalArgumentException

com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Operation

com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Package

Operation

com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class

java.lang.IllegalArgumentException

com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.ReadOnlyElementException

##### Adding element to owner using a direct function

```java

```

##### Moving element with *ModelElementsManager*

For moving an existing model *Element*, use the *moveElement(**Element, Element)*method provided by the *ModelElementsManager*.

```java

```

If a given model element cannot be moved into a given parent, *java.lang.IllegalArgumentException*is thrown. For example, an *Operation* cannot be moved into a *Package* or an *Operation* cannot be moved into a not locked for editing *Class* (in the server project). If an element or parent is null, *java.lang.IllegalArgumentException* also is thrown. If a given element is not editable (read-only), *com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.ReadOnlyElementException* is thrown.

##### Moving element using a direct function

```java

```

##### Removing element with *ModelElementsManager*

```java

```

##### Removing element using direct call

```java

```

****You can find the code examples in <*modeling tool installation direc**tory>\openapi\examples\hierarchyremover*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><div class="WordSection1"><div class="WordSection1"><p>There are two ways to add/move/delete model elements:</p><ul><li>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.ModelElementsManager</em></li><li>Call direct functions on <em>Elements</em></li></ul><p>The <em>ModelElementsManager</em> is the singleton utility class which checks <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</em> editing permissions and the session existence before executing the function. <span>Also, it performs a check, if an element can be added to a parent. If the </span><em>ModelElementsManager </em><span>is not used, a programmer must perform these checks in the code explicitly.</span></p></div></div><div class="WordSection1">Adding and moving functions are similar with mostly one - move functions does more checking such as checking for a name conflict if <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.NamedElement</em> is moved. The general rule would be to use an adding function for newly created <em>Element</em><em>(s)</em> and use a moving function for already existing elements in the model.</div><div class="WordSection1">More advanced model refactoring functions are described in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Refactoring model elements" /></ac:link> page.</div><div class="WordSection1"><h3>Adding element to owner with <em>ModelElementsManager</em></h3><p>For adding a new model <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Element </em>into a model, use the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">addElement(</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Element, Element) </em>method provided by the <em>ModelElementsManager</em>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b066f9ab-b044-4733-abb5-6f5d06b9fa43"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project = ...;
    Class classA = ...;
    Package package = ...;
    // create a new session
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Add class into package");
    try
    {
        // add a class into a package
        ModelElementsManager.getInstance().addElement(classA, package);
    }
    catch (ReadOnlyElementException e)
    {
    }
    // apply changes and add a command into the command history. 
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div>If a given model element cannot be added into a given parent, <em>java.lang.IllegalArgumentException</em> is thrown. For example, an <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Operation</em> cannot be added into a <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Package</em> or an <em class="WordSection1">Operation</em> cannot be added into a not locked for editing <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class</em> (in the server project). If an element or parent is null, <em>java.lang.IllegalArgumentException</em> also is thrown. If a given element is not editable (read-only), <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.ReadOnlyElementException</em> is thrown.</div><h3>Adding element to owner using a direct function</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05943c37-2b41-41df-80e3-21a3175ad88f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Element parent = ...;
    Class classA = ...;
    Project project = ...;
    // create a new session 
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Add class into parent"); 
    if (parent.canAdd(classA))
    {
        classA.setOwner(parent);
    }
    // apply changes and add a command into the command history. 
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><div class="WordSection1"><div class="WordSection1"><h3>Moving element with <em>ModelElementsManager</em></h3><p>For moving an existing model <em>Element</em>, use the <em>moveElement(</em><em>Element, Element) </em>method provided by the <em>ModelElementsManager</em>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ce0de87-0851-4f30-ab48-85adf5d3be46"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project = ...;
    Class classA = ...;
    Package package = ...;
    // create a new session
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Add class into package");
    try
    {
        // add a class into a package
        ModelElementsManager.getInstance().moveElement(classA, package);
    }
    catch (ReadOnlyElementException e)
    {
    }
    // apply changes and add a command into the command history. 
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></div><p>If a given model element cannot be moved into a given parent, <em>java.lang.IllegalArgumentException</em>is thrown. For example, an <em class="WordSection1">Operation</em> cannot be moved into a <em>Package</em> or an <em class="WordSection1">Operation</em> cannot be moved into a not locked for editing <em>Class</em> (in the server project). If an element or parent is null, <em>java.lang.IllegalArgumentException</em> also is thrown. If a given element is not editable (read-only), <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.ReadOnlyElementException</em> is thrown.</p><h3>Moving element using a direct function</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="39454626-5057-4dd2-85d9-bbde833669cf"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Element parent = ...;
    Class classA = ...;
    Project project = ...;
    // create a new session 
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Add class into parent"); 
    if (com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.ModelHelper.canMoveChildInto(parent, classA))
    {
        classA.setOwner(parent);
    } 
    // apply changes and add a command into the command history. 
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Removing element with <em>ModelElementsManager</em></h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fdbb7ac5-f257-4b44-b687-708af26d1050"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Class classA = ...;
    Project project = ...;
    // create a new session 
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Remove class"); 
    try
    {
         // remove a class
        ModelElementsManager.getInstance().removeElement(classA);
    }
    catch (ReadOnlyElementException e)
    {
    }
    // apply changes and add a command into the command history. 
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project); 

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><div class="WordSection1"><h3>Removing element using direct call</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8157f4d1-7fc3-4245-97c3-781164a7988a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Class classA = ...;
    Project project = ...;
    // create a new session 
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Remove class"); 
    if (classA.isEditable()))
    {
         classA.dispose();
    }
    // apply changes and add a command into the command history. 
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in &lt;<em>modeling tool installation direc</em><em>tory&gt;\openapi\examples\hierarchyremover</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ed0cc5f-904b-4b81-9316-e50cc55cc824"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On this page</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ef8a4586-965a-4ddd-a692-2928b514fae3" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff0c20a8-6f81-463f-abbf-70b3ea1f6b2c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Checking Model Element Permissions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Refactoring model elements" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989072 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00013: (2026x Refresh1) Advanced utility functions

- page_id: `303989072`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989072/2026x+Refresh1+Advanced+utility+functions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:16.834+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.ModelHelper* for various advanced UML model related functions.

It provides utility methods for

- Some core functions
  - working with element's documentation (Comments)
  - getting/setting Relationships client and supplier ends
  - collecting connected relationships of element.
  - working with Associations - getting member ends, changing navigability
  - working with multiplicity of MultiplicityElements
  - ownership related functions - looking for valid owners, checking ownership and etc.
- Working with Value Specifications
  - Creating value specifications
  - Setting value to Value specifications
- Working with Classifiers
  - collecting derived classifiers, checking legal inheritance
  - collecting inherited members
  - working with redefined elements
  - checking Classifiers compatibility
  - working with Operation parameters, comparing Operations signatures
- Working with InstanceSpecifications
  - creating Slots, initializing slots values
  - working with Links

Use *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.InformationFlowHelper* for advanced Information Flows related functions

Use *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.InteractionHelper* for Interaction domain related advanced functions

Use *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.StateMachineHelper* for State Machine related advanced functions

Use *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.UseCaseHelper***for Use Case related advanced functions

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ConnectorsCollector* to collect connected Connector elements

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.ModelHelper</em> for various advanced UML model related functions.</p><p>It provides utility methods for </p><ul><li>Some core functions<ul><li>working with element's documentation (Comments)</li><li>getting/setting Relationships client and supplier ends</li><li>collecting connected relationships of element.</li><li><span>working with Associations - getting member ends, changing navigability</span></li><li>working with multiplicity of MultiplicityElements</li><li>ownership related functions - looking for valid owners, checking ownership and etc.</li></ul></li><li><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Working with Value Specifications</span><br /><ul><li>Creating value specifications</li><li>Setting value to Value specifications</li></ul></li><li>Working with Classifiers<ul><li>collecting derived classifiers, checking legal inheritance</li><li>collecting inherited members</li><li>working with redefined elements</li><li>checking Classifiers compatibility</li><li>working with Operation parameters,  comparing Operations signatures</li></ul></li><li>Working with InstanceSpecifications<ul><li>creating Slots, initializing slots values</li><li>working with Links</li></ul></li></ul><p>Use <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.InformationFlowHelper</em> for advanced Information Flows related functions</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.InteractionHelper</em> for Interaction domain related advanced functions</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.StateMachineHelper</em> for State Machine related advanced functions</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.UseCaseHelper</em><em> </em>for Use Case related advanced functions</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ConnectorsCollector</em> to collect connected Connector elements</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````
