<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989376 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00015: (2026x Refresh1) Annotating the elements

- page_id: `303989376`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989376/2026x+Refresh1+Annotating+the+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:34.191+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based
- labels: ['annotation-manager']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Using the Open API you can add an annotation to any base element (a model element or a symbol on the diagram pane). Annotations are used to highlight and adorn elements in the Containment tree and on the diagram pane. For example validation engine is using annotations for showing invalid elements.

Annotation has the following properties:

- severity - like an error, warning, info, debug, fatal
- kind - a string representing the annotation short name
- text - a string representing the annotation text
- target - a target base element
- actions - an optional list of actions. They are shown in a diagram on a symbol’s smart manipulator.

To add or remove annotations, use***com.nomagic.magicdraw.annotation.AnnotationManager*.

Do not forget to call*AnnotationManager.update()*, when you are done with adding or removing in order to refresh UI.

More information is available in javadoc.

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\annotations

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Using the Open API you can add an annotation to any base element (a model element or a symbol on the diagram pane). Annotations are used to highlight and adorn elements in the Containment tree and on the diagram pane. For example validation engine is using annotations for showing invalid elements.</p><p>Annotation has the following properties:</p><ul><li>severity - like an error, warning, info, debug, fatal</li><li>kind - a string representing the annotation short name</li><li>text - a string representing the annotation text</li><li>target - a target base element</li><li>actions - an optional list of actions. They are shown in a diagram on a symbol’s smart manipulator.</li></ul><p>To add or remove annotations, use<em> </em><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.annotation.AnnotationManager</em>.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="694316df-2423-4140-92cc-a88993ad7a03"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Do not forget to call<em> AnnotationManager.update()</em>, when you are done with adding or removing in order to refresh UI.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>More information is available in javadoc.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\annotations</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989288 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00016: (2026x Refresh1) API Changes

- page_id: `303989288`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989288/2026x+Refresh1+API+Changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:29.092+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 > (2026x Refresh1) Alf Plugin Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x Refresh1) Java APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table lists the old and new values of the constants for SimulationProfile, SimulationOptions, and SysMLConstants.

| Old values up to 2022x Refresh2 | New Values |
| --- | --- |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.SimulationOptionConstants were removed altogether, instead, use com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationOptions |  |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989444 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00017: (2026x Refresh1) Application environment

- page_id: `303989444`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989444/2026x+Refresh1+Application+environment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:37.970+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.runtime()* to retrieve a *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.Runtime* object. It contains an application name, version, and file format information.

Use *Application.environment()* to retrieve an *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.Environment* object. It contains application environment directory information like install, configuration, data, templates, profiles, etc.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.runtime()</em> to retrieve a <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.Runtime</em> object. It contains an application name, version, and file format information.</span></p><p>Use <em>Application.environment()</em> to retrieve an <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.Environment</em> object. It contains application environment directory information like install, configuration, data, templates, profiles, etc.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00018: (2026x Refresh1) Attached Files

- page_id: `303989458`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989458/2026x+Refresh1+Attached+Files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:38.257+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.fileattachments.FileAttachmentsHelper* to create, remove or save attached files to disk.

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\fileattachments

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.fileattachments.FileAttachmentsHelper</em> to create, remove or save attached files to disk. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\fileattachments</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989280 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00020: (2026x Refresh1) Binary validation rule

- page_id: `303989280`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989280/2026x+Refresh1+Binary+validation+rule
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:28.662+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Creating validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Sometimes it is hard to specify the rule for some advanced concepts in OCL. For example, the string manipulation is rather weak in OCL2.0, hence the writing rule for string formats, parsing strings is very hard in OCL. In such cases the modeling tool offers possibility to write rules in Java language and invoke them when validating.

Common steps for creating a binary validation rule:

1. Create a constraint
2. Set the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule» for the validation rule
3. Set the severity level, error message, and abbreviation
4. Specify constrained element(s)
5. Specify the specification language binary
6. Create a Java implementation class (see all possible [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Implementing a binary validation rule' space=''] )
7. Compile Java code, resulting in one or several classes.
8. Ensure that your modeling tool can find & load these classes. You can place them in the program classpath or use the modeling tool plugins mechanism. The same rules as writing the code for the open API are used here.

These steps are common to all possible binary validation cases and will not be repeated in each case description.

See the *MyBinaryValidationRuleImpl.java* example in the *<program installation directory>\openapi\examples\validation* directory.

The implementation class must be in the classpath of the program or if the implementation class is in a plugin then the plugin’s classloader must be registered to the validation system (see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Binary validation rule in the plugin' space='']).

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating validation rules' space='']

In such cases the modeling tool offers possibility to write rules in Java language and invoke them when validating.</p><p>Common steps for creating a binary validation rule:</p><ol><li>Create a constraint</li><li>Set the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule» for the validation rule</li><li>Set the severity level, error message, and abbreviation</li><li>Specify constrained element(s)</li><li>Specify the specification language binary</li><li>Create a Java implementation class (see all possible <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Implementing a binary validation rule" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[cases]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>)</li><li>Compile Java code, resulting in one or several classes.</li><li>Ensure that your modeling tool can find &amp; load these classes. You can place them in the program classpath or use the modeling tool plugins mechanism. The same rules as writing the code for the open API are used here.</li></ol></div><p>These steps are common to all possible binary validation cases and will not be repeated in each case description.</p><p>See the <em>MyBinaryValidationRuleImpl.java</em> example in the <em>&lt;program installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\validation</em> directory.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dc1cc02e-8e2c-401c-be1d-e931a383080b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The implementation class must be in the classpath of the program or if the implementation class is in a plugin then the plugin’s classloader must be registered to the validation system (see<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Binary validation rule in the plugin" /></ac:link></span>).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f368d5fe-805a-447b-a01b-fca122ddd589"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating validation rules" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00021: (2026x Refresh1) Binary validation rule in the plugin

- page_id: `303989342`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989342/2026x+Refresh1+Binary+validation+rule+in+the+plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:31.685+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Creating validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The validation engine must be able to load the validation rule implementation class. The validation engine is able to load a class if the implementation class exists in the program classpath or if the validation rule implementation class is in the plugin classpath. The plugin must register the plugin’s classloader to the validation system as follows:

```java
. class.getClassLoader());]]>
```

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989366 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00022: (2026x Refresh1) Browser

- page_id: `303989366`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989366/2026x+Refresh1+Browser
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:33.365+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based
- labels: ['browser', 'browser-tree']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Model Browser (Containment tree, Diagrams tree, etc.) is a based GUI part for displaying various model trees and panels

```java

```

The browser has five trees by default (some of them are be default hidden):

- containment
- diagrams
- inheritance
- extensions
- search results

The browser has 3 panel by default

- documentation
- properties
- zoom

Last activated tree is marked as active:

```java

```

Every tree is based on Swing JTree and all manipulations can be done by using the API provided by Swing:

```java

```

Selected nodes are accessible in the following way:

```java

```

Node is derived from *javax.swing.tree.DefaultMutableTreeNode*.

**Selecting/opening element in tree**

In order to select some element in tree, you need

- Open(activate) a tree using com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.Browser#getContainmentTree ;
- Call com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.Tree#openNode(com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement).

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989587 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00023: (2026x Refresh1) Building a resource distribution file

- page_id: `303989587`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989587/2026x+Refresh1+Building+a+resource+distribution+file
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:43.693+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Distributing Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Users can create their own resource distribution files and install resources from them using the Resource Manager. These files can contain plugins/profiles/model libraries etc. The resources must be packed to separate **.zip** files.

This can be useful for distributing a set of resources accompanied with custom resources or in a locked down environment where the resource bundle is hosted in a local network.

There are multiple ways to create a resource bundle:

1) Using the **Development Tools** plugin GUI

1. Install the Development Tools plugin using your tool using Resource Manager.
2. Restart the modeling tool.
3. Create a new empty folder, place the resources there.
4. In the modeling tool main menu, click Tools > Development Tools > Build Resource Distribution File .
5. Browse and select the created folder, choose the output location and click OK . A resource distribution file will be built in the output folder.

The**Development Tools**plugin can be installed from[nomagic.com](https://www.nomagic.com/)or found in the resource distribution file (.*rdzip*).

2) Using command line tool.

In case the process of building a resource distribution files should be automated, it can be done using command line tool.

Three arguments are required:

- Resource directory path
- Output directory path
- Result distribution file name

Example:

```text

```

Resource distribution file creator checks for validity of each resource. Only valid resources are included in the resource distribution file. The resources must meet the required folder/file structure and must contain appropriate descriptor file. For more information about the folder/file structure visit [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating required files and folders structure' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Users can create their own resource distribution files and install resources from them using the Resource Manager. These files can contain plugins/profiles/model libraries etc. The resources must be packed to separate <strong>.zip</strong> files.</p><p>This can be useful for distributing a set of resources accompanied with custom resources or in a locked down environment where the resource bundle is hosted in a local network.</p><p>There are multiple ways to create a resource bundle:</p><p>1) Using the <strong>Development Tools</strong> plugin GUI</p><ol><li>Install the <strong>Development Tools</strong> plugin using your tool using Resource Manager.</li><li>Restart the modeling tool.</li><li>Create a new empty folder, place the resources there.</li><li>In the modeling tool main menu, click <strong>Tools </strong>&gt;<strong> Development Tools </strong>&gt;<strong> Build Resource Distribution File</strong>.</li><li>Browse and select the created folder, choose the output location and click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">OK</strong>. A resource distribution file will be built in the output folder.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5659f791-f619-43e5-95fc-bc26a98bc206"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The</span><strong> Development Tools</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> plugin can be installed from </span><a class="external-link" href="https://www.nomagic.com/" rel="nofollow">nomagic.com</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> or found in the resource distribution file (.</span><em>rdzip</em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">).</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>2) Using command line tool.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">In case the process of building a resource distribution files should be automated, it can be done using command line tool.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Three arguments are required:</p><ul><li>Resource directory path</li><li>Output directory path</li><li>Result distribution file name</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03b2273a-6b5a-4114-bc74-74151ee8841a"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[java -cp "lib/*;lib/bundles/*" com.nomagic.magicdraw.resourcemanager.distribution.GenerateResourceDistributionFileTask "E:\myResources" "E:\myBundles" "myCustomResourceDistributionFile"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74a60d1f-d3d7-4766-8732-0a95016063c4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Resource distribution file creator checks for validity of each resource. Only valid resources are included in the resource distribution file. The resources must meet the required folder/file structure and must contain appropriate descriptor file. For more information about the folder/file structure visit <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating required files and folders structure" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00025: (2026x Refresh1) Changing path break points

- page_id: `303989348`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989348/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+path+break+points
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:32.159+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Presentation elements (symbols) > (2026x Refresh1) Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To change a break points list for the given path element, use the method *com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.changePathBreakPoints(PathElement, java.util.List)*. The same method must be used if you want to change a client or supplier connection point.

The following code snippet shows how to do this:

```java

```

.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>To change a break points list for the given path element, use the method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.changePathBreakPoints(PathElement, java.util.List)</em>. The same method must be used if you want to change a client or supplier connection point.</p><p>The following code snippet shows how to do this:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81eb7a61-b3f9-4fd8-b334-5374f9e7d02b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    PathElement element = ...;
    ArrayList points = new ArrayList(); 
    points.add(new Point(100, 100));
    points.add(new Point(100, 150));
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession("Test");
    PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().changePathBreakPoints(element, points);
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro>The order of points in the list must be from the supplier to client connection point. The list may or may not include the client and supplier connection points. At first the given points list will be adopted for the current path style (Rectilinear, Bezier<em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">. </em>or Oblique) and only then applied for the path.</div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b7d0be1b-f166-46b8-8b28-4554d9e0577c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989247 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00026: (2026x Refresh1) Changing properties of presentation elements

- page_id: `303989247`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989247/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+properties+of+presentation+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:27.249+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Presentation elements (symbols) > (2026x Refresh1) Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Most of presentation elements can have properties (for example *Autosize* for shapes or *Path Style*for paths):

- To check if an element has some properties (or uses properties from its parent), use the method com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement.useParentStyle() .
- To get all properties of this element, use the method com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.getPropertyManager(*PresentationElement*) .
- To get a property of this element with a given ID, use the method com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager .getProperty(PresentationElement, java.lang.String) .
- To change element properties, use the method com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.setPresentationElementProperties( PresentationElement, PropertyManager) . The given com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyManager can have only few element’s properties (for example, just properties you want to change).

The Ids of all used properties are defined in the class *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyID*.

Look at [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Properties' space=''] page for more details about properties structure.

There is no information about what exactly properties every presentation element has. Use java debugger and analyze *PresentationElementsManager**.getPropertyManager(PresentationElement)* object. You can also guess property ID from the Symbol Properties name.

If you want to change a value of existing property, recommendation would be clone that property, change value for a clone and set it. Don't use constructor if possible.

The following code example shows how to change element properties:

```java

```

The properties must be new instances. You cannot do something like this:

```java

```

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Properties' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Most of presentation elements can have properties (for example <em>Autosize</em> for shapes or <em>Path Style </em>for paths):</p><ul><li>To check if an element has some properties (or uses properties from its parent), use the method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement.useParentStyle()</em>.</li><li>To get all properties of this element, use the method <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.getPropertyManager(<em>PresentationElement</em>)</em>.</li><li>To get a property of this element with a given ID, use the method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager</em><em>.getProperty(PresentationElement, java.lang.String)</em>.</li><li>To change element properties, use the method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.setPresentationElementProperties(</em><em>PresentationElement, PropertyManager)</em>. The given <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyManager</em> can have only few element’s properties (for example, just properties you want to change).</li></ul><p>The Ids of all used properties are defined in the class <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyID</em>.</p><p>Look at <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Properties" /></ac:link> page for more details about properties structure.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76534000-96db-443a-afbc-2a32a34c27d5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>There is no information about what exactly properties every presentation element has. Use java debugger and analyze <em>PresentationElementsManager</em><em>.getPropertyManager(PresentationElement)</em> object. You can also guess property ID from the Symbol Properties name.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36c6fcc7-ed86-41f2-8ecf-5fe252f7261c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to change a value of existing property, recommendation would be clone that property, change value for a clone and set it. Don't use constructor if possible.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The following code example shows how to change element properties:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb4de8c9-109d-46bb-a8d8-dd03610cf555"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ShapeElement element = ...;
    Property property = PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().getProperty(element, PropertyID.AUTOSIZE);
    if (property instanceof BooleanProperty)
    {
        BooleanProperty autoSize = (BooleanProperty) property.clone();
        autoSize.setValue(true);

        PropertyManager properties = new PropertyManager();
        properties.addProperty(autoSize);

        SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Test");
        PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().setPresentationElementProperties(element, properties);
        SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9153917b-9322-4ccd-bd7f-879d217fcb51"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The properties must be new instances. You cannot do something like this: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ad358495-8ca5-47aa-abba-1c0b8258ebc9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ShapeElement element = ...;
    PropertyManager properties = PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().getPropertyManager(element);
    properties.getProperty(PropertyID.AUTOSIZE).setValue(new Boolean(true));
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Test");
    PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().setPresentationElementProperties(element, properties);
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fa467e03-adb1-48ac-9d6c-74ab1f87088b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Properties" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989452 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00027: (2026x Refresh1) Checking Model Element Permissions

- page_id: `303989452`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989452/2026x+Refresh1+Checking+Model+Element+Permissions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:38.125+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Model Modification
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before modifying the model, it is the **developer’s responsibility** to verify that the operation is allowed.

The Open API **does not block modifications automatically**, even if an element is read-only. Failing to check permissions may result in invalid model state or runtime issues.

#### **When an Element Is Read-Only**

An element may be non-editable in the following cases:

- It belongs to a read-only module (used project)
- It is not locked in a Teamwork Cloud environment
- It is restricted by custom or domain-specific rules
- It is restricted by plugin-level or application-level permissions

Permissions are evaluated through multiple handlers registered in the system (Teamwork, modules, proxies, diagrams, etc.).

#### **Types of Permission Checks**

Different operations require different permission checks.

##### 1. Checking Property Editing

Use when modifying **primitive properties** or **references** (e.g., name, type, visibility).

```text

```

- Returns whether the element can be edited
- Covers:
  - Element-level permissions
  - Project state
  - Teamwork locks

##### 2. Checking If Children Can Be Created

Use when creating **new elements inside a parent**.

```text

```

- Checks if the parent allows any children
- Does not validate a specific child type

##### 3. Checking If a Specific Child Can Be Added

Use when adding an element to a parent.

**Basic check**

```text

```

This validates:

- Type compatibility (e.g., cannot add a child of a kind which a parent is not allowed to own)
- Ownership constraints
- Teamwork permissions (if enabled)

**Full permission check**

```text

```

This includes:

- Registered permission handlers
- Custom rules
- Environment-specific restrictions

##### 4. Checking Deletion Permission

Use before removing an element from the model.

```text

```

or

```text

```

Deletion requires:

- Element is editable
- Parent allows removal
- Project is editable

##### 5. Checking Moving Permission

Moving an element is effectively:

- Removing from old parent
- Adding to new parent

```text

```

This performs:

- Ownership validation
- Cycle prevention
- Permission checks (edit + add)

#### **Using ElementPermissionsManager**

The ElementPermissionsManager provides a **centralized permission evaluation layer**.

```text

```

Key characteristics:

- Aggregates multiple permission sources
- Delegates to registered ElementPermissions handlers
- Supports caching for performance
- Used internally by the platform

#### **Plugging Custom Permission Logic**

You can define custom rules by implementing:

```text

```

Then register it:

```text

```

Custom handlers are:

- Combined with existing handlers
- Evaluated by the permissions manager
- Suitable for:
  - Domain-specific restrictions
  - Tooling constraints
  - Validation layers

#### **Recommended Usage Pattern**

Always check permissions **before modifying the model**:

```text

```

For more complex scenarios, prefer the manager:

```text

```

#### **Key Takeaways**

- Permissions are not enforced automatically → must be checked explicitly
- Use:
  - isEditable() → property changes
  - canAddChild() / canAdd () → containment
  - canBeDeleted() → removal
- Use ElementPermissionsManager for full permission evaluation
- Extend via ElementPermissions for custom rules

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Before modifying the model, it is the <strong>developer’s responsibility</strong> to verify that the operation is allowed.</p><p>The Open API <strong>does not block modifications automatically</strong>, even if an element is read-only. Failing to check permissions may result in invalid model state or runtime issues.</p><h2><strong>When an Element Is Read-Only</strong></h2><p>An element may be non-editable in the following cases:</p><ul><li>It belongs to a <strong>read-only module (used project)</strong></li><li>It is <strong>not locked</strong> in a <strong>Teamwork Cloud</strong> environment</li><li>It is restricted by <strong>custom or domain-specific rules</strong></li><li>It is restricted by <strong>plugin-level or application-level permissions</strong></li></ul><p>Permissions are evaluated through multiple handlers registered in the system (Teamwork, modules, proxies, diagrams, etc.).</p><h2><strong>Types of Permission Checks</strong></h2><p>Different operations require different permission checks.</p><h3>1. Checking Property Editing</h3><p>Use when modifying <strong>primitive properties</strong> or <strong>references</strong> (e.g., name, type, visibility).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4ae42b3d-1b63-4b6f-ac35-f3aeddeaf7ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[boolean editable = element.isEditable();
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>Returns whether the element can be edited</li><li>Covers:<ul><li>Element-level permissions</li><li>Project state</li><li>Teamwork locks</li></ul></li></ul><h3>2. Checking If Children Can Be Created</h3><p>Use when creating <strong>new elements inside a parent</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="86abcebc-e46a-4a22-8a93-294a8162a51a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[boolean canCreate = element.canAddChild();
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>Checks if the parent allows <strong>any children</strong></li><li>Does <strong>not</strong> validate a specific child type</li></ul><h3>3. Checking If a Specific Child Can Be Added</h3><p>Use when adding an element to a parent.</p><p><strong>Basic check</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83730209-9bbd-4779-9aad-e42054856152"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[boolean allowed = parent.canAdd(child);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This validates:</p><ul><li>Type compatibility (e.g., cannot add a child of a kind which a parent is not allowed to own)</li><li>Ownership constraints</li><li>Teamwork permissions (if enabled)</li></ul><p><strong>Full permission check</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="941fe988-5166-4302-9247-4cb045df0043"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.permissions.ElementPermissionsManager;

boolean allowed = ElementPermissionsManager
    .getElementPermissionsManager()
    .canAddChild(parent, child);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This includes:</p><ul><li>Registered permission handlers</li><li>Custom rules</li><li>Environment-specific restrictions</li></ul><h3>4. Checking Deletion Permission</h3><p>Use before removing an element from the model.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7797fe15-8e49-4dd6-bd29-3174280ddde8"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[boolean canDelete = element.canBeDeleted();
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>or</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d437f23e-13a2-42de-9d8d-2aa397f71b01"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.permissions.ElementPermissionsManager;

boolean canDelete = ElementPermissionsManager
    .getElementPermissionsManager()
    .canDelete(element);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Deletion requires:</p><ul><li>Element is editable</li><li>Parent allows removal</li><li>Project is editable</li></ul><h3>5. Checking Moving Permission</h3><p>Moving an element is effectively:</p><ul><li>Removing from old parent</li><li>Adding to new parent</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c62c283c-0ea9-427f-ac7a-41388dad2196"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[boolean canMove = element.canChangeParent(elements, newParent);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This performs:</p><ul><li>Ownership validation</li><li>Cycle prevention</li><li>Permission checks (edit + add)</li></ul><h2><strong>Using ElementPermissionsManager</strong></h2><p>The ElementPermissionsManager provides a <strong>centralized permission evaluation layer</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca85cbb1-7572-489d-801e-c16016054e25"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.permissions.ElementPermissionsManager;

ElementPermissionsManager manager =
    ElementPermissionsManager.getElementPermissionsManager();

boolean editable = manager.isElementEditable(element);
boolean canCreate = manager.canCreateChildIn(element);
boolean canAdd = manager.canAddChild(parent, child);
boolean canDelete = manager.canDelete(element);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Key characteristics:</p><ul><li>Aggregates multiple permission sources</li><li>Delegates to registered ElementPermissions handlers</li><li>Supports caching for performance</li><li>Used internally by the platform</li></ul><h2><strong>Plugging Custom Permission Logic</strong></h2><p>You can define custom rules by implementing:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c698ae5a-8f02-49ba-8b3a-d31a907ef631"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.permissions.ElementPermissions;

public interface ElementPermissions
{
    boolean isElementEditable(BaseElement element);
    boolean canCreateChildIn(BaseElement element);
    boolean canAddChild(BaseElement parent, BaseElement child);
    boolean canDelete(BaseElement element);
}
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Then register it:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4acb928b-de5d-405c-9aeb-da6bac8d31c9"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.permissions.ElementPermissionsManager;

ElementPermissionsManager
    .getElementPermissionsManager()
    .addPermissionsHandler(new MyPermissionsHandler());
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Custom handlers are:</p><ul><li>Combined with existing handlers</li><li>Evaluated by the permissions manager</li><li>Suitable for:<ul><li>Domain-specific restrictions</li><li>Tooling constraints</li><li>Validation layers</li></ul></li></ul><h2><strong>Recommended Usage Pattern</strong></h2><p>Always check permissions <strong>before modifying the model</strong>:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d5e4ce8-216a-464c-b27a-9d221ceb17a4"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[if (!element.isEditable())
{
    return;
}

if (!parent.canAdd(child))
{
    return;
}

child.setOwner(parent);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For more complex scenarios, prefer the manager:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7191b348-14a7-46f5-aab0-b4bdf4a9df86"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.permissions.ElementPermissionsManager;

ElementPermissionsManager manager =
    ElementPermissionsManager.getElementPermissionsManager();

if (!manager.canAddChild(parent, child))
{
    return;
}
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Key Takeaways</strong></h2><ul><li>Permissions are <strong>not enforced automatically</strong> → must be checked explicitly</li><li>Use:<ul><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/uml/BaseElement.html#isEditable()">isEditable() </a>→ property changes</li><li><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/uml/BaseElement.html#canAddChild()">canAddChild()</a> / <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/uml/BaseElement.html#canAdd(com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement)">canAdd</a>() → containment</li><li>canBeDeleted() → removal</li></ul></li><li>Use <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/uml/permissions/ElementPermissionsManager.html">ElementPermissionsManager</a> for <strong>full permission evaluation</strong></li><li>Extend via <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/nomagic/magicdraw/uml/permissions/ElementPermissions.html">ElementPermissions</a> for <strong>custom rules</strong></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989411 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00028: (2026x Refresh1) Code engineering set

- page_id: `303989411`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989411/2026x+Refresh1+Code+engineering+set
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:36.628+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Code engineering using API
- labels: ['forward-engineering', 'reverse-engineering']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A *com.nomagic.magicdraw.ce.CodeEngineeringSet* object represents a code-engineering object as a resource for forward or reverse engineering.

Each *CodeEngineeringSet* provides properties, which can be read:

- Name . Represents a name of the code engineering set, visible in the Browser.
- Model elements . Represents elements that are added into the code engineering set from the model.
- W orking directory . Represents a code engineering directory where generated/reversed files are located.

###### **Forwar****d Engineering**

To perform the code generation, elements should be added to the *CodeEngineeringSet* object. Use the *CodeEngineeringSet.addElementsToCodeEngineeringSet(java.util.List<BaseElement[>)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ce/CodeEngineeringSet.html#addElementsToCodeEngineeringSet-java.util.List-)*method to add a list of model elements to the code engineering set.

Model elements should be in the working package, otherwise the model element is not be added to the code engineering set. The working package is set when creating a code engineering set.

**Revers****e Engineering**

Source code files are required to perform the Reverse Engineering. There are 2 methods available for adding files into the code engineering set:

1. CodeEngineeringSet.addFilesToCodeEngineeringSet(java.util.List<java.io.File>); This method adds the given list of files to the code engineering set.
2. CodeEngineeringSet.addAllFilesRecursivelyToCES(java.io.File); This method adds all specific source code files to the code engineering set, starting from the given directory.

Source code files should be in the working directory, in order to have the successful reverse.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Represents a name of the code engineering set, visible in the Browser.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Model elements</strong>. Represents elements that are added into the code engineering set from the model.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">W</strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">orking directory</strong>. Represents a code engineering directory where generated/reversed files are located. </li></ul><h4><strong>Forwar</strong><strong>d Engineering</strong></h4><p>To perform the code generation, elements should be added to the <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em> object. Use the <em>CodeEngineeringSet.addElementsToCodeEngineeringSet(java.util.List&lt;BaseElement<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ce/CodeEngineeringSet.html#addElementsToCodeEngineeringSet-java.util.List-">&gt;)</a> </em>method to add a list of model elements to the code engineering set.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81f4f76b-be13-4403-bd5d-4ad93de08e70"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Model elements should be in the working package, otherwise the model element is not be added to the code engineering set. The working package is set when creating a code engineering set.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);line-height: 1.4285715;">Revers</strong><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);line-height: 1.4285715;">e Engineering</strong></p></div><p>Source code files are required to perform the Reverse Engineering. There are 2 methods available for adding files into the code engineering set:</p><ol><li><em>CodeEngineeringSet.addFilesToCodeEngineeringSet(java.util.List&lt;java.io.File&gt;); </em>This method adds the given list of files to the code engineering set.</li><li><em>CodeEngineeringSet.addAllFilesRecursivelyToCES(java.io.File); </em>This method adds all specific source code files to the code engineering set, starting from the given directory.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee5c32f1-5d22-4d6c-b6ec-db6c906584b4"><ac:rich-text-body><p> Source code files should be in the working directory, in order to have the successful reverse.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989399 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00029: (2026x Refresh1) Code engineering using API

- page_id: `303989399`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989399/2026x+Refresh1+Code+engineering+using+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:35.765+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can perform Code Engineering using the Open API.

Code engineering allows generation of the source code from the specific model, and source code reversing into model elements. API is provided for:

- Code engineering sets creation for particular programming languages.
- Automatic component creation for every class involved in forward engineering and every file involved into reverse engineering.
- The specification of either working, or output, or temporary directories for processing source code files. The destination of the code reverse operation output can be any model package.

In the following topics we will review how to manage code engineering and how to do reverse and forward engineering.

More information is available in javadoc.

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\codeengineering

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can perform Code Engineering using the Open API.</p><p>Code engineering allows generation of the source code from the specific model, and source code reversing into model elements. API is provided for:</p><ul><li>Code engineering sets creation for particular programming languages.</li><li>Automatic component creation for every class involved in forward engineering and every file involved into reverse engineering.</li><li>The specification of either working, or output, or temporary directories for processing source code files. The destination of the code reverse operation output can be any model package.</li></ul><p>In the following topics we will review how to manage code engineering and how to do reverse and forward engineering.</p><p>More information is available in javadoc.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\codeengineering</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="435c38b8-d959-4e50-ab09-d696275959df"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="eb4629e6-9e17-496a-aa8e-a97a73d4d905"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989251 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00030: (2026x Refresh1) Collecting presentation elements, finding usages in diagrams

- page_id: `303989251`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989251/2026x+Refresh1+Collecting+presentation+elements+finding+usages+in+diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:27.402+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Presentation elements (symbols) > (2026x Refresh1) Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Very often you need to find out if some Element has a presentation element in a diagram. You also might need to collect all existing presentation elements for Element. There are different approaches for doing it. Choose the one which suites your needs.

Keep in mind that diagrams in MagicDraw uses lazy content loading approach. It means that content (presentation elements) are loaded only on demand. For example when diagram is opened.

**Use SymbolElementMap**

You can take this map using *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getSymbolElementMap()*. Map has API for collecting all symbols in all diagrams for some specific Element.

Presentation elements can be collected only from loaded diagrams. If diagram is not loaded, presentation elements of this diagram are not registered in a map.

Load diagram using *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement.ensureLoaded()*

****

**Find element usages in loaded and not loaded diagrams (do not load diagrams)**

Use this approach if you are not sure if presentation element exists in a diagram. This approach checks existing of presentation elements without loading a diagram.

*DiagramPresentationElement* API provides very useful methods for that. This API works both in loaded and not loaded diagrams.

*DiagramPresentationElement.getUsedModelElements(boolean)* to get all displayed elements in the diagram

Use** *DiagramPresentationElement.isElementInDiagram(* *Element)* to check if Element has a presentation element in a diagram

If you have not loaded diagram and you need to get presentation element of some symbol, you can do the following

- Use DiagramPresentationElement.isElementInDiagram( Element) to check if element is in diagram
- Use DiagramPresentationElement.*ensureLoaded()* to load a diagram
- Use Project.getSymbolElementMap() API to get a reference to presentation element in a diagram

****

**Find presentation elements in diagrams and load them**

Use this approach only if you are sure that presentation element of Element exists in diagram, because diagram will be loaded always.

Use *DiagramPresentationElement.findPresentationElement(Element, java.lang.Class)* method

More information is available in javadoc.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Very often you need to find out if some Element has a presentation element in a diagram. You also might need to collect all existing presentation elements for Element. There are different approaches for doing it. Choose the one which suites your needs.</p><p>Keep in mind that diagrams in MagicDraw uses lazy content loading approach. It means that content (presentation elements) are loaded only on demand. For example when diagram is opened.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Use SymbolElementMap</strong></p><p>You can take this map using <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getSymbolElementMap()</em>. Map has API for collecting all symbols in all diagrams for some specific Element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7f94ca3-a7c2-4ffc-b4a3-46dc51de4759"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Presentation elements can be collected only from <u>loaded</u> diagrams. If diagram is not loaded, presentation elements of this diagram are not registered in a map.</p><p>Load diagram using <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement.ensureLoaded()</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> <br /></strong></p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Find element usages in loaded and not loaded diagrams (do not load diagrams)</strong></p><p>Use this approach if you are not sure if presentation element exists in a diagram. This approach checks existing of presentation elements without loading a diagram.</p><p><em>DiagramPresentationElement</em> API provides very useful methods for that. This API works both in loaded and not loaded diagrams.</p><p><em>DiagramPresentationElement.getUsedModelElements(boolean)</em> to get all displayed elements in the diagram</p><p>Use<em> </em> <em>DiagramPresentationElement.isElementInDiagram(</em> <em>Element)</em> to check if Element has a presentation element in a diagram</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34d13d2a-0f3a-478a-afb7-93e04f472406"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you have not loaded diagram and you need to get presentation element of some symbol, you can do the following</p><ul><li>Use<em> </em> <em>DiagramPresentationElement.isElementInDiagram(</em> <em>Element)</em> to check if element is in diagram</li><li>Use <em>DiagramPresentationElement.<em>ensureLoaded() </em> </em> <em> </em>to load a diagram</li><li>Use <em>Project.getSymbolElementMap()</em> API to get a reference to presentation element in a diagram</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong> <br /></strong></p><p><strong>Find presentation elements in diagrams and load them </strong></p><p>Use this approach only if you are sure that presentation element of Element exists in diagram, because diagram will be loaded always.</p><p>Use <em>DiagramPresentationElement.findPresentationElement(Element, java.lang.Class)</em> method</p><p><br /></p><p>More information is available in javadoc.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989540 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00031: (2026x Refresh1) Command Line Interface (CLI) for user administration

- page_id: `303989540`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989540/2026x+Refresh1+Command+Line+Interface+CLI+for+user+administration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:40.869+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: HTTP request body

1323594362

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: HTTP request body

1323594382

1323594358

**On this page:**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: HTTP request body

1323594372

Besides using Teamwork Cloud Admin for administrative tasks, you can also use the Command Line Interface (CLI) to create and manage users, roles, and permissions. All of the CLI supported in Teamwork Cloud are properly defined on this page. You can also find them via Swagger where you can find the command-line description and issue it easily. We recommend you use CLI via [Swagger UI](https://osmc.nomagic.com/#/Administrator) because executing the command via Swagger also shows you the correct curl command. This link [https://osmc.nomagic.com/#/Administrator](https://osmc.nomagic.com/#/Administrator) takes you to the Teamwork Cloud CLI documentation in Swagger.

###### Assign a project role to a particular user

Sending POST to */osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resouceId}/roles/{roleId}/users* assigns a project role to a specified user.

```text

```

```text

```

**HTTP Status**

201 with empty body.

###### List all permissions

The */osmc/admin/permissions* lists all permissions in the server.

```text

```

```text

```

###### createroleCreate a role

Sending POST to */osmc/admin/roles* creates a row in the server.

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

###### Edit a role

Sending PATCH to */osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}* edits a row in the server. For the format of the request and the response, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Command Line Interface (CLI) for user administration' space=''].

###### Delete a role

DELETE to */osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}* deletes a row in the server.

###### Create a user

Sending POST to */osmc/admin/users* creates a new user. The user must not be an LDAP user.

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

###### Edit a user

Sending PATCH to */osmc/admin/users/{username}* edits a user.

```text

```

The key *userName* is ignored. By default, new **otherAttributes** are merged into the server. If the value of a key in **otherAttributes** is empty, the key will be removed from the server.

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

###### List all LDAP configurations

The */osmc/admin/ldaps* returns a JSON array containing all LDAP configurations in the server.

```text

```

```text

```

###### Import a user from LDAP

Sending POST to */osmc/admin/ldaps/{ldapId}/import{username}* imports an LDAP user into Teamwork Cloud.

```text

```

```text

```

###### Search a user in an LDAP server

Sending GET to */osmc/admin/ldaps/{ldapId}/search?<query string>* searches users from a specified LDAP server.

```text

```

```text

```

###### List all users

The */osmc/admin/users* returns a JSON array containing usernames in the server.

```text

```

```text

```

###### Get user information

The */osmc/admin/users/{username}* returns a JSON object containing information of a specified user.

```text

```

```text

```

###### LIst all roles

The */osmc/admin/roles* lists all rows in the server.

```text

```

```text

```

###### List users assigned to a particular role

The *_/osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}/users* lists users assigned to a particular role.

```text

```

```text

```

###### Unassign a role from a particular user

Sending HTTP DELETE to */admin/roles/{roleId}/users/{username}* unassigns a particular role from a specified user.

**HTTP Response**

204 with empty body.

###### Unassign a project role from a particular user

Sending DELETE to *_/osmc/workspaces/\{workspaceId\}/resources/\{resourceId\}/roles/\{roleId\}/users/\{username\}_* unassigns a project role from a particular user.

**HTTP Status**

204 with empty body.

###### Assign a role to a particular user

Sending POST to */osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}/users* assigns a role to a user.

```text

```

```text

```

**HTTP Status**

201 with empty body

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea0a8109-e778-4f46-931a-1ab165000bfc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1323594362</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f3efaac-6c35-4bfd-b0fd-06e754f5316a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1323594382</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f935161-ebdc-4951-9d6f-a75b2d8781c0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1323594358</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="26b55dcc-9a3e-4692-bb20-2b1d3572a5fa" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0d7474ee-1eb6-4659-85cc-a49625396ca3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1323594372</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Besides using Teamwork Cloud Admin for administrative tasks, you can also use the Command Line Interface (CLI) to create and manage users, roles, and permissions. This link <a href="https://osmc.nomagic.com/#/Administrator">https://osmc.nomagic.com/#/Administrator</a> takes you to the Teamwork Cloud CLI documentation in Swagger.</p><h4>Assign a project role to a particular user</h4><p>Sending POST to <em>/osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resouceId}/roles/{roleId}/users</em>  assigns a project role to a specified user.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2523ce80-55ce-4ac6-9056-6ba251a64e4a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: text/plain]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1db6103d-a876-4271-b03b-5057b71ef037"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[user1,user2]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>HTTP Status</strong></p><p>201 with empty body.</p><p><br /></p><h4>List all permissions</h4><p>The <em>/osmc/admin/permissions</em> lists all permissions in the server.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6fc347bf-a120-44da-90ec-90763f9fa0a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="15ac7364-2bca-4ad0-aa16-c234df537991"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[[{
    "operationAssignableAccessScope": "GLOBAL_ONLY",

    "protectedTypeDisplayName": "Project"
}, {

    "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.resource",
    "name": "com.nomagic.esi.resource_read.resource",
    "operationName": "read.resource",
    "ID": "9649cb30-6933-49f1-b309-7aade63340cc",
    "operationDisplayName": "Read Projects",

}, {
    "operationAssignableAccessScope": "GLOBAL_ONLY",

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca05d13f-084c-4758-a3c2-83c9f9f7dcdd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "permissions": ["9649cb30-6933-49f1-b309-7aade63340cc"],
    "name": "new role name",
    "description": "new row description",
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e54d0b6d-ac93-4170-be4a-c09152697cf2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="649f8efc-2bcd-4e90-909c-0efd7d5b7572"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "permissions": [{
        "operationAssignableAccessScope": {},
        "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.resource",
        "name": "com.nomagic.esi.resource_edit.resource",
        "operationName": "edit.resource",
        "ID": "0b972f77-368c-4511-9285-0069a1a8bf07",
        "operationDisplayName": "Edit Projects",
        "protectedTypeDisplayName": "Project"
    }],
    "name": "Project Contributor",
    "description": "Project-specific role. Users who are assigned to this role can modify content of selected project.",
    "ID": "417494bc-d0e8-449a-a8ac-5476dc2e6537"
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4>Edit a role</h4><p>Sending PATCH to <em>/osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}</em> edits a row in the server. For the format of the request and the response, see <ac:link ac:anchor="createrole"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a role]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Command Line Interface (CLI) for user administration" /></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><h4>Delete a role</h4><p>DELETE to <em>/osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}</em> deletes a row in the server.</p><h4>Create a user</h4><p>Sending POST to <em>/osmc/admin/users</em> creates a new user. The user must not be an LDAP user.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0c42859-2137-4b3d-9f45-b27d12ef19bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e27577da-6df9-4ce8-b122-089b29f81f77"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request body (note that the otherAttributes map must not contain realmid key)</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "userName": "test2",
	"password": "a passward",
    "otherAttributes": {
        "mobile": "456",
        "name": "a full name",
        "department": "department",
        "email": "user1@notset.com"
    },
    "enabled": true
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1618f2a1-9cec-4d4d-9bb5-34b9119876c0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="31827ac7-726d-4d71-9ab9-ecbc613b087b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "userName": "test2",
    "otherAttributes": {
        "mobile": "456",
        "name": "a full name",
        "department": "department",
        "email": "user1@notset.com"
    },
    "enabled": true
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4>Edit a user</h4><p>Sending PATCH to <em>/osmc/admin/users/{username}</em> edits a user. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e41558a-2f85-4837-a331-b87ab13f241d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The key <em>userName</em> is ignored. By default, new <strong>otherAttributes</strong> are merged into the server. If the value of a key in <strong>otherAttributes</strong> is empty, the key will be removed from the server.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e165216e-796b-4d06-b165-428df6004704"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "userName": "test2",
	"password": "a passward",
    "otherAttributes": {
        "mobile": "456",
        "name": "a full name",
        "department": "department",
        "email": "user1@notset.com"
    },
    "enabled": true
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f73afe86-a8fe-4f51-b4ea-2702acdb91f4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a204ef23-3565-4f79-a13c-265a4130f764"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "userName": "test2",
    "otherAttributes": {
        "mobile": "456",
        "name": "a full name",
        "department": "department",
        "email": "user1@notset.com"
    },
    "enabled": true
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4>List all LDAP configurations</h4><p>The <em>/osmc/admin/ldaps</em> returns a JSON array containing all LDAP configurations in the server.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81793cdb-7e88-4bae-9ff0-3be38292c4a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95405d87-0c87-4d9a-af74-8a86524cc5d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[[{
    "environment": {
        "authentype": "simple",
        "searchbase": "ou=Users,dc=nomagicasia,dc=com",
        "authen_dntype": "template",
        "anoymousbind": "false",
        "ldap_realm_name": "ldap0",
        "java.naming.factory.initial": "com.sun.jndi.ldap.LdapCtxFactory",
        "java.naming.security.protocol": "none",
        "userDNTemplate": "uid={0},ou=Users,dc=nomagicasia,dc=com",
        "com.sun.jndi.ldap.read.timeout": "500",
        "java.naming.provider.url": "ldap://ldap.th.nomagic.com:389",
        "weight": "1073741823",
        "enabled": "true"
    },
    "protocol": "ldap",
    "port": "389",
    "IP": "ldap.th.nomagic.com",
    "ID": "36f8b736-f51c-43a2-a9a6-63905419838a",
    "authen": "simple",
    "userName": "cn=admin,dc=nomagicasia,dc=com",
    "url": "ldap://ldap.th.nomagic.com:389"
}]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4>Import a user from LDAP</h4><p>Sending POST to <em>/osmc/admin/ldaps/{ldapId}/import{username}</em> imports an LDAP user into Teamwork Cloud.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4e4039fe-5cc2-4fba-b8f3-4f59dfa3eef1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="633ae014-fa29-4246-8bd2-aad4e767890f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "userName": "test2",
    "password": "a passward",
    "otherAttributes": {
        "mobile": "456",
        "realmid": "36f8b736-f56c-43a2-a9a6-54128919838a",
        "name": "a full name",
        "department": "department",
        "email": "user1@notset.com"
    },
    "enabled": true
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4>Search a user in an LDAP server</h4><p>Sending GET to <em>/osmc/admin/ldaps/{ldapId}/search?&lt;query string&gt;</em> searches users from a specified LDAP server.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="716cf3c7-d0a7-497e-9c85-e858fbaea137"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="833c82a9-15ff-4d4a-830a-a64b297e4c18"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[[{
    "mobile": "0123456789",
    "fullName": "Bat",
    "department": "production",
    "userName": "bat",
    "email": "bat@nma.com",
    "userDN": "uid=bat,ou=Users,dc=nomagicasia,dc=com"
}, {
    "mobile": "",
    "fullName": "abc",
    "department": "",
    "userName": "abc",
    "email": "",
    "userDN": "uid=abc,ou=Users,dc=nomagicasia,dc=com"
}, {
    "mobile": "",
    "fullName": "aaaa",
    "department": "",
    "userName": "aaa",
    "email": "",
    "userDN": "uid=aaa,ou=Users,dc=nomagicasia,dc=com"
}]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4>List all users</h4><p>The <em>/osmc/admin/users</em> returns a JSON array containing usernames in the server.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0eedc27c-44a6-49ae-87c3-b4cbb4d56698"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="021da6af-cb78-46e6-90dc-2d0a3b4b6d14"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[["admin", "observer1", "user1", "user2", "worker1"]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4>Get user information</h4><p>The <em>/osmc/admin/users/{username}</em> returns a JSON object containing information of a specified user.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb1b2b61-98a6-4e4c-b20f-204926726d51"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aff6d549-2ed1-4bbc-ac71-3c3da655be30"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
````
