## PAGE 00046: (2026x Refresh1) Copying elements and symbols

- page_id: `303989431`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989431/2026x+Refresh1+Copying+elements+and+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:37.709+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can copy model elements and symbols either to another location in the same project or to another project. This must be done in the same session.

There are two modes of making copies:

- Deep copying (with new data)
- Shallow copying (with reused data)

###### **Example #1: Copying an element**

****

```java

```

###### **Example #2: Copying multiple elements and symbols**

```java

```

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>**\openapi\examples\copypaste*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Checking Model Element Permissions' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>You can copy model elements and symbols either to another location in the same project or to another project. This must be done in the same session.</p><p>There are two modes of making copies:</p><ul><li>Deep copying (with new data)</li><li>Shallow copying (with reused data)</li></ul><p> </p><h4><strong>Example #1: Copying an element</strong></h4><p> </p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="deac64b1-d060-474a-a06a-bf2681845ca5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Element element = ...; // An element to copy/paste.
    Element parent = ...; // A parent to which the element has to be pasted: either the same project or another project.
    Project project = ...;
    SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance();
    sessionManager.createSession(project, "Clone");
 
    // A 3rd parameter indicates whether an element name uniqueness should be preserved in the parent.
    CopyPasting.copyPasteElement(element, parent, true);
 
    sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"> </p></div><h4><strong>Example #2: Copying multiple elements and symbols</strong></h4><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88ba897e-6dca-4f24-b872-a494d56d1ed5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    List elements = ...; // Elements to copy/paste. 
    List views = ...; // Symbols to copy/paste.
    Element parent = ...; // A parent to which elements should be pasted: either the same project or another project.
    BaseElement symbolParent = ...; // A parent to which symbols should be pasted. 
    Project project = ...;
    SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance();
    sessionManager.createSession(project, "Clone");


    // A 4th parameter indicates whether deep or shallow copy is applied.
    // A 5th parameter indicates whether an element name uniqueness should be preserved in the parent.
    List baseElements = CopyPasting.copyPasteElements(views, parent, symbolParent, true, true);
 
    sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;</em><em>\openapi\examples\copypaste</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b50b2c6-e274-460d-b17d-b7fdbdbe92c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Checking Model Element Permissions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989305 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00047: (2026x Refresh1) Create OCL2.0 validation rule

- page_id: `303989305`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989305/2026x+Refresh1+Create+OCL2.0+validation+rule
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:29.848+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Creating validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The OCL2.0 validation rule describes the validation logic using OCL2.0.

To create the OCL2.0 validation rule

1. Create a constraint.
2. Set the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule» for the validation rule.
3. Set the severity level, error message, and abbreviation.
4. Specify constrained element(s).
5. Specify the specification language OCL2.0
6. Enter the OCL2.0 expression as a body of the specification
7. Add/import the created validation rule to a validation suite

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating validation rules' space='']

The OCL2.0 validation rule can be used in an active validation suite. In this case the validation rule will be executed on any change of constrained elements that are from the validation scope. Executing of the validation rule might be triggered even if no properties important to the validation rule actually changed and this can slow down the active validating speed. In order to avoid degradation of the performance you can create a binary validation rule that uses the OCL2.0 expression to validate model elements but also provides additional information for the modeling tool that allows to optimize triggering of executing after model elements change. For example, we want to check whether all classes have names. This can be done by creating the OCL2.0 validation rule and specifying the OLC2.0 expression:

```text
name <> ‘’
```

The problem is that such validation rule actually is interested in a class property name value change but it will be executed on every property value of a class change. How we can fix this problem and inform the modeling tool to execute the validation rule only on the class property name change:

1. Create a constraint.
2. Set the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule» for the validation rule.
3. Set the severity level, error message, and abbreviation.
4. Specify constrained element(s).
5. Specify the specification language OCL2.0
6. Enter the OCL2.0 expression as a body of the specification
7. Create a Java class that extends the com.nomagic.magicdraw.validation.DefaultValidationRuleImpl class
8. Override the method DefaultValidationRuleImpl.getListenerConfigurations()

```java
, Collection> getListenerConfigurations()
        {
            Map, Collection> configs = 
						new HashMap, Collection>();
            Collection configsForClass = new ArrayList();
            configsForClass.add(SmartListenerConfig.NAME_CONFIG);
            configs.put(com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class.class, configsForClass);
            return configs;
        }]]>
```

9. Specify the validation rule’s implementation property value - a qualified name of the class

10. Add/import the created validation rule to a validation suite

See the *MyOCLBasedValidationRuleImpl.java* example in the *<program installation directory>\openapi\examples\validation* directory.

The implementation class must be in the classpath of the modeling tool or if the implementation class is in a plugin then the plugin’s classloader must be registered to the validation system (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Binary validation rule in the plugin' space='']).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The OCL2.0 validation rule describes the validation logic using OCL2.0.</p><p>To create the OCL2.0 validation rule</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a constraint.</li><li>Set the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule» for the validation rule.</li><li>Set the severity level, error message, and abbreviation.</li><li>Specify constrained element(s).</li><li>Specify the specification language OCL2.0</li><li>Enter the OCL2.0 expression as a body of the specification</li><li>Add/import the created validation rule to a validation suite</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f368d5fe-805a-447b-a01b-fca122ddd589"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating validation rules" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>The OCL2.0 validation rule can be used in an active validation suite. In this case the validation rule will be executed on any change of constrained elements that are from the validation scope. Executing of the validation rule might be triggered even if no properties important to the validation rule actually changed and this can slow down the active validating speed. In order to avoid degradation of the performance you can create a binary validation rule that uses the OCL2.0 expression to validate model elements but also provides additional information for the modeling tool that allows to optimize triggering of executing after model elements change. For example,  we want to check whether all classes have names. This can be done by creating the OCL2.0 validation rule and specifying the OLC2.0 expression:</p></div><pre>    name &lt;&gt; ‘’</pre><p>The problem is that such validation rule actually is interested in a class property name value change but it will be executed on every property value of a class change. How we can fix this problem and inform the modeling tool to execute the validation rule only on the class property name change:</p><ol><li>Create a constraint.</li><li>Set the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule» for the validation rule.</li><li>Set the severity level, error message, and abbreviation.</li><li>Specify constrained element(s).</li><li>Specify the specification language OCL2.0</li><li>Enter the OCL2.0 expression as a body of the specification</li><li>Create a Java class that extends the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.validation.DefaultValidationRuleImpl</em> class</li><li>Override the method<em> DefaultValidationRuleImpl.getListenerConfigurations()</em></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="973fd9f7-00bc-4430-9d30-eb80eb0615b2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[        public Map<Class<? extends Element>, Collection<SmartListenerConfig>> getListenerConfigurations()
        {
            Map<Class<? extends Element>, Collection<SmartListenerConfig>> configs = 
						new HashMap<Class<? extends Element>, Collection<SmartListenerConfig>>();
            Collection<SmartListenerConfig> configsForClass = new ArrayList<SmartListenerConfig>();
            configsForClass.add(SmartListenerConfig.NAME_CONFIG);
            configs.put(com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class.class, configsForClass);
            return configs;
        }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>    9. Specify the validation rule’s implementation property value - a qualified name of the class</p><p>    10. Add/import the created validation rule to a validation suite</p><p><br /></p><p>See the <em>MyOCLBasedValidationRuleImpl.java</em> example in the <em>&lt;program installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\validation</em> directory.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32f9080c-2334-45c1-b680-ee01eae87bd7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The implementation class must be in the classpath of the modeling tool or if the implementation class is in a plugin then the plugin’s classloader must be registered to the validation system (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Binary validation rule in the plugin" /></ac:link>).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303988979 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00048: (2026x Refresh1) Creating a custom dependency matrix type

- page_id: `303988979`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303988979/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+a+custom+dependency+matrix+type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:11.097+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Working with Dependency Matrix
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a custom dependency matrix type:

1. 
2. .

###### **creatingCreatin****g a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Plugins' space=''] for custom dependency matrix type**

Use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixConfigurator* class to register components of the new dependency matrix type as shown in the following example:

```java

```

Remember the name of the new dependency matrix type that you pass as an argument while creating a new instance of the *DependencyMatrixConfigurator* class (in the preceding example, it is “Sample Extended Matrix”). You will have to specify the exact name when registering a new diagram type.

The sample of more detailed configuration code is provided in *<program installation folder>\openapi\examples\dependencymatrix*.

###### **registeringRegisterin****g a new dependency matrix type in UI**

Once the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Plugins' space='']is created, you have to register the new dependency matrix type by using the **Customize****Dependency Matrix Wizard**dialog. Only then it will be added to program toolbars and menus.

To register a new dependency matrix type

1. Start a modeling tool, for example, MagicDraw.
2. On the Diagrams menu, click Customize . The Customize Diagrams dialog opens.
3. Click Create and then select Dependency Matrix Type . The Customize Dependency Matrix W izard dialog opens.
4. In the Type box, specify the name of the new dependency matrix type. The value must be the same as the one passed to the DependencyMatrixConfigurator class as the argument. [ATTACHMENT filename='CustomizeMatrixWizard.png']

*Specifying name of new Dependency Matrix type*

5. In the **Abbreviation**box, specify the abbreviation of the new dependency matrix type.

6. Click **Finish**.

7. Restart your modeling tool.

You can now create a custom dependency matrix.

For more information on how to customize a new dependency matrix type by using the **Customize Dependency Matrix Wizard**dialog, **see “Creating New Dependency Matrix Type” in theMagicDraw UMLProfiling&DSL UserGuide.pdf.**

````html
    {
        @Override
        public void init()
        {
            DependencyMatrixConfigurator.registerConfiguration(new DependencyMatrixSampleConfigurator("Sample Extended Matrix"));

        public boolean close()

        public boolean isSupported()
        {
            return true;
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c1f58ccb-07d4-45bb-846c-efc41fe67055"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Remember the name of the new dependency matrix type that you pass as an argument while creating a new instance of the <em>DependencyMatrixConfigurator</em> class (in the preceding example, it is “Sample Extended Matrix”). Only then it will be added to program toolbars and menus.</p><p>To register a new dependency matrix type</p><hr /><ol><li>Start a modeling tool, for example, MagicDraw.</li><li>On the <strong>Diagrams </strong>menu, click <strong>Customize</strong>. The <strong>Customize Dependency Matrix </strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">W</strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">izard </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Type </strong>box, specify the name of the new dependency matrix type. ## PAGE 00049: (2026x Refresh1) Creating a diagram
````

- page_id: `303989206`

- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989206/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+a+diagram

- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every diagram has a diagram type and this type should be provided during diagram creation.

An example shows how to create and add a diagram to a parent element:

```java

```

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><div>Every diagram has a diagram type and this type should be provided during diagram creation.</div><div>An example shows how to create and add a diagram to a parent element:</div><div><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7545b1a6-1ac5-499e-9b55-35faef700a9b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project = ...;
    Package parent = project.getPrimaryModel();
    // create a new session
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Create a diagram");
    try
    {
        //a class diagram is created and added to a parent model element
        Diagram diagram = ModelElementsManager.getInstance().createDiagram(DiagramTypeConstants.UML_CLASS_DIAGRAM, parent);
        //open a diagram 
        project.getDiagram(diagram).open();
    }
    catch (ReadOnlyElementException e)
    {
    }
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f7849a87-66ab-4f1a-96c8-df51caad3ac7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating an execution engine, you are required to implement three abstract methods in the Subclass of ExecutionEngine (see JavaDoc for more details). These abstract methods are as follows

```text

```

API developers can insert any desired code into the above abstract methods and get the events when the execution engine is initialized, executed, and terminated. 
 You can execute the following code to create a MyExecutionEngine class

```text

```

The created class MyExecutionEngine must be returned from createEngine() of its descriptor as defined in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating an execution engine descriptor' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When creating an execution engine, you are required to implement three abstract methods in the Subclass of ExecutionEngine (see JavaDoc for more details). These abstract methods are as follows<br /> <span style="color: rgb(153,51,102);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6dab922e-1f3f-47d9-a939-2d7f2e34ae4c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	public abstract class ExecutionEngine {
		...
		public abstract void init(Element element);
		public abstract void execute(Element element);
		public abstract void onClose();
		...
	} ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> API developers can insert any desired code into the above abstract methods and get the events when the execution engine is initialized, executed, and terminated. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> You can execute the following code to create a MyExecutionEngine class</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c5c36737-bb57-4b71-b927-fe7663c85a59"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	public class MyExecutionEngine extends ExecutionEngine {

		public MyExecutionEngine(ExecutionEngineDescriptor engineDescriptor) {
			super(engineDescriptor);
		} 
	
		@Override
		public void execute(Element element) {
		...
		} 
		
		@Override
		public void init(Element element) {
			Project project = Project.getProject(element);
			// add engine listener if needed
			EngineListener listener = new MyEngineListener(project);
			addEngineListener(listener);
		} 

		@Override
		public void onClose() {
		...
		}
	} ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />The created class MyExecutionEngine must be returned from createEngine() of its descriptor as defined in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating an execution engine descriptor" /></ac:link>.</p>
````

## PAGE 00051: (2026x Refresh1) Creating an execution engine descriptor

- page_id: `303989382`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989382/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+an+execution+engine+descriptor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:34.626+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 > (2026x Refresh1) Alf Plugin Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x Refresh1) Java APIs > (2026x Refresh1) Engine
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following example shows how to create an Execution Engine Descriptor

```text
getValidationSuite();
	}]]>
```

The new execution engine descriptor must implement the ExecutionEngineDescriptor or extend the AbstractExecutionEngineDescriptor abstract Class. You can find details about these Classes in JavaDoc.

```text

```

The created execution engine descriptor must then be registered to the simulation manager (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Registering an execution engine to the Simulation Manager' space='']).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0b5f501d-cf79-4fde-bd45-cfea051e77b6"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2986d1da-593f-473f-88bf-adc22be60bc1">The following example shows how to create an Execution Engine Descriptor</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><br /> <span style="color: rgb(127,0,85);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="1717ad49-21fb-46b4-9eef-5f836bc57e86" ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	public interface ExecutionEngineDescriptor {
		String getEngineName();
		ImageIcon getEngineIcon();
		boolean canExecute(Element element);
		ExecutionEngine createEngine();
		boolean canAnimate(PresentationElement element);
		boolean isAutoDiagramOpened();
		boolean canDebug();
		boolean canUserTriggerEvents();
		boolean isDiagramPerSession();
		boolean isHasIdle();
		boolean isFindEngine(Element element);
		List<? Extends ValidationSuite> getValidationSuite();
	}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>The new execution engine descriptor must implement the ExecutionEngineDescriptor or extend the AbstractExecutionEngineDescriptor abstract Class. You can find details about these Classes in JavaDoc.<br /> <span style="color: rgb(127,0,85);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="300849dc-2a03-4030-8de9-bc925d475c22" ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	public class MyExecutionEngineDescriptor extends AbstractExecutionEngineDescriptor {
	@Override
	
	public boolean canExecute(Element element) {
		return element instanceof Interaction;
	} 
	
	@Override
	public ExecutionEngine createEngine() {
		return new MyExecutionEngine(this);
	} 

	@Override
	public String getEngineName() {
		return "My Execution Engine";
}
} ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> The created execution engine descriptor must then be registered to the simulation manager (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Registering an execution engine to the Simulation Manager" /></ac:link>).</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989403 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00052: (2026x Refresh1) Creating an execution engine listener

- page_id: `303989403`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989403/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+an+execution+engine+listener
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:36.038+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 > (2026x Refresh1) Alf Plugin Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x Refresh1) Java APIs > (2026x Refresh1) Engine
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following example shows how to create an engine listener

```text
values) { 
		System.out.printIn("--Activate Element-- : element name = " ((NamedElement)element).getName());
	} 

	@Override
	public void elementDeactivated(Element element, Collection> values) { 
		System.out.printIn("--Deactivate Element-- : element name = " + ((NamedElement)element).getName());
	} 

	@Override
	public void eventTriggered(String eventID) {
		NamedElement element = (NamedElement)project.getElementByID(eventID);
		System.out.printIn("--Event Trigger-- : event name = " + ((NamedElement)element).getName());
	} 

	@Override
	public void executionTerminated() {
		System.out.printIn("--Engine Terminated--");
	}
} ]]>
```

Once you have created an EngineListener, you can register it to the specified ExecutionEngine if you want to receive events occurring in each execution engine. All engine listeners of a specific engine will be activated under the conditions as follows

- An element is activated.
- An element is deactivated.
- An event is triggered.
- An engine is terminated.

```text
values); 
		void elementDeactivated(Element element, Collection> values); 
		void eventTriggered(String eventID); 
		void executionTerminated();	
	}]]>
```

See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating a new execution engine' space=''] for more information about adding execution engine listeners to the *MyExecutionEngine* class.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

You can add more than one execution engine listener to an execution engine.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The following example shows how to create an engine listener<br /> <span style="color: rgb(127,0,85);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a808a382-6c31-4dd6-aeb3-054feb1defd9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	public class MyEngineListener implements EngineListener {
	
	private Project project; 
		public MyEngineListener(Project project) {
		this.project = project;
	} 

	@Override
	public void elementActivated(Element element, Collection<?> values) { 
		System.out.printIn("--Activate Element-- : element name = " ((NamedElement)element).getName());
	} 

	@Override
	public void elementDeactivated(Element element, Collection<?> values) { 
		System.out.printIn("--Deactivate Element-- : element name = " + ((NamedElement)element).getName());
	} 

	@Override
	public void eventTriggered(String eventID) {
		NamedElement element = (NamedElement)project.getElementByID(eventID);
		System.out.printIn("--Event Trigger-- : event name = " + ((NamedElement)element).getName());
	} 

	@Override
	public void executionTerminated() {
		System.out.printIn("--Engine Terminated--");
	}
} ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> Once you have created an EngineListener, you can register it to the specified ExecutionEngine if you want to receive events occurring in each execution engine. All engine listeners of a specific engine will be activated under the conditions as follows</p><ul><li>An element is activated.</li><li>An element is deactivated.</li><li>An event is triggered.</li><li>An engine is terminated.</li></ul><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68658132-16ea-476d-8dc7-54965276de92"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	public interface EngineListener { 
		void elementActivated(Element element, Collection<?> values); 
		void elementDeactivated(Element element, Collection<?> values); 
		void eventTriggered(String eventID); 
		void executionTerminated();	
	}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> See <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a new execution engine" /></ac:link> for more information about adding execution engine listeners to the <em>MyExecutionEngine</em> class.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a3f3f95e-b48a-4621-8a08-e79f342933b5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>You can add more than one execution engine listener to an execution engine.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00053: (2026x Refresh1) Creating and registering a new SimulationExecutionListener

- page_id: `303989416`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989416/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+and+registering+a+new+SimulationExecutionListener
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:37.016+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 > (2026x Refresh1) Alf Plugin Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x Refresh1) Java APIs > (2026x Refresh1) Execution
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Acom.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationExecutionListener is a listener for various events that can occur during execution of a model.

```text
values, ActivationContext context); 
		void elementDeactivated(Element element, Collection> values, ActivationContext context); 
		void eventTriggered(SignalInstance signal); 
		void operationCalled(Operation operation, List parameterValues, Object_ caller, Object_ target, boolean isSynchronous); 
		void behaviorCalled(Behavior behavior, List parameterValues, Object_ caller, Object_ target, boolean isSynchronous); 
		void objectCreated(Object_ sender, Object_ object); 
        void beforeObjectDestroyed(Object_ object)
        void valueChange(StructuredValue context, FeatureValue feature, Object oldValue, Object newValue)
        void objectStateActivated(StructuredValue context, State newState) 
		void busyStatusChange(StructuredValue context, Object oldValue, Object newValue);
    }]]>
```

To register a created execution listener to a global list, that ensures this listener to be added to each created SimulationExecution, use the following code:

```text

```

If the SimulationExecution is already created, listener can be registered directly to that specific execution by calling the following:

| Open API | Function |
| --- | --- |
| SimulationManager.registerSimulationExecutionListener(SimulationExecutionListener listener, SimulationExecution execution) | Registers SimulationExecutionListener dynamically during the execution. |
| SimulationManager.unregisterSimulationExecutionListener(SimulationExecutionListener listener, SimulationExecution execution) | Unregisters SimulationExecutionListener. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989469 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00054: (2026x Refresh1) Creating custom drag and drop handlers

- page_id: `303989469`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989469/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+custom+drag+and+drop+handlers
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:38.513+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

MagicDraw itself has many default drag and drop handlers for doing various things. Such handlers consist of :

- Dropping elements from various UI components(trees, specification windows, etc.) diagram or its presentation elements.
- Dropping external application items onto trees or diagrams.

Default drag and drop handlers for example create new presentation elements, change the properties of elements, external application image drop on diagram creates an image and etc. Same is true if presentation elements are dropped on top of other presentation elements.

API allows to register a custom drag and drop handler for doing some specific things using drag and drop mechanism.

OpenAPI drag and drop handler registrations:

- Use com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.CustomDropDiagramHandlerFactory.register( com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.CustomDragAndDropHandlerFactory) to register a factory which creates a handler for dropping elements from browser(trees) into diagram.
- Use com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.CustomShapeMoveHandlerFactory.register(com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.CustomDragAndDropHandlerFactory[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/dnd/CustomShapeMoveHandlerFactory.html#register-com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.CustomDragAndDropHandler-) to register a factory which creates a handler for dropping one presentation element on other presentation element in the same diagram
- Use com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.BrowserTabTreeDragAndDropHandlerRegistry#register(com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.BrowserTabTreeDragAndDropHandlerFactory) to register a factory which creates a handler for dropping external application items into the browser tree nodes(elements).
- Use com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.DiagramTransferableDragAndDropHandlerRegistry#register(com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.DiagramTransferableDragAndDropHandlerFactory) to register a factory which creates a handler for dropping external application items into the diagram.

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory> \openapi\examples\customdraganddrop

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/dnd/CustomShapeMoveHandlerFactory.html#register-com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.CustomDragAndDropHandler-">)</a></em> to register a factory which creates a handler for dropping one presentation element on other presentation element in the same diagram</li><li>Use <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989257 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00055: (2026x Refresh1) Creating diagram images

- page_id: `303989257`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989257/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+diagram+images
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:27.671+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Open API of a modeling tool provides the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.export.image.ImageExporter* class for creating images from the whole diagram or just a part of it.

Image formats are predefined as constants in *ImageExporter*.

To create an image from the whole diagram, use the *export(DiagramPresentationElement, int, java.io.File)*method.

To create an image from selected symbols in the diagram, use the *export(DiagramPresentationElement**, int, java.io.File, boolean)*method.

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\imagegenerator*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Open API of a modeling tool provides the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.export.image.ImageExporter</em> class for creating images from the whole diagram or just a part of it.</p><p>Image formats are predefined as constants in <em>ImageExporter</em>. </p><p>To create an image from the whole diagram, use the <em>export(DiagramPresentationElement, int, java.io.File) </em>method.</p><p>To create an image from selected symbols in the diagram, use the <em>export(DiagramPresentationElement</em><em>, int, java.io.File, boolean) </em>method.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\imagegenerator</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989598 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00056: (2026x Refresh1) Creating JUnit test case

- page_id: `303989598`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989598/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+JUnit+test+case
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:44.546+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Plugins > (2026x Refresh1) Creating test cases
- labels: ['magicdraw-test-case']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Let's use MagicDraw as an example to describe the creation of JUnit test cases.

###### JUnit 3 test case

The JUnit test case should be created by extending the abstract *com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawTestCase* class from Open API (see the following *MyTest* sample). The *MagicDrawTestCase* class starts MagicDraw automatically for each test case method defined in *MagicDrawTestCase* and performs the memory leak test after each completed test case.

*MagicDrawTestCase* provides default and specific constructors for creating a test case instance with a specific custom name for the specific test case method. Test cases with specific names might be helpful when several instances of the same test case are created and analyzed.

The *MagicDrawTestCase* initialization and tear down should be implemented in overridden *setUpTest()* and *tearDownTest()* methods. The standard JUnit method *suite* might be used for preparing the tests suite with several instances of test cases which may use different test data.

```java

```

*MagicDrawTestCase* also provides methods for opening, saving, and closing MagicDraw projects. It also performs the memory leak test after the test case has been completed and the project has been closed. The memory leak test for the whole test case can be disabled using the*setMemoryTestReady(boolean)* method, while the *setSkipMemoryTest(boolean)* method can be used to disable the memory leak test on closing the MagicDraw project.

The list of required MagicDraw plugins for the test case can be configured by overriding the *getRequiredPlugins()* method of *MagicDrawTestCase*. The overridden method implementation should return the list of required plugins IDs. Textual information about required plugins and their loading status can be obtained using *getRequiredPluginsDescription()* and *isRequiredPluginsLoaded()* methods.

Textual information about the test process can be logged using the Log4j logger. The test case logger for the TEST category can be accessed using the *getLogger()* method of *MagicDrawTestCase*. More information about the Log4j logger configuration and usage can be found at[http://logging.apache.org/log4j/1.2/manual.html.](http://logging.apache.org/log4j/1.2/manual.html)

###### JUnit 4 test case

The JUnit4 test class should be annotated to use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawTestRunner* from Open API (see a code sample bellow). The** *MagicDrawTestRunner* class starts MagicDraw automatically and performs the memory leak test after each completed test case.

```java

```

###### JUnit 5 test case

The JUnit5 test class should be annotated to extend *com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawApplication* from Open API (see a code sample bellow). The**MagicDrawApplication**extension starts MagicDraw automatically before running the test cases.

```java

```

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>Let's use MagicDraw as an example to describe the creation of JUnit test cases.</p><h4>JUnit 3 test case</h4><p>The JUnit test case should be created by extending the abstract <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawTestCase</em> class from Open API (see the following <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MyTest</em> sample). The <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">MagicDrawTestCase</em> class starts MagicDraw automatically for each test case method defined in <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> MagicDrawTestCase </em> and performs the memory leak test after each completed test case.</p><p><em> MagicDrawTestCase </em> provides default and specific constructors for creating a test case instance with a specific custom name for the specific test case method. Test cases with specific names might be helpful when several instances of the same test case are created and analyzed.</p></div><p>The  <em>MagicDrawTestCase</em> initialization and tear down should be implemented in overridden <em> setUpTest() </em> and <em>tearDownTest()</em> methods. The standard JUnit method <em>suite</em> might be used for preparing the tests suite with several instances of test cases which may use different test data.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a4627c32-1eab-4e4f-b541-30e9277442d7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    import com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawTestCase;
    public class MyTest extends MagicDrawTestCase
    {
        public MyTest(String testMethodToRun, String testName)
        {
            super(testMethodToRun, testName);
        }
 
        @Override
        protected void setUpTest() throws Exception
        {
            super.setUpTest();
            //do setup here
        }
 
        @Override
        protected void tearDownTest() throws Exception
        {
            super.tearDownTest();
            //do tear down here
        }
 
        public void testSomething()
        {
              //implement the unit test here
        }
 
        public static Test suite() throws Exception
        {
            //you may create a test suite with several instances of the test. 
            TestSuite suite = new TestSuite();
            suite.addTest(new MyTest("testSomething", "MagicDraw Test Sample"));
            suite.addTest(new MyTest("testSomething", "Another MagicDraw Test Sample"));
            return suite;
        }
    }

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><em>MagicDrawTestCase</em> also provides methods for opening, saving, and closing MagicDraw projects. It also performs the memory leak test after the test case has been completed and the project has been closed. The memory leak test for the whole test case can be disabled using the<em> setMemoryTestReady(boolean) </em> method, while the <em>setSkipMemoryTest(boolean)</em> method can be used to disable the memory leak test on closing the MagicDraw project.</p><p>The list of required MagicDraw plugins for the test case can be configured by overriding the <em>getRequiredPlugins()</em> method of <em>MagicDrawTestCase</em>. The overridden method implementation should return the list of required plugins IDs. Textual information about required plugins and their loading status can be obtained using <em> getRequiredPluginsDescription() </em> and <em>isRequiredPluginsLoaded()</em> methods.</p><p>Textual information about the test process can be logged using the Log4j logger. The test case logger for the TEST category can be accessed using the <em>getLogger()</em> method of <em> MagicDrawTestCase</em>. More information about the Log4j logger configuration and usage can be found at<a href="http://logging.apache.org/log4j/1.2/manual.html"> http://logging.apache.org/log4j/1.2/manual.html.</a></p><h4>JUnit 4 test case</h4><p>The JUnit4 test class should be annotated to use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawTestRunner</em> from Open API (see a code sample bellow). The<em> </em> <em>MagicDrawTestRunner</em> class starts MagicDraw automatically and performs the memory leak test after each completed test case.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="473ea7c3-30e9-4279-8205-7333d03d41eb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawTestRunner;
import org.junit.*;
import org.junit.runner.RunWith;

@RunWith(MagicDrawTestRunner.class)
public class MyTest
{
   @Before
   public void beforeTestBegins()
   {
      //do test case setup here, e.g. load project
   }

   @Test
   public void testSomething()
   {
      //implement the unit test case here
   }

   @After
   public void afterTestDone()
   {
      //do tear down here, e.g. close project
   }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>JUnit 5 test case</h4><p>The JUnit5 test class should be annotated to extend <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawApplication</em> from Open API (see a code sample bellow). The<em> <em>MagicDrawApplication</em> </em>extension starts MagicDraw automatically before running the test cases.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28dbef5b-f677-453a-8c52-c95a6b72c731"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawApplication;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.*;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.ExtendWith;

@ExtendWith(MagicDrawApplication.class)
public class MyTest
{
   @BeforeEach
   void beforeTestBegins()
   {
      //do test case setup here, e.g. load project
   }

   @Test
   void testSomething()
   {
      //implement the unit test case here
   }

   @AfterEach
   void afterTestDone()
   {
      //do tear down here, e.g. close project
   }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f0c867d-301c-4da9-a3c2-78929aa6fca7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On this page</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="00bd8cc7-7484-43a6-a882-888fff6df655" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><br /><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p> </ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303988947 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00057: (2026x Refresh1) Creating new actions

- page_id: `303988947`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303988947/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+new+actions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:09.717+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Adding new functionality
- labels: ['create-command', 'create-button', 'custom-command', 'custom-button']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

****You can find the code examples in

- < modeling tool installation direc tory>\openapi\examples\actiontypes
- < modeling tool installation direc tory>\openapi\examples\simpleconfigurators
- < modeling tool installation direc tory>\openapi\examples\selectionactions

##### Step #1: Create a new action class

All actions used in a modeling tool must be subclasses of the*com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.MDAction* class (seejavadocfor more details). The following*MDAction*subclasses that are used for basic purposes are already created in a modeling tool:

- com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.actions.DefaultBrowserAction – an action class, used for a browser action. It enables to access some browser tree and nodes and is recommended to use for performing some actions with the selected browser nodes.
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.actions.DefaultDiagramAction – an action class for a diagram action. It enables to access some diagram elements and is recommended to use for performing some actions with the selected diagram elements.
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.PropertyAction – an action for changing some element or application property. It can be used for changing properties defined by a user.

You must override at least the *actionPerformed(java.awt.event.ActionEvent)* method and implement in it what this actions is going to do.

###### **Example #1****: A simple action**

```java

```

###### **Example #2****: An action for a browser**

```java

```

##### Step #2: Specify action properties

| Property | Function |
| --- | --- |
| ID | ID is a unique String for identifying an action. If the action ID is set to null, a new ID is generated. An action name can be used as ID. All MagicDraw (or another modeling tool developed by us) default actions IDs are defined in the com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsID class (for more details, see JavaDoc). You can use these constants for accessing actions. Do not use it as new actions ID. |
| Name | The action name is visible in all GUI elements. |
| Shortcut and mnemonic | Every action can have an assigned keyboard shortcut. Shortcuts can be customized from the Environment Options dialog box.(see MagicDraw User Manual, Section “Environment Options”.)The action must have an ID assigned (not null). In other case, shortcuts cannot be restored after restarting an application. |
| Icon | Every action can have a small and large icon. A small icon is described as javax.swing.Action.SMALL_ICON and can be used in menu items. A large icon is used in toolbar buttons. The action for a toolbar must have a large icon, otherwise it is displayed as a button with an action name. java |
| Description | The action’s description that is shown as a tool tip text. |

****

##### Step #3: Describe Enabling/Disabling logic

There are two ways for controlling the update of the actions state:

1. Add an action to the predefined actions group.

Actions can be added into one of predefined actions groups. All actions of the one group are disabled/enabled together. Conditions for groups enabling/disabling and status updating are predefined and cannot be changed.

Example:

```java

```

2. **Implement the*****updateState()*********method for the action**.

Here you may describe all conditions when an action must be enabled and disabled. Example of the *updateState()* method for the browser shortcut menu action:

```java

```

If the action is not added to any group, the *updateState()*****method for all such actions will be invoked after executing any command and after closing/opening a project or window.

When some actions need to refresh their state, all actions without a group can be updated manually:

```java

```

##### Step #4: Configure actions

Every action must be added into an *com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory* class. *ActionsCategory* is a small group for actions. It can be represented as a separator or submenu (a nested category).

Categories are added into the *com.nomagic.actions.ActionsManager* class, which is some kind of an actions container. One *ActionsManager* represents one GUI element – a menu bar, a shortcut menu, or a toolbar.

The following table explains how classes of a modeling tool maps into GUI elements:

|  | ActionsManager | Category | Action |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Menu | Menu bar | Menu | Menu item |
| Toolbar | All toolbars | One toolbar | Button |
| Shortcut Menu | Shortcut menu | Submenu | Menu item |

Actions in *ActionsManagers* are configured by many *Configurators*. A *Configurator* is responsible for adding or removing the action into a strictly defined place and position between other actions.

There are three types of configurators:

- com.nomagic.actions.AMConfigurator - a configurator for a general purpose. It is used for menus, toolbars, browser, and diagrams shortcuts.
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.BrowserContextAMConfigurator - a configurator for configuring managers for a browser shortcut (pop-up) menu. It can access a browser tree and nodes.
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.DiagramContextAMConfigurator - a configurator for configuring shortcut menus in a diagram. It can access a diagram, selected diagram elements, and an element that requests a shortcut menu.

Once *ActionsManagers* for the main menu and all toolbars are created and configured, the actions can be only disabled but not removed later.

Shortcut menus are created on every invoking, so *ActionsManagers* are created and configured every time and actions can be added or removed every time.

###### **Exampl****e #1: Add some action into a browser’s shortcut menu**

```java

```

###### **Exampl****e #2: add some action into main menu, after creating a new project**

```java

```

##### Step #5: Register Configurator

All configurators are registered in the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsConfiguratorsManager* class. *ActionsConfiguratorsManager* enables to add or remove many configurators to every configuration predefined by a modeling tool.

All available configurations are accessible in the following way:

```java
Configurator(configurator);]]>
```

###### **Example: Add new configurator for CONTAINMENT_BROWSER_CONTEXT configuration**

```java

```

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li>&lt;<em>modeling tool installation direc</em><em>tory&gt;\openapi\examples\actiontypes</em></li><li>&lt;<em>modeling tool installation direc</em><em>tory&gt;\openapi\examples\simpleconfigurators<br /></em></li><li>&lt;<em>modeling tool</em> <em>installation direc</em><em>tory&gt;\openapi\examples\selectionactions</em><em><br /></em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Step #1: Create a new action class</h3><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">All actions used in a modeling tool must be subclasses of the </span><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.MDAction</em> class (see </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">javadoc</span><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> for more details). The following </span><em>MDAction</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </span><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">subclasses that are used for basic purposes are already created in a modeling tool:</span></p><div class="WordSection1"><ul><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.actions.DefaultBrowserAction</em> – an action class, used for a browser action. It enables to access some browser tree and nodes and is recommended to use for performing some actions with the selected browser nodes.</li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.actions.DefaultDiagramAction</em> – an action class for a diagram action. It enables to access some diagram elements and is recommended to use for performing some actions with the selected diagram elements.</li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.PropertyAction</em> – an action for changing some element or application property. It can be used for changing properties defined by a user.</li></ul><p>You must override at least the <em>actionPerformed(java.awt.event.ActionEvent)</em> method and implement in it what this actions is going to do.</p><h4><strong>    Example #1</strong><strong>: A simple action</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e84c3c8f-3f54-499e-bfae-e88922872360"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    class SimpleAction extends MDAction
    { 
        public SimpleAction(String id, String name)
        {
             super(id, name, null, null); 
        }
        /**
        * Shows message.
        */
        public void actionPerformed(ActionEvent e)
        { 
            JOptionPane.showMessageDialog(MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().getDialogParent(), "This is:" + getName()); 
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p><br /></p><div class="WordSection2"><h4><strong>    Example #2</strong><strong>: An action for a browser</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ebd762e-1a8d-44d2-87a1-0f4cbe4aa4a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    public class BrowserAction extends DefaultBrowserAction
    {
        /**
        * Creates action with name "ExampleAction"
        */
        public BrowserAction()
        { 
          super("", "ExampleAction", null, null); 
        }
        public void actionPerformed(ActionEvent e)
        {
            Tree tree = getTree();
            String text="Selected elements:";
            for (int i = 0; i < tree.getSelectedNodes().length; i++)
            { 
                Node node = tree.getSelectedNodes()[i]; 
                Object userObject = node.getUserObject(); 
                if (userObject instanceof BaseElement)
                {
                    BaseElement element = (BaseElement) userObject;
                    text += "\n"+element.getHumanName(); 
                }
            }
            JOptionPane.showMessageDialog(MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().getDialogParent(), text);
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Step #2: Specify action properties</h3><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Property</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td><strong>ID</strong></td><td><p>ID is a unique String for identifying an action. If the action ID is set to <em>null, a </em>new ID is generated. An action name can be used as ID. All MagicDraw (or another modeling tool developed by us) default actions IDs are defined in the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsID</em> class (for more details, see <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">JavaDoc</span>). You can use these constants for accessing actions. Do not use it as new actions ID.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Name</strong></td><td>The action name is visible in all GUI elements.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Shortcut and mnemonic</strong></td><td><p>Every action can have an assigned keyboard shortcut. Shortcuts can be customized from the<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Environment Options </strong>dialog box.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-only" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d87b1ccd-736c-405f-9134-fe8e415c923b"><ac:rich-text-body>(see MagicDraw User Manual, Section “Environment Options”.)</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54fcf749-a24f-4355-9268-69fdc52cfa4e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The action must have an ID assigned (not null). In other case, shortcuts cannot be restored after restarting an application.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Icon</strong></td><td><p>Every action can have a small and large icon. A small icon is described as <em>javax.swing.Action.SMALL_ICON</em> and can be used in menu items. A large icon is used in toolbar buttons. The action for a toolbar must have a large icon, otherwise it is displayed as a button with an action name.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exampleAction_button_with_name.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating new actions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exampleAction_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating new actions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2a386e7-c748-41d6-8a28-2ec2ae67f5c1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[// setting an icon. On the toolbar, the button with an icon looks better than with the text.
action.setLargeIcon( new ImageIcon(getClass().getResource("main_toolbar_icon.gif") ) );]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Description</strong></td><td>The action’s description that is shown as a tool tip text.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">         </strong></p><div class="WordSection1"><h3>Step #3: Describe Enabling/Disabling logic</h3><p> There are two ways for controlling the update of the actions state:</p><ol><li><strong> Add an action to the predefined actions group.</strong></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Actions can be added into one of predefined actions groups. All actions of the one group are disabled/enabled together. Conditions for groups enabling/disabling and status updating are predefined and cannot be changed.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a2c5c9b7-cfc9-44a7-844d-a298d4bed16a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[MDAction action = new MDAction("Example","Example",KeyEvent.VK_E, ActionsGroups.PROJECT_OPENED_RELATED);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>    2.  <strong>Implement the </strong><em><strong>updateState()</strong></em><strong> </strong><strong>method for the action</strong>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Here you may describe all conditions when an action must be enabled and disabled. Example of the <em>updateState()</em> method for the browser shortcut menu action:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b09cb2a1-c539-47ae-bce8-d397356a100b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[        public void updateState()
        { 
           //action will be enabled only if there are some selected nodes. 
			Tree tree = getTree();
			if(tree != null)
			{
   				setEnabled(tree.getSelectedNode() != null);
			}
        }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>If the action is not added to any group, the <em>updateState()</em><strong> </strong>method for all such actions will be invoked after executing any command and after closing/opening a project or window.</p><p>When some actions need to refresh their state, all actions without a group can be updated manually:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="96206eaa-b210-4a79-8dbf-476532529d1a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ActionsStateUpdater.updateActionsState();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br style="margin-left: 30.0px;" /><h3>Step #4: Configure actions</h3><p>Every action must be added into an <em>com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory</em> class. <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">ActionsCategory</em> is a small group for actions. It can be represented as a separator or submenu (a nested category).</p></div><p>Categories are added into the <em>com.nomagic.actions.ActionsManager</em> class, which is some kind of an actions container. One <em>ActionsManager</em> represents one GUI element – a menu bar, a shortcut menu, or a toolbar. </p><div class="WordSection2">The following table explains how classes of a modeling tool maps into GUI elements:</div><div class="WordSection2"><p><br /></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th style="text-align: left;">ActionsManager</th><th style="text-align: left;">Category</th><th style="text-align: left;">Action</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Menu</strong></td><td><span>Menu bar</span></td><td><span>Menu</span></td><td><span>Menu item</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>T</strong><strong>oolbar</strong></td><td><span>All toolbars</span></td><td><span>One toolbar</span></td><td><span>Button</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Shortcut Menu</strong></td><td><span>Shortcut menu</span></td><td><span>Submenu</span></td><td><span>Menu item</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>Actions in <em>ActionsManagers</em> are configured by many <em>Configurators</em>. A <em>Configurator</em> is responsible for adding or removing the action into a strictly defined place and position between other actions.</p></div><p>There are three types of configurators:</p><ul><li><em>com.nomagic.actions.AMConfigurator</em> - a configurator for a general purpose. It is used for menus, toolbars, browser, and diagrams shortcuts.</li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.BrowserContextAMConfigurator</em> - a configurator for configuring managers for a browser shortcut (pop-up) menu. It can access a browser tree and nodes.</li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.DiagramContextAMConfigurator</em> - a configurator for configuring shortcut menus in a diagram. It can access a diagram, selected diagram elements, and an element that requests a shortcut menu.</li></ul><p>Once <em>ActionsManagers</em> for the main menu and all toolbars are created and configured, the actions can be only disabled but not removed later.</p><p>Shortcut menus are created on every invoking, so <em>ActionsManagers</em> are created and configured every time and actions can be added or removed every time.</p><p><br /></p><h4><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">    Exampl</strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">e #1: Add some action into a browser’s shortcut menu</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02415b63-5905-407e-aa6e-fb1a2128fdc8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    final DefaultBrowserAction browserAction = ...
    BrowserContextAMConfigurator brCfg = new BrowserContextAMConfigurator()
    {
        // Implement configuration.
        // Add or remove some actions in ActionsManager.
        // A tree is passed as an argument, provides ability to access nodes.
       public void configure(ActionsManager mngr, Tree browser)
       {
           	// Actions must be added into some category.
           	// So create the new one, or add an action into the existing category.
           	MDActionsCategory category = new MDActionsCategory("", "");
          	category.addAction(browserAction);
            // Add a category into the manager.
          	// A category isn't displayed in a shortcut menu.
          	mngr.addCategory(category);
       }
       public int getPriority()
       {
          return AMConfigurator.MEDIUM_PRIORITY;
       }
   };]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><div class="WordSection5"><h4><strong>    Exampl</strong><strong>e #2: add some action into main menu, after creating a new project</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f42d1b97-1134-4495-bb00-36125c230ed4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    // Create an action.
    final MDAction someAction = ...
    AMConfigurator conf = new AMConfigurator()
    {
        public void configure(ActionsManager mngr)
        {
           // Searching for an action after which an insertion should be done. 
           NMAction found= mngr.getActionFor(ActionsID.NEW_PROJECT);
 
           // The action found, inserting 
           if( found != null )
           {
              	// find the category of the "New Project" action. 
              	ActionsCategory category = (ActionsCategory)mngr.getActionParent(found);


             	// Get all actions from this category (menu). 
             	List actionsInCategory = category.getActions();


             	//Add the action after the "New Project" action.
             	int indexOfFound = actionsInCategory.indexOf(found);
             	actionsInCategory.add(indexOfFound+1, someAction);
 
             	// Set all actions. 
             	category.setActions(actionsInCategory);
       		}
    	}

    	public int getPriority()
    	{
        	return AMConfigurator.MEDIUM_PRIORITY;
    	}
   	};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Step #5: Register Configurator</h3><p>All configurators are registered in the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsConfiguratorsManager</em> class. <em>ActionsConfiguratorsManager</em> enables to add or remove many configurators to every configuration predefined by a modeling tool.</p><p>All available configurations are accessible in the following way:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91f767b2-ebe7-457b-a37d-dba86f541a99"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ActionsConfiguratorsManager.getInstance().add<configuration_name>Configurator(configurator);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><h4>    <strong>Example: Add new configurator for CONTAINMENT_BROWSER_CONTEXT configuration</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dffdfcae-5c92-4e62-bec1-7fb553cdd076"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    //See previous examples, how to create a configurator for browser actions.
    //Add the configurator into ActionsConfiguratorsManager.
    ActionsConfiguratorsManager.getInstance().addContainmentBrowserContextConfigurator(brCfg);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00058: (2026x Refresh1) Creating new model elements

- page_id: `303989509`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989509/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+new+model+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:39.603+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the *com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ElementsFactory*class for creating model elements. To create a model element, a session with the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.SessionManager* must be created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The *create<model element type>Instance(*) method creates a new model element instance. The figure above shows only a subset of all available *create<...>Instance()* methods.

All changes in the UML model will be registered and, on the session closing, will be added into the command history.

```java

```

****You can find the code examples in <*modeling tool installation direc**tory>\openapi\examples\accessors*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating a diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Use the <em>com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ElementsFactory </em>class for creating model elements. To create a model element, a session with the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.SessionManager</em> must be created.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ElementsFactory.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating new model elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">create&lt;model element type&gt;Instance(</em>) method creates a new model element instance. The figure above shows only a subset of all available <em>create&lt;...&gt;Instance()</em> methods.</p><p>All changes in the UML model will be registered and, on the session closing, will be added into the command history.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a82fadd-6c32-4d08-845e-a0c4c29e39ea"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project =...;
    ElementsFactory f = project.getElementsFactory(); 
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Create a package");
    Package packageA = f.createPackageInstance();
    //add created package into a root of the project
    packageA.setOwner(project.getPrimaryModel());
    ...
    // apply changes and add a command into the command history.
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in &lt;<em>modeling tool installation direc</em><em>tory&gt;\openapi\examples\accessors</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8340d8e4-37a3-49ae-b9bb-080d4acd05d6"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989513 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00059: (2026x Refresh1) Creating new relationship objects

- page_id: `303989513`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989513/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+new+relationship+objects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:39.708+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A model element is a relationship, if it implements one of the following interfaces:

- com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Relationship,
- com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.activities.mdbasicactivities.ActivityEdge ,
- com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.statemachines.mdbehaviorstatemachines.Transition ,
- com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.activities.mdextrastructuredactivities.ExceptionHandler ,
- com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.compositestructures.mdinternalstructures.Connector .

For checking if the model element is a relationship, call the *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.CoreHelper.isRelationship(Element[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#isRelationship-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)* method.

For getting supplier and client elements of a relationship, use *CoreHelper**class getSupplierElement(Element[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#getSupplierElement-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)*, *getClientElement(Element[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#getClientElement-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)*methods.

Use *CoreHelper**class**setSupplierElement(**Element, Element)* , *setClientElement(Element[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#setClientElement-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)Element[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#setClientElement-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)*methods to set supplier and client elements of a relationship.

To create a new Relationship

1. Create a new relationship model element.
2. Set client and supplier model elements by using *CoreHelper* class *setSupplierElement(**Element, Element)* , *setClientElement(Element[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#setClientElement-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)Element[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#setClientElement-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)* methods.
3. Add a new relationship into some parent by using the com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element.setOwner( Element) method.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Checking Model Element Permissions' space='']
