<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989210 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00060: (2026x Refresh1) Creating path elements

- page_id: `303989210`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989210/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+path+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:26.192+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Presentation elements (symbols) > (2026x Refresh1) Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.paths.PathElement* for given *ModelLink* between given client and supplier elements, use method *com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.createPathElement(**Element, PresentationElement, PresentationElement)*:

```java

```

The diagram is not passed into *createPathElement(**Element, PresentationElement, PresentationElement)*method. A new path element is created in the same diagram as client or supplier presentation elements.

****You can find the code examples in

- *<installation_directory>*\openapi\examples\symbolcreation
- **<installation_directory>*\openapi\examples\sequencecreation*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.paths.PathElement</em> for given <em>ModelLink</em> between given client and supplier elements, use method <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.createPathElement(</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Element, PresentationElement, PresentationElement)</em>:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18114fb6-b146-4506-ab53-7645667de387"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mddependencies.Dependency link = ...; 
    PresentationElement clientPE = ...;
    PresentationElement supplierPE = ...; 
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession("Test"); 
    PathElement path = PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().createPathElement(link, clientPE, supplierPE);
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The diagram is not passed into <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">createPathElement(</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Element, PresentationElement, PresentationElement) </em>method. A new path element is created in the same diagram as client or supplier presentation elements.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;</em>\openapi\examples\symbolcreation</em></li><li><em><em><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;</em>\openapi\examples\sequencecreation</em></em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9fab8a4f-e60b-4600-ac77-cdd7ef0ea882"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989450 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00061: (2026x Refresh1) Creating PopupMenu or other menu

- page_id: `303989450`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989450/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+PopupMenu+or+other+menu
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:38.085+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Using and extending UI components
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

API provides a way to create a popup menu from actions. Of course, you can use the standard java api for that, but we provide our own creators if you use our Actions.

Use*com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.MenuCreatorFactory.getMenuCreator()* to get an instance of a creator.

Use *com.nomagic.actions.ActionsMenuCreator.createPopupMenu(**ActionsManager)* to create a popup menu.

Steps to create a popup menu would be

1. Create an instance of com.nomagic.actions.ActionsManager.
2. Add com.nomagic.actions.NMAction actions and com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory categories into ActionsManager .
3. Call ActionsMenuCreator.createPopupMenu( ActionsManager) to create a popup for your actions.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating new actions' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>API provides a way to create a popup menu from actions. Of course, you can use the standard java api for that, but we provide our own creators if you use our Actions.</p><p>Use<em> com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.MenuCreatorFactory.getMenuCreator()</em> to get an instance of a creator.</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.actions.ActionsMenuCreator.createPopupMenu(</em><em>ActionsManager)</em> to create a popup menu.</p><p><br /></p><p>Steps to create a popup menu would be</p><ol><li>Create an instance of <em>com.nomagic.actions.ActionsManager.</em></li><li>Add<em> </em><em>com.nomagic.actions.NMAction</em> actions and<em> com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory</em> categories into <em>ActionsManager</em>.</li><li>Call <em>ActionsMenuCreator.createPopupMenu(</em><em>ActionsManager)</em>  to create a popup for your actions.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6bac02bc-b67f-4173-a430-6fd772fe6374"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating new actions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989578 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00062: (2026x Refresh1) Creating required files and folders structure

- page_id: `303989578`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989578/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+required+files+and+folders+structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:43.236+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Distributing Resources
- labels: ['distributed-resources', 'distributing-diagrams', 'distributing-profiles', 'distributing-templates', 'distributing-samples', 'distributing-documentation', 'distributing-plugins']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

960869435

960869448

960869437

To distribute resources, you must create the required files and folders for a particular resource type. Some of the resource file names should match the standard names.

For each resource file, there should be a created folders structure, which should match the folders structure of the program installation folder.

To distribute resources, you must create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Resource manager descriptor file' space=''].

##### Distributing**Custom** Diagrams

A required file for the custom diagram distribution is *descriptor.xml* - a Custom Diagram descriptor provided by MagicDraw Resource Manager. The file and folder structure is depicted in the following figure:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The custom diagram type (in diagram descriptor.xml file) should match the folder name it is placed in.

For example, the custom diagram with type *My Custom Structure Diagram* should be placed in a folder named **My Custom Structure Diagram**.

on about creating new diagram types,

UML Profiling and DSL UserGuide.pdf

##### Distributing Profiles

You can distribute your profile as the following structure:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

For more information about workingwithProfiles see *MagicDraw UserManual.pdf*and *UML Profiling and DSL UserGuide.pdf*.

##### Distributing Templates

Files for the template distribution are as follows:

- CustomTemplate.mdzip (required) - the template project file.
- CustomTemplate.html - the description of the template project.
- CustomTemplate.html - the description of the template group.

The file and folder structure is depicted in the following figure:**

*[IMAGE alt='' src='']*

****

##### Distributing Samples and Documentation

You can distribute your created samples and documentation and import them into your modeling tool with the MagicDraw ResourceManager. The file and folder structure is depicted in the following figure:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Distributing Plugins

Required files for a plugin distribution are as follows:

- plugins.xml - A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Plugin descriptor' space=''] .
- customPlugin.jar - jarred [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Plugin classes' space=''] . You may select any title for this file.

The file and folder structure is depicted in the following figure:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The plugin term may include all resources that could be distributed, such as custom diagrams, profiles, templates, samples, and others.

960869434

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Resource manager descriptor file' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Plugin descriptor' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Plugin classes' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

- page_id: `303989168`

- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:24.997+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Jython Scripting
- labels: ['plugin-descriptor', 'python', 'jython']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Let's describe creating a script in an example. We will create a script showing a message on a program startup. The creation process consists of three steps:

1. Creating a directory
2. Writing a script descriptor
3. Writing a script code

****

**Ste****p #1: Create Directory**

In the *plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.jpython*folder, create a *scripts*sub-folder, and then a folder for the particular script. For example, *plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.jpython/scripts/example*

**Ste****p #2: Write Script Descriptor**

A script descriptor is a file written in XML and named *script.xml*. The script descriptor provides information about a script file to run, version of script, ID, and other.

In the created directory, create a *script.xml*file:

```xml
]]>
```

The following table describes the *script.xml* file structure:

| Element | Description |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| script | Attributes |  |
| Name | Description |  |
| id | A scrip ID, should be unique. Used to identify a script. Example: “my.first.script.0” |  |
| name | A script name. No strict rules applied to this attribute. Example: “Example script” |  |
| version | A script version. Allows numbers separated with one dot value. Examples: “1.0”, “0.1” |  |
| provider-name | A script provider name. A company or an author name. Example: "No Magic" |  |
| script-file | A relative path to a script file. This file will be executed. Example:”main.py” |  |
| requires-api | A program API version required by a script. Example:”1.0” |  |

**Ste****p #3: Write Script Code**

Then in the same directory, create the *main.py*file:

```py

```

After saving files, restart your modeling tool. On a program startup, a message dialog should appear.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**V****ariable****s Passed to Script**

The program passes the one variable to the script *pluginDescriptor*. This variable contains information from the parsed *script.xml* file. A variable is an instance of a *com.nomagic.magicdraw.jpython.PythonPluginDescriptor* class.

A script can retrieve the script directory and other necessary information from the *pluginDescriptor* variable. There is no need to change any other fields for this variable.

The location of the created shape will be (0,0).

The following code example shows how to do this:

```

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303988960 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00065: (2026x Refresh1) Creating test cases

- page_id: `303988960`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303988960/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+test+cases
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:10.289+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Plugins
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tools, including MagicDraw, provide a JUnit ([www.junit.org)](http://www.junit.org/)-based test frameworks. The main purpose of the test framework is to simplify the automatic unit and integration tests development for the program and its plugins. The test framework can be used by developers for testing their own plugins or standard essential program features.

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

###### JUnit 3 test cases

The test framework consists of an abstract JUnit test case implementation (*com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawTestCase*) and a number of tools (*com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.common.TestEnvironment*, *com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.common.comparators.ProjectsComparator*) that can be used for the following purposes:

- Starting the application
- Managing projects
- Checking program for memory leaks

###### JUnit 4 test cases

The test framework also has test class runner implementations (*com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawTestRunner*, *com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.ParametrizedMagicDrawTestRunner*) that:

- Start the application
- Check program for memory leaks.

###### JUnit 5 test cases

Extend your test class with *com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.MagicDrawApplication* to start the application before running the test methods.

****You can find the examples of the code in <*modeling tool**installation direc**tory>\openapi\examples\testframework*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

## PAGE 00066: (2026x Refresh1) Creating textual element representation

- page_id: `303989093`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989093/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+textual+element+representation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:18.230+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.RepresentationTextCreator* to create a textual representation of Element. This utility class is used for creating a textual element representation in various UI - trees, list and etc.

```java

```

**Providing a custom representation text**

You can override default custom text creation for the specific element.

Use *RepresentationTextCreator*class method *addProvider(**RepresentationTextCreator.RepresentationTextProvider)* to register a custom representation text provider.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

This utility class is used for creating a textual element representation in various UI - trees, list and etc.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df30bbe4-8ce9-4f59-9609-9e4a7b764012"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[   Element element = ...
   //create a preformatted text for any kind of element
   String text =RepresentationTextCreator.getRepresentedText(element);
 
   //create a text with special options for com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Property
   Property property = ...;
   String propertyString = RepresentationTextCreator.createPropertyText(property, true, true, true, true, true, false, false, false, true, false);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><p><strong>Providing a custom representation text</strong></p><p>You can override default custom text creation for the specific element. </p><p>Use <em>RepresentationTextCreator </em>class method <em>addProvider(</em><em>RepresentationTextCreator.RepresentationTextProvider)</em> to register a custom representation text provider.</p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00067: (2026x Refresh1) Creating Use Case Scenario

- page_id: `303989384`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989384/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+Use+Case+Scenario
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:34.846+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Modeling tools provide an API for creating and manipulating a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case Scenario sketch' space='MT'] for a use case:

- The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.usecasescenarios.scenarios.Scenario* class represents a Use Case Scenario. It contains methods for manipulating it.
- The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.usecasescenarios.scenarios.ScenarioFactory* class is used for creating use case scenarios.
- The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.usecasescenarios.scenarios.ScenarioManager* class contains utility methods for creating and manipulating scenarios.
- The scenario management calls should be wrapped with the session management calls.

See an example of how to create the use case scenario with the basic, alternative, and exceptional flows. After the use case scenario is created, the Activity diagram is opened. In the Activity diagram, the use case scenario is represented as an action flow.

```java

```

More information is available in javadoc.

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\scenario

For more information about the use case scenario, see Use Case Scenario in MagicDraw UserManual.pdf.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>Modeling tools provide an API for creating and manipulating a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Use Case Scenario sketch" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Case Scenario]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for a use case:</p><ul><li><p>The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.usecasescenarios.scenarios.Scenario</em> class represents a Use Case Scenario. It contains methods for manipulating it.</p></li><li><p>The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.usecasescenarios.scenarios.ScenarioFactory</em> class is used for creating use case scenarios.</p></li><li><p>The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.usecasescenarios.scenarios.ScenarioManager</em> class contains utility methods for creating and manipulating scenarios.</p></li><li><p>The scenario management calls should be wrapped with the session management calls.</p></li></ul><p>See an example of how to create the use case scenario with the basic, alternative, and exceptional flows. After the use case scenario is created, the Activity diagram is opened. In the Activity diagram, the use case scenario is represented as an action flow.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="340c6200-35e2-4d2d-86c8-0214c646855c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(...);
	// Creates a use case scenario.
    Scenario scenario = ScenarioManager.createScenario(useCase);
    // Sets the scenario name.
    scenario.setName("Extract money from ATM.");
 
    // Adds a basic flow step.
    FlowStep flowStep1 = scenario.addFlowStep();
    // Sets a name for the basic flow step.
    flowStep1.setName("Insert card");
 
    FlowStep flowStep2 = scenario.addFlowStep();
    flowStep2.setName("Enter pin");
 
    FlowStep flowStep3 = scenario.addFlowStep();
    flowStep3.setName("Good bye");
 
    // Adds an alternative condition for the basic flow step.
    AlternativeCondition condition = scenario.addAlternativeCondition(flowStep2);
    // Sets a condition guard.
    condition.setIfCondition("Pin correct");
 
    // Sets a name for the alternative flow step.
    FlowStep flowStep = condition.getAlternativeFlowSteps().get(0);
    flowStep.setName("Extract money");
    // Adds an exception type to the basic flow step.
    ExceptionType exceptionType = scenario.addExceptionType(flowStep2);
 
    // Sets a name for the exception type. 
    exceptionType.setName("Card expired");
    // Sets a name for the exceptional flow step.
    FlowStep exceptionalFlowStep = exceptionType.getExceptionalFlowSteps().get(0);
    exceptionalFlowStep.setName("Inform customer about expired card");
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession();

    // Opens and layouts the Activity diagram. 
    ScenarioManager.displayScenario(scenario, true, true, "Open ATM Scenario");]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>More information is available in javadoc.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\scenario</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-only" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="237b7985-afd5-4c27-8a62-1d6b1bd3e60e"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information about the use case scenario, see Use Case Scenario in MagicDraw UserManual.pdf.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989267 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00068: (2026x Refresh1) Creating validation rules

- page_id: `303989267`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989267/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+validation+rules
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:28.083+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based
- labels: ['validation-suite', 'validation-rule', 'active-validation-suite', 'annotation']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Validation rules and validation suites specify what will be validating and how. They also specify how a problem that is found by a validation rule can be solved.

A**v****alidation rule**is a constraint with the applied stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule». Validation rules can validate model elements and non model elements (for example, presentation elements) as well. The UML metaclass specified as a constrained element defines the validation rule that validates elements of the specified metaclass. The stereotype specified as a constrained element specifies the validation rule that validates elements having the stereotype applied. A classifier specified as a constrained element specifies the validation rule that validates instances of the specified classifier. Validation rule’s implementation can be OCL2.0 based or binary. Binary validation rules can be implemented in Java. OCL2.0 based validation rules are described using the OCL2.0 language. The validation rule can be global or local one. Global validation rules are executed only once per validation session. Local validation rules are executed for each model element. The validation rule is treated as global if:

- it is the OCL2.0 based validation rule, the OCL2.0 specification is valid, and the OCL2.0 specification does not use the “self” variable (explicitly or implicitly by using the property of a constrained element only)
- it is binary based, has the specified implementation class name, and it has no specified constrained elements.

A**v****alidation suite**is a package with the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationSuite». The validation suite organizes several validation rules into a unit that can be used for performing the validation. Validation rules can be added or imported into the validation suite.

An**active validation suite**is a package with the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::activeValidationSuite». Active validation rules can be checked constantly or on the model elements change. OCL2.0 or binary validation rules can be used in the active validation. We suggest to prefer binary, because they give the better performance.

An**annotation**defines the validation result. It contains information about what is incorrect, severity of the problem, and possible actions that can be used to solve the problem.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Annotating the elements' space='']

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Validation rules and validation suites specify what will be validating and how. They also specify how a problem that is found by a validation rule can be solved.</p><p>A<strong> v</strong><strong>alidation rule </strong>is a constraint with the applied stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationRule». Validation rules can validate model elements and non model elements (for example, presentation elements) as well. The UML metaclass specified as a constrained element defines the validation rule that validates elements of the specified metaclass. The stereotype specified as a constrained element specifies the validation rule that validates elements having the stereotype applied. A classifier specified as a constrained element specifies the validation rule that validates instances of the specified classifier. Validation rule’s implementation can be OCL2.0 based or binary. Binary validation rules can be implemented in Java. OCL2.0 based validation rules are described using the OCL2.0 language. The validation rule can be global or local one. Global validation rules are executed only once per validation session. Local validation rules are executed for each model element. The validation rule is treated as global if:</p><ul><li>it is the OCL2.0 based validation rule, the OCL2.0 specification is valid, and the OCL2.0 specification does not use the “self” variable (explicitly or implicitly by using the property of a constrained element only)</li><li>it is binary based, has the specified implementation class name, and it has no specified constrained elements.</li></ul><p>A<strong> v</strong><strong>alidation suite </strong>is a package with the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::validationSuite». The validation suite organizes several validation rules into a unit that can be used for performing the validation. Validation rules can be added or imported into the validation suite.</p><p>An<strong> active validation suite </strong>is a package with the stereotype «UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile::activeValidationSuite». Active validation rules can be checked constantly or on the model elements change. OCL2.0 or binary validation rules can be used in the active validation. We suggest to prefer binary, because they give the better performance.</p><p>An<strong> annotation </strong>defines the validation result. It contains information about what is incorrect, severity of the problem, and possible actions that can be used to solve the problem.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58027d06-d3f1-4061-922c-52b0ca0eb8d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Annotating the elements" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="75652ee4-97db-427f-a361-a972a67a65cb"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989263 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00069: (2026x Refresh1) Custom diagram painters

- page_id: `303989263`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989263/2026x+Refresh1+Custom+diagram+painters
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:27.890+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams
- labels: ['diagram-surface']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Open API provides a way to add your own custom diagram painters for painting some additional stuff on the diagram canvas. A good sample would be some highlighting in the diagram.

The painter can be added only into the opened diagram's *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramSurface*. Only the opened diagram has *DiagramSurface*. A closed diagram returns *null*.

###### Example of the code:

```java

```

```text

```

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\customdiagrampainter*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>The Open API provides a way to add your own custom diagram painters for painting some additional stuff on the diagram canvas. A good sample would be some highlighting in the diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cc78d5d3-78d1-479e-a45e-3b1a6bab7f5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The painter can be added only into the opened diagram's <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramSurface</em>. Only the opened diagram has <em>DiagramSurface</em>. A closed diagram returns <em>null</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Example of the code:</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="223a2e8d-b1dd-4546-80f1-c344a7640e85"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Application.getInstance().addProjectEventListener(new ProjectEventListenerAdapter()
{
   public void projectOpened(Project project)
   {
      project.addPropertyChangeListener(new PropertyChangeListener()
      {
         public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
         {
            if(evt.getPropertyName().equals(Project.DIAGRAM_OPENED))
            {
               DiagramPresentationElement diagram = Application.getInstance().getProject().getActiveDiagram();
               diagram.getDiagramSurface().addPainter(new DiagramSurfacePainter()
               {
                  public void paint(Graphics g, DiagramPresentationElement diagram)
                  {
                     g.setColor(Color.BLUE);
                     List symbols = diagram.getPresentationElements();
                     for (int i = 0; i < symbols.size(); i++)
                     {
                        PresentationElement o = (PresentationElement)symbols.get(i);
                        if( o instanceof ShapeElement)
                        {
                           Rectangle bounds = o.getBounds();
                           bounds.grow(5,5);
                           ((Graphics2D)g).draw(bounds);
                        }

                     }

                  };

               });

            }

         }

      });

   }

});]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre>    </pre></div><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\customdiagrampainter</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989294 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00070: (2026x Refresh1) Custom elements numbering

- page_id: `303989294`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989294/2026x+Refresh1+Custom+elements+numbering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:29.410+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The generic numbering open API provides two interfaces of different complexity. They allow a user to modify how the numbering process executes.

- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) IJavaNumberPart interface' space=''] interface produces a single part of a number generated by the framework. It does not interfere with the execution of the framework, but simply provides a little piece of information included in the numbering.
- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) INumberingAction interface' space=''] allows for generating complete numbers for sets of elements. All sorting and numbering is delegated to the implementing class. The framework will only register the generated numbers internally.

Classes that implement these interfaces can be set as the expression value of an Expression NumberPart. (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Numbering Scheme property values' space='MT']).

These classes have to be specified with the complete package and class name, for example, *com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.sample.SquareNumberPart*.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

It does not interfere with the execution of the framework, but simply provides a little piece of information included in the numbering.</li><li>The <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) INumberingAction interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[INumberingAction]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em> allows for generating complete numbers for sets of elements. All sorting and numbering is delegated to the implementing class. The framework will only register the generated numbers internally.</li></ul><p>Classes that implement these interfaces can be set as the expression value of an Expression NumberPart. (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Numbering Scheme property values" ri:space-key="MT" /></ac:link>).<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></p><p>These classes have to be specified with the complete package and class name, for example, <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.sample.SquareNumberPart</em>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="918a5c6d-e796-417e-ae7a-a2c46c77d972"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="845f50c5-47fd-4d7e-b95e-17c259663b9e"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989378 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00071: (2026x Refresh1) Custom Legend Item Implementation

- page_id: `303989378`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989378/2026x+Refresh1+Custom+Legend+Item+Implementation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:34.347+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is possible to create Java implementation describing what model element or presentation element should a Legend Item adorn in a diagram.

1. The Java class should be implemented following the very same rules as when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Implementing a binary validation rule' space=''] . Each com.nomagic.magicdraw.annotation.Annotation object returned by the rule will cause the targeted model or presentation element to be adorned in the diagram (but only if the Legend is used in that diagram).
2. Specify the full class name as the value of "Implementation" property for the Legend Item in the modeling application.
3. Compile Java code, resulting in .class files possibly contained by a .jar file.
4. Ensure that your modeling tool can find and load these classes. You can place them in the program classpath or use the modeling tool plugins mechanism.

#### TIP: Example

Example

See *AdornComponentsOfInterestInDiagramValidator.java* example implementation in the *<program installation directory>\openapi\examples\legendItem*directory.

Also see: [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Each <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.annotation.Annotation</em> object returned by the rule will cause the targeted model or presentation element to be adorned in the diagram (but only if the Legend is used in that diagram).</li><li>Specify the full class name as the value of &quot;Implementation&quot; property for the Legend Item in the modeling application. </li><li>Compile Java code, resulting in .class files possibly contained by a .jar file.</li><li>Ensure that your modeling tool can find and load these classes. You can place them in the program classpath or use the modeling tool plugins mechanism.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6964f9b3-6d3c-4d75-bec5-da99cd79962f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>See <em>AdornComponentsOfInterestInDiagramValidator.java</em> example implementation in the <em>&lt;program installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\legendItem </em>directory.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />Also see: <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Legends" /></ac:link></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303988996 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00072: (2026x Refresh1) Custom presentation elements rendering

- page_id: `303988996`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303988996/2026x+Refresh1+Custom+presentation+elements+rendering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:11.828+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Presentation elements (symbols)
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The custom symbol rendering API allows change a default default symbol view. This API includes:

1. Renderer provider API, which allows to provide a custom renderer for the specific presentation element.
2. Renderers, which can modify the default presentation element appearance.

In this section, we will review, how to register the specific presentation element views provider and give the example covering the custom renderers for the package, slots, and dependency link symbols.

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\symbolrendering.*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

This API includes:</p><ol><li>Renderer provider API, which allows to provide a custom renderer for the specific presentation element.</li><li>Renderers, which can modify the default presentation element appearance.</li></ol><p>In this section, we will review, how to register the specific presentation element views provider and give the example covering the custom renderers for the package, slots, and dependency link symbols.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\symbolrendering.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5400cec7-8f81-40d0-809c-38f6bac028a7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="05088e21-c8f3-4865-8c3f-35850941b106"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303988925 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00073: (2026x Refresh1) Custom profile implementation

- page_id: `303988925`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303988925/2026x+Refresh1+Custom+profile+implementation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:09.003+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with stereotypes and tagged values
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Instead of using low level *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.StereotypesHelper* API to access information about profile elements, it is possible to generate a profile implementation class out of the UML Profile model. This implementation class wraps all mapping, naming details with a nice Java API. MagicDraw comes with already generated profiles implementation for standard profiles. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Standard profiles implementation' space=''] page for details.

To create your own custom profile implementation you need to follow these steps

1. Install Development Tools plugin from Help->Resource Manager
2. Open UML Profile model with MagicDraw.
3. Generate profile implementation code using Tools->Development Tools->Generate Profile Class Implementation (wrappers) ...

Profile implementation class has these features

- There is a constant defined for every DataType, Enumeration name
- There is a getter for every DataType element defined in the profile
- Java enumeration is generated for every Enumeration element defined in the profile. This class has following features
  - There are methods to get enumeration literals by generated enumeration and vice versa
- Inner java class is generated for every Stereotype defined in the profile. This class has following features
  - There is a constant defined for Stereotype name
  - There are constants defined for all stereotype tags names
  - There is a setter/getter method available for every stereotype tag. Signature of these methods corresponds to tag multiplicity type and etc. No more need to work with strings and casting values to primitives as in StereotypesHelper case.
  - There is a check method to check if Element has specific stereotype applied
  - There is a getter for Stereotype element itself

It is highly recommended to assign URI to Profile element in the UML model before generation. URI is an unique Profile identifier and helps to avoid conflicts if model has several profiles with same name applied.

**Custom sample profile**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Usages example**

```java

```

```text
Generated implementation class for this custom profile
```

```java
value)
      {
         Profiles.setValue(element, getStereotype(), getFriendsProperty(), value);
      }

      public void clearFriends(Element element)
      {
         Profiles.clearValue(element, getFriendsProperty());
      }

      public void addFriends(Element element, String value)
      {
         Profiles.addValue(element, getStereotype(), getFriendsProperty(), value);
      }

      public void removeFriends(Element element, String value)
      {
         java.util.List values = getFriends(element);
         values.remove(value);
         setFriends(element, values);
      }

      @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
      public java.util.List getFriends(Element element)
      {
         return (java.util.List) Profiles.getValue(element, getFriendsProperty());
      }

      @Override
      protected void clear()
      {
         super.clear();
         country = null;
         friends = null;
      }

      @Override
      public boolean is(@CheckForNull Element element)
      {
         return element instanceof com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdusecases.Actor &&
               _p.isTypeOf(element, getStereotype());
      }

      public static boolean isInstance(@CheckForNull Element element)
      {
         if (element instanceof com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdusecases.Actor)
         {
            CustomProfile instance = getInstance(element);
            return instance.isTypeOf(element, instance.person().getStereotype());
         }
         return false;
      }
   }

   @Override
   protected Collection generatedGetAllElementWrappers()
   {
      Collection wrappers = new ArrayList<>();
      wrappers.add(personStereotype);
      return wrappers;
   }

   @Override
   protected Collection generatedGetAllStereotypes()
   {
      if (getProfile() != null)
      {
         final Collection stereotypes = new HashSet<>();
         stereotypes.add(personStereotype.getStereotype());
         return stereotypes;
      }
      return Collections.emptyList();
   }
}]]>
```

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Standard profiles implementation' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Instead of using low level <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.StereotypesHelper</em> API to access information about profile elements, it is possible to generate a profile implementation class out of the UML Profile model. This implementation class wraps all mapping, naming details with a nice Java API. MagicDraw comes with already generated profiles implementation for standard profiles. See <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Standard profiles implementation" /></ac:link> page for details.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create your own custom profile implementation you need to follow these steps</p><ol><li>Install Development Tools plugin from Help-&gt;Resource Manager</li><li>Open UML Profile model with MagicDraw. </li><li>Generate profile implementation code using Tools-&gt;Development Tools-&gt;Generate Profile Class Implementation (wrappers) ...</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Profile implementation class has these features</p><ul><li>There is a constant defined for every DataType, Enumeration name</li><li>There is a getter for every DataType element defined in the profile</li><li>Java enumeration is generated for every Enumeration element defined in the profile. This class has following features<ul><li>There are methods to get enumeration literals by generated enumeration and vice versa</li></ul></li><li>Inner java class is generated for every Stereotype defined in the profile. This class has following features<br /><ul><li>There is a constant defined for Stereotype name</li><li>There are constants defined for all stereotype tags names</li><li>There is a setter/getter method available for every stereotype tag. Signature of these methods corresponds to tag multiplicity type and etc. No more need to work with strings and casting values to primitives as in <em>StereotypesHelper</em> case.</li><li>There is a check method to check if Element has specific stereotype applied</li><li>There is a getter for Stereotype element itself</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5dce80e5-9c6e-41b1-b514-60eaa771f2dc"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is highly recommended to assign URI to Profile element in the UML model before generation. URI is an unique Profile identifier and helps to avoid conflicts if model has several profiles with same name applied.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong>Custom sample profile</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2015-7-1 11:26:9.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Custom profile implementation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Usages example</strong></p><div><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76f1e3c4-f8cd-4155-8c47-5be8016f1e78"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Element element = ...;
CustomProfile customProfile = CustomProfile.getInstance(element);
//check if element has Person stereotype applied
customProfile.person().is(element);
//get Person.country tag value
String country = customProfile.person().getCountry(element);
//set Person.country tag value
customProfile.person().setCountry(element, "US");]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><pre><strong>Generated implementation class for this custom profile</strong></pre><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81a5c4b5-6d3a-4665-84b9-2944d60941d9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml2.Profiles;
import com.nomagic.profiles.ProfileCache;
import com.nomagic.profiles.ProfileImplementation;
import com.nomagic.profiles.ProfilesBridge;
import com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element;
import com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Property;
import com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles.Stereotype;

import javax.annotation.CheckForNull;
import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.Collection;
import java.util.Collections;
import java.util.HashSet;

@SuppressWarnings("WeakerAccess, unused")
public class CustomProfile extends ProfileImplementation
{
   public static final String PROFILE_URI = "http://custom.com/custom_profile";

   public static final String PROFILE_NAME = "CustomProfile";

   private final PersonStereotype personStereotype;

   public static CustomProfile getInstance(BaseElement baseElement)
   {
      CustomProfile profile = ProfilesBridge.getProfile(CustomProfile.class, baseElement);
      if (profile == null)
      {
         return ProfilesBridge.createProfile(CustomProfile.class, baseElement, CustomProfile::new, PROFILE_NAME, PROFILE_URI);
      }
      return profile;
   }

   public CustomProfile(ProfileCache cache)
   {
      super(cache);
      personStereotype = new PersonStereotype(this);

   }

   public PersonStereotype person()
   {
      return personStereotype;
   }

   public static class PersonStereotype extends StereotypeWrapper
   {

      //stereotype Person and its tags
      public static final String STEREOTYPE_NAME = "Person";
      public static final String COUNTRY = "country";
      public static final String FRIENDS = "friends";

      private final CustomProfile _p;
      @CheckForNull
      private Property country;
      @CheckForNull
      private Property friends;

      protected PersonStereotype(CustomProfile profile)
      {
         super(profile);
         _p = profile;
      }

      @Override
      @SuppressWarnings("ConstantConditions")
      public Stereotype getStereotype()
      {
         return getElementByName(STEREOTYPE_NAME);
      }

      @CheckForNull
      public Property getCountryProperty()
      {
         if (country == null)
         {
            country = getTagByName(getStereotype(), COUNTRY);
         }
         return country;
      }

      @CheckForNull
      public Property getFriendsProperty()
      {
         if (friends == null)
         {
            friends = getTagByName(getStereotype(), FRIENDS);
         }
         return friends;
      }

      public void setCountry(Element element, @CheckForNull String value)
      {
         Profiles.setValue(element, getStereotype(), getCountryProperty(), value);
      }

      public void clearCountry(Element element)
      {
         Profiles.clearValue(element, getCountryProperty());
      }

      @CheckForNull
      public String getCountry(Element element)
      {
         return toString(Profiles.getFirstValue(element, getCountryProperty()));
      }

      public void setFriends(Element element, @CheckForNull java.util.Collection<String> value)
      {
         Profiles.setValue(element, getStereotype(), getFriendsProperty(), value);
      }

      public void clearFriends(Element element)
      {
         Profiles.clearValue(element, getFriendsProperty());
      }

      public void addFriends(Element element, String value)
      {
         Profiles.addValue(element, getStereotype(), getFriendsProperty(), value);
      }

      public void removeFriends(Element element, String value)
      {
         java.util.List<String> values = getFriends(element);
         values.remove(value);
         setFriends(element, values);
      }

      @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
      public java.util.List<String> getFriends(Element element)
      {
         return (java.util.List<String>) Profiles.getValue(element, getFriendsProperty());
      }

      @Override
      protected void clear()
      {
         super.clear();
         country = null;
         friends = null;
      }

      @Override
      public boolean is(@CheckForNull Element element)
      {
         return element instanceof com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdusecases.Actor &&
               _p.isTypeOf(element, getStereotype());
      }

      public static boolean isInstance(@CheckForNull Element element)
      {
         if (element instanceof com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdusecases.Actor)
         {
            CustomProfile instance = getInstance(element);
            return instance.isTypeOf(element, instance.person().getStereotype());
         }
         return false;
      }
   }

   @Override
   protected Collection<ProfileElementWrapper> generatedGetAllElementWrappers()
   {
      Collection<ProfileElementWrapper> wrappers = new ArrayList<>();
      wrappers.add(personStereotype);
      return wrappers;
   }

   @Override
   protected Collection<Stereotype> generatedGetAllStereotypes()
   {
      if (getProfile() != null)
      {
         final Collection<Stereotype> stereotypes = new HashSet<>();
         stereotypes.add(personStereotype.getStereotype());
         return stereotypes;
      }
      return Collections.emptyList();
   }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c183a4e-7197-4e37-ba4d-c9762ce8158c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Standard profiles implementation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989188 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00074: (2026x Refresh1) Custom renderer provider

- page_id: `303989188`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989188/2026x+Refresh1+Custom+renderer+provider
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:25.664+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Presentation elements (symbols) > (2026x Refresh1) Custom presentation elements rendering
- labels: ['presentation-element-render-provider', 'presentation-element-renderer-manager']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElementRendererProvider* is an object, which provides a custom symbol renderer for a given presentation element.

*PresentationElementRendererProvider.getRenderer(**PresentationElement)* method provides a specific renderer for the given *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement* (Symbol) or null.

To make your provider start working, you must register it into the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElementRendererManager*using:

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElementRendererProvider</em> is an object, which provides a custom symbol renderer for a given presentation element. </p><p><em>PresentationElementRendererProvider.getRenderer(</em><em>PresentationElement)</em> method provides a specific renderer for the given <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement</em> (Symbol) or null.</p><p>To make your provider start working, you must register it into the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElementRendererManager </em>using:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e41b4cdd-300e-44b8-83fc-143bc79ebb89"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[PresentationElementRendererManager.getInstance().addProvider(new RendererProvider());]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989191 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00075: (2026x Refresh1) Custom renderers sample

- page_id: `303989191`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989191/2026x+Refresh1+Custom+renderers+sample
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:25.723+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Presentation elements (symbols) > (2026x Refresh1) Custom presentation elements rendering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This example covers:

- The package without inner elements is filled with a green color.
- The slot values are rounded.
- The dependency link is a blue thicker line with custom line ends.

```text

```

This example shows, how to create custom renderers for the preceding pointed symbols.

```text

```

**The custom package rendererThe custom package renderer.**The empty package (that is without inner elements) is filled with green color:

```java

```

**The custom slot rendererThe custom slot renderer (used in the instance specification symbol).**The slot values are rounded:

```java
values = slot.getValue();
                        for (ValueSpecification value : values)
                        {
                            if (value instanceof LiteralString)
                            {
                                LiteralString literalString = (LiteralString) value;
                                if (string.length() > 0)
                                {
                                   string += "; ";

                                }
                                String literalValue = literalString.getValue();
                                try
                                {
                                    // round a value
                                    double doubleValue = Double.parseDouble(literalValue); 
                                    double rounded = Math.round(doubleValue * 100) / 100; 
                                    literalValue = Double.toString(rounded);
                                }
                                catch (NumberFormatException e)
                                {
                                }
                                string += literalValue;
                            }
                        }
                        return slot.getDefiningFeature().getName() + "=" + string;
                    }
                }
            }
            return super.getText(presentationElement, textEnum);
        }
    }]]>
```

**The custom dependency link rendererThe custom dependency link renderer.**The dependency link is a blue thicker line with custom line ends:

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>This example covers:</p><ul><li style="text-align: left;"><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><ac:link ac:anchor="The custom package renderer"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[A custom renderer for the package.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>The package without inner elements is filled with a green color.</li><li style="text-align: left;"><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><ac:link ac:anchor="The custom slot renderer"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[A custom renderer for the slot.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>The slot values are rounded.</li><li style="text-align: left;"><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><ac:link ac:anchor="The custom dependency link renderer"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[A custom renderer for the dependency link.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>The dependency link is a blue thicker line with custom line ends.</li></ul><div class="WordSection8"><pre> </pre><p>This example shows, how to create custom renderers for the preceding pointed symbols.</p><pre><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></pre><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="397ce5c4-bcbc-4d41-95af-cef715ca7ce5"><ac:parameter ac:name="">The custom package renderer</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>The custom package renderer. </strong>The empty package (that is without inner elements) is filled with green color:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be4a4052-0b22-4a8d-82d9-666a84f7689c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    class PackageRenderer extends ShapeRenderer
    {
        public Color getColor(PresentationElement presentationElement, PresentationElementColorEnum colorEnum)
        {
            if (PresentationElementColorEnum.FILL.equals(colorEnum))
            {
                // the color to fill
                Element element = presentationElement.getElement();
                if (element instanceof com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Package && 
                    !((com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Package) element).hasOwnedElement())
                {
                    // the package has no elements, use the green color to fill 
                    return Color.GREEN;
                }
            }
            return super.getColor(presentationElement, colorEnum);
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ad861a70-cfba-48c7-8f42-1e2ef552c0df"><ac:parameter ac:name="">The custom slot renderer</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>The custom slot renderer (used in the instance specification symbol). </strong>The slot values are rounded:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b629cb6d-b54a-4f8e-b68a-2e1114e57727"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    class SlotRenderer extends ShapeRenderer
    {
        public String getText(PresentationElement presentationElement, PresentationElementTextEnum textEnum)
        {
            if (PresentationElementTextEnum.NAME.equals(textEnum))
            {
                // the slot text is shown as a name
                Element element = presentationElement.getElement();
                if (element instanceof Slot)
                {
                    Slot slot = (Slot) element;
                    if (slot.hasValue())
                    {
                        String string = "";
                        List<ValueSpecification> values = slot.getValue();
                        for (ValueSpecification value : values)
                        {
                            if (value instanceof LiteralString)
                            {
                                LiteralString literalString = (LiteralString) value;
                                if (string.length() > 0)
                                {
                                   string += "; ";

                                }
                                String literalValue = literalString.getValue();
                                try
                                {
                                    // round a value
                                    double doubleValue = Double.parseDouble(literalValue); 
                                    double rounded = Math.round(doubleValue * 100) / 100; 
                                    literalValue = Double.toString(rounded);
                                }
                                catch (NumberFormatException e)
                                {
                                }
                                string += literalValue;
                            }
                        }
                        return slot.getDefiningFeature().getName() + "=" + string;
                    }
                }
            }
            return super.getText(presentationElement, textEnum);
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09575b30-840e-4689-aeec-e5d212c2e471"><ac:parameter ac:name="">The custom dependency link renderer</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>The custom dependency link renderer. </strong>The dependency link is a blue thicker line with custom line ends:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="817c14d2-f29c-4c12-a37b-0098e2d0b912"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    class DependencyRenderer extends PathRenderer
    {
        private PathEndRenderer mClientEndRenderer; 
        DependencyRenderer()
        {
            // a custom client end renderer, use a filled circle at the end
            mClientEndRenderer = new PathEndRenderer(PathEndAdornment.CIRCLE, PathEndAdornmentModifier.FILLED);
        }
 
        public Color getColor(PresentationElement presentationElement, PresentationElementColorEnum colorEnum)
        {
            if (PresentationElementColorEnum.LINE.equals(colorEnum))
            {
                // use a blue color for a line 
                return Color.BLUE;
            }
            return super.getColor(presentationElement, colorEnum);
        }


        protected PathEndRenderer getClientEndRenderer(PathElement pathElement)
        {
            // use a custom end renderer 
            return mClientEndRenderer;
        }
 
        public int getLineWidth(PresentationElement presentationElement)
        {
            // a line width is 2 
            return 2;
        }
 
        protected void drawPathAdornment(Graphics g, PathElement pathElement)
        {
            super.drawPathAdornment(g, pathElement);
 
            // draw a circle at the middle of the dependency line
            Color background = Color.WHITE;
            Property property = pathElement.getDiagramPresentationElement().getProperty(PropertyID.DIAGRAM_BACKGROUND_COLOR);
            if (property != null)
            {
                Object value = property.getValue();
                if (value instanceof Color)
                {
                    background = (Color) value;
                }
            }
            Point middlePoint = pathElement.getMiddlePoint();
            int diameter = 10;
            int radius = diameter / 2;
            int x = middlePoint.x - radius;
            int y = middlePoint.y - radius;
            Color color = g.getColor(); 
            g.setColor(background);
            g.fillOval(x, y, diameter, diameter);
            g.setColor(color);
            g.drawOval(x, y, diameter, diameter);
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303988986 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00077: (2026x Refresh1) Customizing a dependency matrix

- page_id: `303988986`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303988986/2026x+Refresh1+Customizing+a+dependency+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:11.462+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Working with Dependency Matrix
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The topic explains how to configure the following components of the dependency matrix:

- Command bar
- Shortcut keys
- Shortcut menus of the row or column header element, cell, and filter panel
- Cell entries (dependencies)
- Appearance of cells and headers of rows and columns

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

*Components of dependency matrix pane*

The names of the components differ in other documentation as follows:

- “Toolbar” is used for “Command bar”
- “ Criteria area” is used for “Filter panel”
- “Row elements” is used for “Row header elements”

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>The topic explains how to configure the following components of the dependency matrix:</p><ul><li>Command bar</li><li>Shortcut keys</li><li>Shortcut menus of the row or column header element, cell, and filter panel</li><li>Cell entries (dependencies)</li><li>Appearance of cells and headers of rows and columns</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Dep_matrix_components.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Customizing a dependency matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><em>Components of dependency matrix pane</em></p><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="549ce9c7-9c94-48f3-9a30-65c1f6df960c"><ac:rich-text-body><p align="center" style="text-align: left;">The names of the components differ in other documentation as follows:</p><ul><li>“Toolbar” is used for “Command bar”</li><li>“<strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Criteria </strong>area” is used for “Filter panel”</li><li>“Row elements” is used for “Row header elements”</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9ab2505-7714-456b-bfb3-1934ea08df7c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="a8a2e96a-7687-479a-b58c-75dad9215cdf"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989386 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00078: (2026x Refresh1) Customizing double-click behavior

- page_id: `303989386`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989386/2026x+Refresh1+Customizing+double-click+behavior
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:34.971+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Managing navigation in model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The default application behavior on a double-click on the element is to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] or open a diagram. This behavior can be changed by registering custom element activators.

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.ElementActivationManager.addActivator(ElementActivator[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/ElementActivationManager.html#addActivator-com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.ElementActivator-)* for that purpose.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The default application behavior on a double-click on the element is to open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> or open a diagram. This behavior can be changed by registering custom element activators.</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.ElementActivationManager.addActivator(ElementActivator<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/ElementActivationManager.html#addActivator-com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.ElementActivator-">)</a></em> for that purpose.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989352 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00079: (2026x Refresh1) Customizing templates

- page_id: `303989352`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989352/2026x+Refresh1+Customizing+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:32.483+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Oracle DDL generation and customization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To change the generation of the particular Oracle Object, add a new functionality to its macro in the velocity template. The Helper utility class will assist in retrieving model information.

We suggest to make a back up of the default template. The default template is stored in <*program installation folder>\ data\DB_engineering\Oracle_template*folder. The template file can be changed in the **CG Properties Editor**dialog box (in the Oracle DDL set shortcut menu, choose the **Properties**command).

When generating DDL, objects are passed from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Code engineering set' space=''] to the velocity engine. Objects, that are passed to the Oracle DDL velocity engine are presented in the following table:

| Object name | Object type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| $CESList | List<NamedEleme nt> | The list of model elements that are added to the . |
| $dropRequired | boolean | Flag to notify if the drop objects is required. This is a drop setting option which generates the Drop statement. |
| $oracleHelper | Java.lang.Class | This is a helper class for retrieving information from model elements. Use this class to check the element for applied stereotypes, get tagged values and element’s owned information. |
| $newLineBracket | boolean | The generation Option to generate a bracket in a new line. |
| $genDocumentation | boolean | The language option for the documentation generation. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To change the generation of the particular Oracle Object, add a new functionality to its macro in the velocity template. The Helper utility class will assist in retrieving model information. </p><p>We suggest to make a back up of the default template. The default template is stored in &lt;<em>program installation folder&gt;\ data\DB_engineering\Oracle_template </em>folder. The template file can be changed in the <strong>CG Properties Editor </strong>dialog box (in the Oracle DDL set shortcut menu, choose the <strong>Properties </strong>command).</p><p>When generating DDL, objects are passed from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Code engineering set" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[code engineering set]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the velocity engine. Objects, that are passed to the Oracle DDL velocity engine are presented in the following table:</p><table><tbody><tr><th><strong>Object name</strong></th><th><strong>Object type</strong></th><th><strong>Description</strong></th></tr><tr><td><pre>$CESList</pre></td><td><pre>List&lt;NamedEleme nt&gt;</pre></td><td><span>The list of model elements that are added to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Code engineering set" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[code engineering set]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></td></tr><tr><td><pre>$dropRequired</pre></td><td><pre>boolean</pre></td><td><span>Flag to notify if the drop objects is required. This is a drop setting option which generates the Drop statement.</span></td></tr><tr><td><pre>$oracleHelper</pre></td><td><pre>Java.lang.Class</pre></td><td><span>This is a helper class for retrieving information from model elements. Use this class to check the element for applied stereotypes, get tagged values and element’s owned information.</span></td></tr><tr><td><pre>$newLineBracket</pre></td><td><pre>boolean</pre></td><td><span>The generation Option to generate a bracket in a new line.</span></td></tr><tr><td><pre>$genDocumentation</pre></td><td><pre>boolean</pre></td><td><span>The language option for the documentation generation.</span></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989307 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00080: (2026x Refresh1) Deleting presentation elements

- page_id: `303989307`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989307/2026x+Refresh1+Deleting+presentation+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:29.952+02:00`
