### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To remove the given *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement* from the diagram, use the method *com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.deletePresentationElement(**PresentationElement)*:

```java

```

All related presentation elements with all children and connected paths will be removed too.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To remove the given <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement</em> from the diagram, use the method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.deletePresentationElement(</em><em>PresentationElement)</em>:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fac020d6-9cfa-4b60-8e68-3b1eea64df87"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    PresentationElement element = ...; 
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession("Test"); 
    PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().removePresentationElement(element); 
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>All related presentation elements with all children and connected paths will be removed too.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21356239-0c67-43fd-9dd9-2ffb96badd06"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303988966 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00083: (2026x Refresh1) Developing plugins using IDE

- page_id: `303988966`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303988966/2026x+Refresh1+Developing+plugins+using+IDE
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:10.514+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Plugins
- labels: ['eclpise', 'intellij']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Plugins extending your modeling tool can be effectively developed using popular IDEs (Integrated Development Environment) such as Eclipse, IntelliJ, and others. Developers may use theirs favorite IDE for an agile modeling tool plugin coding, building, testing, and debugging. This chapter describes the plugin development in Eclipse ([www.eclipse.org](http://www.eclipse.org/)) and IntelliJ IDEA ([www.jetbrains.com](http://www.jetbrains.com/)) IDEs.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Plugins extending your modeling tool can be effectively developed using popular IDEs (Integrated Development Environment) such as Eclipse, IntelliJ, and others. Developers may use theirs favorite IDE for an agile modeling tool plugin coding, building, testing, and debugging. This chapter describes the plugin development in Eclipse (<a href="http://www.eclipse.org/">www.eclipse.org</a>) and IntelliJ IDEA (<a href="http://www.jetbrains.com/">www.jetbrains.com</a>) IDEs.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91d26bab-dc7b-4c36-9466-26315846dc64"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="92ac631c-7363-4f6c-baa3-80257d3a1295"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303988968 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00084: (2026x Refresh1) Development in Eclipse

- page_id: `303988968`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303988968/2026x+Refresh1+Development+in+Eclipse
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:10.635+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Plugins > (2026x Refresh1) Developing plugins using IDE
- labels: ['setup-eclipse-environment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The pre-configured Eclipse projects for two sample plugins and a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Running programs in batch mode' space=''] (command-line) tool are provided with a program installation. These projects can be found in *<modeling tool installation directory>/openapi/ide/eclipse.zip*.

Let's use MagicDraw as an example to describe the following procedures.

To setup the Eclipse environment for the modeling tool development

1. Import java projects from <*modeling tool installation directory>/openapi/ide/eclipse.zip*into your Eclipse Workspace: 
1.1. In the main menu, click **File** > **Import**. The **Import** dialog opens. 
1.2. In the **Select an import wizard** list, select**General**> **Existing Projects into Workspace**and click**Next**. 
1.3. Copy the java projects zip location to the **Select archive file** box or click **Browse** and browse to the zip file. The project list appears in the **Projects** box. 
1.4. Select projects you need and click **Finish.** [ATTACHMENT filename='import_projects.png']
2. Open the **Package Explorer** view, expand the MagicDraw project and edit the MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY link: 
2.1. Right-click the link and select**Properties**. 
2.2. On the left side of the opened dialog, select **Resource**and click the **Edit**button on the right side of the dialog. The **Edit Link Location**dialog opens. 
2.3. Select the location and click **OK**. Be sure the link points to your MagicDraw (or other according to a modeling tool you are using) installation directory (see the following figure). [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Make sure that precisely Java 17 version is used by the **MagicDraw** project in your Eclipse IDE: 
3.1. Right-click the **MagicDraw** project and select**Properties**. 
3.2. On the left side of the open dialog, select **Java Build Path** and then select **Libraries** tab on the right. 
3.3. Double-click **JRE System Library [JavaSE-17]**. 
3.4. Click **Installed JREs** and check if Java 17 is available here. If there is no Java 17, add it. *Java 17 version must be added, because later versions may not be supported by our tool.* 3.5. Click **Apply and Close**. 3.6. Select **Execution environment** check box and in the combo box, select **JavaSE-17**. 3.7. C lick **Environments** button. 
3.8. In the **Execution Environments** list, select **JavaSE-17** and in the **Compatible JREs** list, select Java 17. 
3.9.Click **Apply and Close** and then **Finish** buttons. 
3.10.Select **Order and Export** tab, click **Select All** button to select all check boxes. 
3.11. Click**Apply and Close**.

Eclipse Workspace is ready for the source code development and running/debugging.

To use one of the prepared launch configurations

- On the main menu, click Run > Run Configurations... (or Debug Configurations... ). The Run Configurations dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The launch configuration is designed to load plugins from the MagicDraw (or other according to a modeling tool you are using ) installation directory () and two plugins from the Eclipse Workspace. Thus, if the *md.plugins.dir* java system property is not defined (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Plugins directories' space='']), developing plugins are not loaded.

- MagicDraw with all plugins launches MagicDraw (or other modeling tool) with plugins available in the IDE workspace namely My Plugin 1 and My Plugin 2 .
- My Commandline launches batch mode tool My Commandline .
- MyPlugin1JUnit<n>Test launches sample JUnit<n> test.

The libraries (jar files) of the plugin must be added to the development class path throughout the plugin dependency hierarchy if the developing code depends on that plugin. 
For example, if the code depends on plugin A; plugin A depends on plugins B and C; plugin B depends on plugin D, the libraries of all plugins (A, B, C, and D) must be added to the class path. 
For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Identifying plugin dependencies' space=''] page.

When you launch your own plugin, you need to add all jar filesthat are required by your pluginfrom appropriate plugins. The MagicDraw jar files can be found in *MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY/lib* and its sub directories, whereas plugins' jar files can be found in *MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY/plugins* and its sub directories.

Even if the plugin descriptor file contains information about the runtime plugin *.jar* file, it is not necessary to build and deploy this *.jar* file to a plugin directory while the plugin is developed under Eclipse.

````html

The <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Run Configurations</strong> dialog opens.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="run_configurations.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Development in Eclipse" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e85c7fe6-7a02-4c0e-be8f-abb4e2c1051a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The launch configuration is designed to load plugins from the MagicDraw <span>(</span><span>or other according to a modeling tool you are using</span> <span>)</span> installation directory (<ac:link ac:anchor="step2"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[see step #2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) and two plugins from the Eclipse Workspace. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303988998 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00085: (2026x Refresh1) Development in IntelliJ IDEA

- page_id: `303988998`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303988998/2026x+Refresh1+Development+in+IntelliJ+IDEA
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:11.946+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Plugins > (2026x Refresh1) Developing plugins using IDE
- labels: ['setup-intellij-idea-environment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The preconfigured IntelliJ IDEA project with modules for two sample plugins and a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Running programs in batch mode' space=''] (command-line) tool is provided with a program installation directory. The project can be found in *<modeling tool installation directory>/openapi/ide/intellij.zip.*

Let's use MagicDraw as an example to describe the following procedures.

To setup the IntelliJ IDEA environment for the MagicDraw (or other program according to a modeling tool you are using) development

1. In IntelliJ IDEA, add a path variable named MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY and pointing to your version of MagicDraw (CSM, CEA that you have the licence for, as it starts like a normal version and will require an activated licence) installation directory.

2. Extract *<MagicDraw installation directory>/openapi/ide/intellij.zip*to a chosen directory.

3. Open the extracted IntelliJ IDEA project *MagicDraw development.ipr*from that directory.

The launch configuration is designed to load plugins from the program installation directory (see step #1) and two plugins from the IDEA project. Thus, if the *md.plugins.dir* java system property is not defined (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Plugins directories' space='']), developing plugins are not loaded.

After projects are imported and the launch configurations are prepared, the IDEA project are ready for the source code development and running/debugging.

The libraries (jar files) of the plugin must be added to the development class path throughout the plugin dependency hierarchy if the developing code depends on that plugin. 
For example, if the code depends on plugin A; plugin A depends on plugins B and C; plugin B depends on plugin D, the libraries of all plugins (A, B, C, and D) must be added to the class path.For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Identifying plugin dependencies' space=''] page.

When you launch your own plugin, you need to add all jar filesthat are required by your pluginfrom appropriate plugins. The MagicDraw jar files can be found in *MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY/lib* and its sub directories, whereas plugins' jar files can be found in *MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY/plugins* and its sub directories.

Even if the plugin descriptor file contains information about a runtime plugin *.jar* file, it is not necessary to build and deploy this *.jar*file to a plugin directory while a plugin is developed under IntelliJ IDEA.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The project can be found in <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">&lt;modeling tool  installation directory&gt;/openapi/ide/intellij.zip.</em></p><p>Let's use MagicDraw as an example to describe the following procedures.</p><p><br /></p><p>To setup the IntelliJ IDEA environment for the MagicDraw (<span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">or other program according to a modeling tool you are using</span>) development</p><hr /><ol><li>In IntelliJ IDEA, add a path variable named MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY and pointing to your version of MagicDraw (CSM, CEA that you have the licence for, as it starts like a normal version and will require an activated licence) installation directory.</li></ol><p>      2. Extract <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">&lt;MagicDraw installation directory&gt;/openapi/ide/intellij.zip </em>to a chosen <span>directory </span>.</p><p>      3. Open the extracted IntelliJ IDEA project <em>MagicDraw development.ipr </em>from that <span>directory </span>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="979e476a-c332-4829-b545-e533465a22a7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The launch configuration is designed to load plugins from the program installation directory (see step #1) and two plugins from the IDEA project. Thus, if the <em>md.plugins.dir</em> java system property is not defined (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Plugins directories" /></ac:link>), developing plugins are not loaded.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p>After projects are imported and the launch configurations are prepared, the IDEA project are ready for the source code development and running/debugging.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c27f445-666e-4c1f-918f-1b0d5c52c5fa"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The libraries (jar files) of the plugin must be added to the development class path throughout the plugin dependency hierarchy if the developing code depends on that plugin.</span><br /><span>For example, if the code depends on plugin A; plugin A depends on plugins B and C; plugin B depends on plugin D, the libraries of all plugins (A, B, C, and D) must be added to the class path.<br /></span><span>For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Identifying plugin dependencies" /></ac:link> page.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="535eba12-13fe-4164-9536-bcf3bc0281c1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When you launch your own plugin, you need to add all jar files</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> that are required by your plugin</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> from appropriate plugins. The MagicDraw jar files can be found in <em>MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY/lib</em> and its sub directories, whereas plugins' jar files can be found in <em>MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY/plugins</em> and its sub directories.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b206bb9c-fbb2-4cd4-abdf-61dfd0080691"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Even if the plugin descriptor file contains information about a runtime plugin <em>.jar</em> file, it is not necessary to build and deploy this <em>.jar </em>file to a plugin directory while a plugin is developed under IntelliJ IDEA.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989083 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00087: (2026x Refresh1) Diagram presentation element

- page_id: `303989083`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989083/2026x+Refresh1+Diagram+presentation+element
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:17.593+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Presentation elements (symbols)
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement* is a one to one mapping to UML *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram*. It means for every *Diagram* in a model exists one and only one *DiagramPresentationElement*. Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getDiagram(Diagram[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Project.html#getDiagram-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram-)* to get it for a Diagram.

The purpose of this presentation element is to manage all other presentation elements used in a Diagram:

- To get a collection of inner presentation elements, use the com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement.getPresentationElements() method. This method returns only direct children of the diagram.
- To open a diagram in the program UI, call DiagramPresentationElement.open() .
- To create/modify/delete inner presentation elements of the diagram, use com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement</em> is a one to one mapping to UML <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram</em>. It means for every <em>Diagram</em> in a model exists one and only one <em>DiagramPresentationElement</em>. Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getDiagram(Diagram<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Project.html#getDiagram-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram-">)</a></em> to get it for a Diagram.</p><p>The purpose of this presentation element is to manage all other presentation elements used in a Diagram:</p><ul><li>To get a collection of inner presentation elements, use the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement.getPresentationElements()</em> method. This method returns only direct children of the diagram.</li><li>To open a diagram in the program UI, call <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">DiagramPresentationElement.open()</em>.</li><li>To create/modify/delete inner presentation elements of the diagram, use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager</em>.<br /><br /></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989087 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00088: (2026x Refresh1) Diagram types

- page_id: `303989087`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989087/2026x+Refresh1+Diagram+types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:17.814+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are two groups of diagrams in our modeling tools – creatable and not creatable. Only the diagrams of a creatable type can be created (instantiated). A not creatable diagram serves as the base for other types of diagrams.

There are 17 predefined types of diagrams (13 creatable and 4 not). Creatable diagrams are:

- Class
- Use Case
- Object
- Communication
- Sequence
- State Machine
- Protocol State Machine
- Activity
- Composite Structure
- Component
- Deployment
- Package
- Profile

Not creatable diagrams:

- Interaction
- Behavior
- Any
- Static

Communication and Sequence diagrams are subdiagrams of the Interaction diagram. Activity, Interaction, State Machine, and Protocol State Machine diagrams are subdiagrams of the Behavior diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The only way to add a new diagram type to the modeling tool is to extend one of the already existing diagram types and register it. This mechanism is described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Adding new diagram types' space=''].

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

Only the diagrams of a creatable type can be created (instantiated). A not creatable diagram serves as the base for other types of diagrams.</p><p>There are 17 predefined types of diagrams (13 creatable and 4 not). Creatable diagrams are:</p><ul><li>Class</li><li>Use Case</li><li>Object</li><li>Communication</li><li>Sequence</li><li>State Machine</li><li>Protocol State Machine</li><li>Activity</li><li>Composite Structure</li><li>Component</li><li>Deployment</li><li>Package</li><li>Profile</li></ul><p><br />Not creatable diagrams:</p><ul><li>Interaction</li><li>Behavior</li><li>Any</li><li>Static</li></ul><p>Communication and Sequence diagrams are subdiagrams of the Interaction diagram. Activity, Interaction, State Machine, and Protocol State Machine diagrams are subdiagrams of the Behavior diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_type_hierarchy.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Diagram types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The only way to add a new diagram type to the modeling tool is to extend one of the already existing diagram types and register it. This mechanism is described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding new diagram types" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d2f565e-f93a-44ee-a965-cd4978a35814"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="eb619759-b916-4ba7-89cb-addce6204b76"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989354 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00089: (2026x Refresh1) Display Values in Diagrams

- page_id: `303989354`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989354/2026x+Refresh1+Display+Values+in+Diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:32.636+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 > (2026x Refresh1) Alf Plugin Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x Refresh1) Java APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Displaying Runtime Values

When any native model execution is not running in the Cameo Simulation Toolkit, an external tool executes the model. There is a capability to dynamically display any custom value string on parts and/or ports in structure diagrams, without modifying the model in Cameo Simulation Toolkit. Currently, only parts and ports that are typed with a primitive data type are supported.

Start by extending *com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.dashboard.api.ExternalValueProvider*class to create a custom implementation. The returned custom values will be displayed on the diagrams representing the *context*for which the *ExternalValueProvider* instance was constructed*.* The model is not modified in any way when these values are displayed. The color in which the values are displayed is configurable via the **Runtime Value Text Color on Shapes**project option. In the image below, two such values are provided and displayed in a diagram of the *Transmission.mdzip* sample project: **30.0 mi/h** for **carSpeed**and **19.0**for **wheelDiameter**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Image displaying the runtime values

Below is the implementation that was designed specifically for the *Transmission.mdzip* sample and provides just the two values seen in the above image:

```java
pathInContext)
	{
		// values should be pre-collected for diagram related performance reasons. Avoid time demanding implementations (e.g. remote calls) of this method
		if (element == carSpeedElement)
		{
			return externalExecution.getCarSpeed() + " mi/h";
		}
		else if (element == wheelDiameterElement)
		{
			return externalExecution.getWheelDiameter() + "\""; // double quote to represent inches
		}
		return null; // do not provide custom value for other elements in this context
	}

	/**
	 * To be called every time speed changes to call {@link #getValueText(Element, List)} for that element again
	 * @see #invalidate(Element)
	 * @see #invalidateAll() 
	 */
	public void invalidateCarSpeed()
	{
		invalidate(carSpeedElement);
	}
}]]>
```

To have the effect, an instance of the implemented provider must be registered to the *ExternalValueProviders* service, as illustrated here:

```java
// The root context of the external execution. Values will only be visible in the Diagrams that have this element set as context
	Classifier context = Finder.byQualifiedName().find(project, "Transmission::Structure::PowerTrain", Class.class);
	if (context != null)
	{
		EngineControlUnitValueProvider valueProvider = new EngineControlUnitValueProvider(context);
		ExternalValueProviders.getInstance(project).register(valueProvider);

		// if later the provider needs to be unregistered, it should be done by calling:
		// ExternalValueProviders.getInstance(project).unregister(valueProvider);
		// Since providers are registered for specific open project, there is no obligation to unregister manually on project close.
	}]]>
```

****The full code example is available in*<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\externalvalueindiagrams.*

##### Animating flowing information

Additionally, it is possible to display animation for the information that flows via connector from one end to another. For a fixed amount of time, a banner with the custom value will move from the source towards the target. During the full duration of the animation, the connector presentation element will be highlighted in color which is controlled by a project option **Active Color**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Image displaying the animation in between the connectors

```java
// select connector via which to animate
            ConnectorEnd sourceEnd = selectSourceEnd(connector); // pick end from which to start the animation
			String flowingInformation = "myFlowingInformation"; // hardcoded for example, should be some actual data compatible with end/connector/flow types
			FlowingInformationDescriptor descriptor = new FlowingInformationDescriptor(flowingInformation, connector, sourceEnd);

			long duration = 4000; // 4 seconds to reach the other end
			FlowAnimation.getInstance(Project.getProject(connector)).animate(descriptor, duration, finished -> {/* do something when animation completes*/});]]>
```

The full code example for this feature is available in*<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\flowanimation.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Displaying Runtime Values</h3><p>When any native model execution is not running in the Cameo Simulation Toolkit, an external tool executes the model. There is a capability to dynamically display any custom value string on parts and/or ports in structure diagrams, without modifying the model in Cameo Simulation Toolkit. Currently, only parts and ports that are typed with a primitive data type are supported.</p><p>Start by extending <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.dashboard.api.ExternalValueProvider </em>class to create a custom implementation. The returned custom values will be displayed on the diagrams representing the <em>context </em>for which the <em>ExternalValueProvider</em> instance was constructed<em>.</em> The model is not modified in any way when these values are displayed. The color in which the values are displayed is configurable via the  <strong>Runtime Value Text Color on Shapes </strong>project option. In the image below, two such values are provided and displayed in a diagram of the <em>Transmission.mdzip</em> sample project: <strong>30.0 mi/h</strong> for <strong>carSpeed </strong>and <strong>19.0 </strong>for <strong>wheelDiameter</strong>.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displayed_Values.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Display Values in Diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Image displaying the runtime values</h6><p>Below is the implementation that was designed specifically for the <em>Transmission.mdzip</em> sample and provides just the two values seen in the above image:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ac38052-7ee8-43ed-8f46-f0f3fe974dd0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[//Engine Control Unit refers to ECU block in the Transmission sample project
public class EngineControlUnitValueProvider extends ExternalValueProvider
{
	private final Element carSpeedElement;
	private final Element wheelDiameterElement;
	
	//Mimics some model execution process that happens outside this tool and produces values to display
	private final MockExternalExecution externalExecution;

	public EngineControlUnitValueProvider(Element context)
	{
		super(context);
		Project project = Project.getProject(context);
		carSpeedElement = FinderByQualifiedName.getInstance().find(project, "Transmission::Structure::ECU::carSpeed", Property.class);
		wheelDiameterElement = FinderByQualifiedName.getInstance().find(project, "Transmission::Structure::ECU::wheelDiameter", Property.class);
		externalExecution = new MockExternalExecution(this);
	}

	@CheckForNull
	@Override
	public String getValueText(Element element, List<? extends Element> pathInContext)
	{
		// values should be pre-collected for diagram related performance reasons. Avoid time demanding implementations (e.g. remote calls) of this method
		if (element == carSpeedElement)
		{
			return externalExecution.getCarSpeed() + " mi/h";
		}
		else if (element == wheelDiameterElement)
		{
			return externalExecution.getWheelDiameter() + "\""; // double quote to represent inches
		}
		return null; // do not provide custom value for other elements in this context
	}

	/**
	 * To be called every time speed changes to call {@link #getValueText(Element, List)} for that element again
	 * @see #invalidate(Element)
	 * @see #invalidateAll() 
	 */
	public void invalidateCarSpeed()
	{
		invalidate(carSpeedElement);
	}
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To have the effect, an instance of the implemented provider must be registered to the <em>ExternalValueProviders</em> service, as illustrated here:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b0b5143-d3dd-4a29-bfc0-8b89d868f934"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	Project project = <...>
	// The root context of the external execution. Values will only be visible in the Diagrams that have this element set as context
	Classifier context = Finder.byQualifiedName().find(project, "Transmission::Structure::PowerTrain", Class.class);
	if (context != null)
	{
		EngineControlUnitValueProvider valueProvider = new EngineControlUnitValueProvider(context);
		ExternalValueProviders.getInstance(project).register(valueProvider);

		// if later the provider needs to be unregistered, it should be done by calling:
		// ExternalValueProviders.getInstance(project).unregister(valueProvider);
		// Since providers are registered for specific open project, there is no obligation to unregister manually on project close.
	}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f9d9d5b-ed4b-48a7-a50a-738aab1b1876"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The full code example is available in<span> </span></span><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\externalvalueindiagrams.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Animating flowing information</h3><p>Additionally, it is possible to display animation for the information that flows via connector from one end to another. For a fixed amount of time, a banner with the custom value will move from the source towards the target. During the full duration of the animation, the connector presentation element will be highlighted in color which is controlled by a project option <strong>Active Color</strong>.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Flow_Animation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Display Values in Diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Image displaying the animation in between the connectors</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1f39e5ca-598c-46ef-a577-bb49460f9e71"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[			Connector connector = <..> // select connector via which to animate
            ConnectorEnd sourceEnd = selectSourceEnd(connector); // pick end from which to start the animation
			String flowingInformation = "myFlowingInformation"; // hardcoded for example, should be some actual data compatible with end/connector/flow types
			FlowingInformationDescriptor descriptor = new FlowingInformationDescriptor(flowingInformation, connector, sourceEnd);

			long duration = 4000; // 4 seconds to reach the other end
			FlowAnimation.getInstance(Project.getProject(connector)).animate(descriptor, duration, finished -> {/* do something when animation completes*/});]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a5b4d2f-fdca-4666-bbd0-7e9d025f71f6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The full code example for this feature is available in<span> </span></span><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\flowanimation.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989153 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00090: (2026x Refresh1) Displaying ports

- page_id: `303989153`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989153/2026x+Refresh1+Displaying+ports
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:23.460+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Presentation elements (symbols) > (2026x Refresh1) Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayPorts#displayAllPorts

com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayPorts#collectAvailablePorts

com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayPorts#displayPorts(com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement, java.util.List<com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.compositestructures.mdports.Port>)

com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayPorts#displayPorts(com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement, java.util.List<com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.compositestructures.mdports.Port>, boolean, boolean, boolean

###### **Example: Displaying unnamed ports**

```java
ports = DisplayPorts.collectAvailablePorts(view, false, false);
	List unnamedPorts = ports.stream().filter(port -> port.getName().isEmpty()).toList();
	DisplayPorts.displayPorts(view, unnamedPorts);

	sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]>
```

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\displayports

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell> We provide an API to simplify displaying of Ports on Symbols. Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayPorts#displayAllPorts</em> to display all ports or <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayPorts#collectAvailablePorts</em> to get available ports and then <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayPorts#displayPorts(com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement, java.util.List&lt;com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.compositestructures.mdports.Port&gt;)</em> or <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayPorts#displayPorts(com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement, java.util.List&lt;com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.compositestructures.mdports.Port&gt;, boolean, boolean, boolean</em>) to display just some of them.<br /><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Example: Displaying unnamed ports<br /></strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="27d9045e-ee43-4a27-b18b-9e033face031"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance();
	sessionManager.createSession(project, getName());

	PresentationElement view = ...; // A symbol for which you want to display ports

	List<Port> ports = DisplayPorts.collectAvailablePorts(view, false, false);
	List<Port> unnamedPorts = ports.stream().filter(port -> port.getName().isEmpty()).toList();
	DisplayPorts.displayPorts(view, unnamedPorts);

	sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li style="font-style: italic;"><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\displayports</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58e45d31-88de-4344-8caa-c6382236cc70"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989172 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00091: (2026x Refresh1) Displaying related elements in the diagram

- page_id: `303989172`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989172/2026x+Refresh1+Displaying+related+elements+in+the+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:25.363+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Presentation elements (symbols) > (2026x Refresh1) Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tools allow displaying symbols that are related to a given symbol via relationships. The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayRelatedSymbols* class provides API methods for this. Using this class you can control any of the following behaviors for displaying the related symbols logic:

- What relationship types should be included.
- The depth of a relationship tree.
- Whether or not existing symbols should be reused.

###### **Example: Displaying related generalizations and interface realizations**

```java

```

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\displayrelated

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Our modeling tools allow displaying symbols that are related to a given symbol via relationships. The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayRelatedSymbols</em> class provides API methods for this. Using this class you can control any of the following behaviors for displaying the related symbols logic:</p><ul><li>What relationship types should be included.</li><li>The depth of a relationship tree.</li><li>Whether or not existing symbols should be reused.</li></ul><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Example: Displaying related generalizations and interface realizations</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="27d9045e-ee43-4a27-b18b-9e033face031"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance();
    sessionManager.createSession(project, "Display related");
 
    Set linkTypes = new HashSet();
    linkTypes.add(new LinkType(Generalization.class));
    linkTypes.add(new LinkType(InterfaceRealization.class));
    
    DisplayRelatedSymbolsInfo info = new DisplayRelatedSymbolsInfo(linkTypes);
    info.setDepthLimited(true);
    info.setDepthLimit(3);
 
    PresentationElement view = ...; // A symbol for which you need to invoke the displaying related symbols action.
    DisplayRelatedSymbols.displayRelatedSymbols(view, info);
    sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\displayrelated</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58e45d31-88de-4344-8caa-c6382236cc70"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00092: (2026x Refresh1) Distributing Resources

- page_id: `303989292`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989292/2026x+Refresh1+Distributing+Resources
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:29.300+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General
- labels: ['resource-manager']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

515448144

515448146

515448145

In this section, you will find information about how to share your created resources with other users. The easiest way to distribute data is to store resources in the one zip archive and import files to your modeling tool with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Manager' space='MT'].

You can distribute the following types of resources:

- Custom Diagrams
- Profiles
- Templates
- Samples and Documentation
- Plugins

You can distribute the one resource type or you may distribute the whole set of resources.

515448143

**Related pages**

true

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989223 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00093: (2026x Refresh1) Eclipse Development Setup

- page_id: `303989223`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989223/2026x+Refresh1+Eclipse+Development+Setup
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:26.534+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The SysML v2 plugin for CEA/CSM/MSOSA/MCSE includes a fully functional Eclipse example project. This project demonstrates how to interact with the SysML v2 metamodel using the Open API.

It serves as a reference implementation and a starting point for developing custom plugins.

The tool and the SysML v2 plugin need to be installed before we proceed with setting up the environment.

#### **Locating the Eclipse Project**

After installing the SysML v2 plugin via the Resource Manager, the Eclipse project archive is available at:

**<tool.install.dir>*\openapi\ide\md_sysml2_openapi-…-ide-eclipse.zip.*

#### **Importing the Eclipse Projects**

The archive contains three related Eclipse projects bundled in a single ZIP file.

To import them:

1. Open Eclipse
2. Select File → Import → Existing Projects into Workspace
3. Choose Select archive file and point to the ZIP file
4. Import all projects

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### **Configuring the MagicDraw Installation Path**

The projects rely on an Eclipse Linked Resource variable:

MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY

Set this variable to your installation directory. This allows Eclipse to resolve all required Open API JAR dependencies.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### **Running the Ant Build Script**

Before compiling the projects, you must initialize dependencies:

1. Locate the build.xml script in the MagicDraw project
2. Run the script to link the required API JAR files from your installation into the workspace

After the build completes:

- Refresh the workspace (F5) or
- Restart Eclipse

Once refreshed, Eclipse will rebuild the classpath and all error markers should be resolved.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### **Launching MagicDraw with the Example Plugin**

After the projects compile successfully, you can run MagicDraw directly from Eclipse:

- Use the provided launch configuration in the Launch project
- This configuration loads:
  - Plugins from the installation directory
  - The SysML v2 example plugin from your workspace

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### **Ready for Development**

Your Eclipse environment is now ready for:

- Developing SysML v2 plugins
- Running and debugging directly from Eclipse
- Exploring example Open API usage

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989567 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00094: (2026x Refresh1) Editing API

- page_id: `303989567`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989567/2026x+Refresh1+Editing+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:42.507+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Model Modification
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The `[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.editing.Editing](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/editing/Editing.html)` API is the recommended way to perform model modifications. It provides built-in support for **command tracking** and **undo/redo**.

All changes executed through this API are:

- Recorded as a [command](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/editing/Command.html) Each execution is captured as a named command
- Stored in command history Enables undo and redo of model changes

This ensures that model modifications are **safe, consistent, and traceable**.

#### **Getting an Editing Instance**

Obtain an `[Editing](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/editing/Editing.html)` instance from the current project:

```java

```

#### **Executing Model Changes**

Use the `execute` method to perform model modifications.

- The provided name identifies the command in the undo/redo history
- All changes inside the lambda are executed within a single transaction

```java
{
    // model changes here
});]]>
```

#### **Best Practices**

- Prefer [Editing.execute(...)](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/editing/Editing.html#execute(java.lang.String,java.lang.Runnable)) for all model changes
- Use meaningful command names (they appear in undo/redo history)
- Group related changes into a single execution block
- Avoid modifying the model outside of an execution context

#### **When to Use This API**

Use the Editing API for:

- Creating, updating, or deleting model elements
- Batch modifications that should be treated as one operation
- Any changes that must support undo/redo

For lower-level control over sessions, see: **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API' space='']**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/editing/Editing.html">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.editing.Editing</a></code> API is the recommended way to perform model modifications. It provides built-in support for <strong>command tracking</strong> and <strong>undo/redo</strong>.</p><p>All changes executed through this API are:</p><ul><li data-uuid="f90e3587-c8c0-4dc2-9a05-9f4c4b874fea">Recorded as a <strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/editing/Command.html">command</a></strong><br />Each execution is captured as a named command</li><li data-uuid="d1be5e0f-3f28-4f50-a209-3b0d43322eab">Stored in <strong>command history</strong><br />Enables undo and redo of model changes</li></ul><p>This ensures that model modifications are <strong>safe, consistent, and traceable</strong>.</p><h2><span><strong>Getting an Editing Instance</strong></span></h2><p>Obtain an <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/editing/Editing.html">Editing</a></code> instance from the current project:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb723b17-8711-4e6f-955d-d4733302f21a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.editing.Editing;

Editing editing = Editing.getInstance(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><span><strong>Executing Model Changes</strong></span></h2><p>Use the <code>execute</code> method to perform model modifications.</p><ul><li data-uuid="aff01d1a-acda-4a5b-b65d-8490edbd7a28">The provided name identifies the command in the undo/redo history</li><li data-uuid="b5990400-dda1-47d2-ac52-a983172f69cb">All changes inside the lambda are executed within a single transaction</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0dd73da-f721-4f42-b599-6db263d8463a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.editing.Editing;

Editing editing = Editing.getInstance(project);

editing.execute("Create element", () -> {
    // model changes here
});]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><span><strong>Best Practices</strong></span></h2><ul><li data-uuid="e4f0f243-fcd3-498a-945a-ccfcac735c5e">Prefer <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/foundation/editing/Editing.html#execute(java.lang.String,java.lang.Runnable)">Editing.execute(...)</a></code> for all model changes</li><li data-uuid="2a46f50e-7f67-4c7d-93ca-a0750dc54979">Use meaningful command names (they appear in undo/redo history)</li><li data-uuid="82b316be-0c26-4616-a807-6dc9c6699bc6">Group related changes into a single execution block</li><li data-uuid="9c5e6c91-e0e6-42ec-9b17-784439858144">Avoid modifying the model outside of an execution context</li></ul><h2><span><strong>When to Use This API</strong></span></h2><p>Use the Editing API for:</p><ul><li data-uuid="548e2c26-a28c-402f-aabd-2994eaddb9b3">Creating, updating, or deleting model elements</li><li data-uuid="d393cae1-6cb6-46b0-a986-fb3b3be3d9cc">Batch modifications that should be treated as one operation</li><li data-uuid="c919b600-7e13-4aaf-ba08-2a1cdf6f66e7">Any changes that must support undo/redo</li></ul><p>For lower-level control over sessions, see: <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API" /></ac:link></strong></p>
````

## PAGE 00095: (2026x Refresh1) Editing hyperlinks

- page_id: `303989145`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989145/2026x+Refresh1+Editing+hyperlinks
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:22.547+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Managing navigation in model > (2026x Refresh1) Managing hyperlinks
- labels: ['hyperlink-text', 'hyperlink-text-active', 'hyperlink-model', 'hyperlink-model-active']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Hyperlinks are implemented using stereotypes and tag values. The *HyperlinkOwner*stereotype has the following its properties (tags):

- hyperlinkText - all simple hyperlinks
- hyperlinkTextActive - an active simple hyperlink
- hyperlinkModel - all hyperlinks to a model element
- hyperlinkModelActive - an active hyperlink to a model element

You can use generic profiling API to edit/add/remove hyperlinks to model elements. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with stereotypes and tagged values' space=''] for more details.

Recommended way is to use utility class *com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinkUtils*. This class provides utility methods for adding/removing/retrieving***com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.Hyperlink* from model Element,

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with stereotypes and tagged values' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````
