<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989180 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00147: (2026x Refresh1) MD Customization for SysML Profile

- page_id: `303989180`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989180/2026x+Refresh1+MD+Customization+for+SysML+Profile
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:25.494+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You need to import *com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile* to use this API class.

**Get a string constant for each property of stereotype (tag)**

Usage includes “MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.STEREOTYPE_PROPERTY_NAME”.

For example, MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.NUMBEROWNER_PREFIX_PROPERTY returns a string of “prefix”.

****

**Get a stereotype element**

Usage includes:

- “MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.getInstance(project).getStereotype()” - where project refers to the project which uses MD Customization for SysML Profile.
- “MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.getInstance(element).getStereotype()” - where element refers to the element in the project which uses MD Customization for SysML Profile.

For example, MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.getInstance(project).getPartProperty() returns the reference to the «PartProperty» stereotype object.

****

**Check if an element is stereotyped**

Usage includes “MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.isStereotype(Elem)” - where Elem is the element you would like to check.

For example, given an element “Elem”, MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.isValueProperty(Elem) returns True if the element “Elem” has «ValueProperty» stereotype applied, and returns false otherwise.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You need to import <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.sysml.util.MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile</em> to use this API class.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Get a string constant for each property of stereotype (tag)</strong></p><p>Usage includes “MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.STEREOTYPE_PROPERTY_NAME”.</p><p>For example, MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.NUMBEROWNER_PREFIX_PROPERTY returns a string of “prefix”.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Get a stereotype element</strong></p><p>Usage includes:</p><ul><li>“MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.getInstance(project).getStereotype()” - where project refers to the project which uses MD Customization for SysML Profile.</li><li>“MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.getInstance(element).getStereotype()” - where element refers to the element in the project which uses MD Customization for SysML Profile.</li></ul><p>For example, MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.getInstance(project).getPartProperty() returns the reference to the «PartProperty» stereotype object.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Check if an element is stereotyped</strong></p><p>Usage includes “MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.isStereotype(Elem)” - where Elem is the element you would like to check.</p><p>For example, given an element “Elem”, MDCustomizationForSysMLProfile.isValueProperty(Elem) returns True if the element “Elem” has «ValueProperty» stereotype applied, and returns false otherwise.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989143 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00148: (2026x Refresh1) MDEL hyperlink

- page_id: `303989143`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989143/2026x+Refresh1+MDEL+hyperlink
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:22.334+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Managing navigation in model > (2026x Refresh1) Managing hyperlinks
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

MagicDraw has a functionality to expose every element as URL. Later this URL can be added into external documents. Also that URL can be used to open/activate an element in a project.

For that purpose the **mdel://** protocol is introduced.

Use c*om.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinkUtils* to retrieve mdel URL of elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>MagicDraw has a functionality to expose every element as URL. Later this URL can be added into external documents. Also that URL can be used to open/activate an element in a project.</p><p>For that purpose the <strong>mdel://</strong> protocol is introduced. </p><p>Use c<em>om.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.HyperlinkUtils</em> to retrieve mdel URL of elements.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989047 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00149: (2026x Refresh1) MDP protocol

- page_id: `303989047`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989047/2026x+Refresh1+MDP+protocol
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:15.213+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General > (2026x Refresh1) Adding new functionality > (2026x Refresh1) Working with icons
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

MagicDraw registers a custom URL protocol handler **mdp://**for loading resources (icons) from MagicDraw or plugins jars/classpath. Resources can also be loaded from attached files in an opened project.

Usage samples:

- Use the mdp protocol in the HTML based model element documentation to point to some image from a plugin jar.
- Use the mdp protocol in the HTML based model element documentation to point to some attached image from a project.

**URL syntax**

| mdp://resource/ | Use the "resource" host to load resources from jars/classpaths. For example, to load the activitydiagram.png icon from MagicDraw jar, use such URL:mdp://resource/com/nomagic/magicdraw/icons/diagrams/activitydiagram.png |
| --- | --- |
| mdp://attachedFile/ | Use the "attachedFile" host to load resources from the attached file into the active project. For example, to load the icon from the attached file element with ID "_18_2_8ca0285_1436339188295_24687_13288", use such URL:mdp://attachedFile#_18_2_8ca0285_1436339188295_24687_13288 |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>MagicDraw registers a custom URL protocol handler <strong>mdp:// </strong>for loading resources (icons) from MagicDraw or plugins jars/classpath. Resources can also be loaded from attached files in an opened project. </p><div><p>Usage samples:</p><ul><li>Use the <em>mdp</em> protocol in the HTML based model element documentation to point to some image from a plugin jar.</li><li>Use the <em>mdp</em> protocol in the HTML based model element documentation to point to some attached image from a project.</li></ul><p> </p><p><strong>URL syntax</strong></p><table><tbody><tr><td>mdp://resource/</td><td><p>Use the &quot;<em>resource</em>&quot; host to load resources from jars/classpaths. For example, to load the <em>activitydiagram.png</em> icon from MagicDraw jar, use such URL:</p><pre><span style="color: rgb(0,128,0);">mdp://resource/com/nomagic/magicdraw/icons/diagrams/activitydiagram.png</span></pre></td></tr><tr><td>mdp://attachedFile/</td><td><p><span>Use the &quot;<em>attachedFile</em>&quot; host to load resources from the attached file into the active project. For example, to load the icon from the attached file element with ID &quot;<em>_18_2_8ca0285_1436339188295_24687_13288</em>&quot;, use such URL:</span></p><pre><span style="color: rgb(0,128,0);">mdp://attachedFile#_18_2_8ca0285_1436339188295_24687_13288</span></pre></td></tr></tbody></table></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989390 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00150: (2026x Refresh1) Merging and differencing

- page_id: `303989390`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989390/2026x+Refresh1+Merging+and+differencing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:35.270+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The modeling tools provide APIs for project merging and differencing. There are two separate APIs:

- Merge - allows for project merging. The Merge API comes together with the Project Merge plugin, which requires a license. You can access the Merge API javadoc at <installation_directory>\openapi\docs\merge_javadoc.zip once you acquire and install the Project Merge plugin.
- Compare - allows for project differences comparison. The Compare API comes together with the internal Merge Core plugin, which is available pre-installed on the modeling tool. You can access the Compare API javadoc at <installation_directory>\openapi\docs\javadoc.zip once you have the modeling tool installed.

Project merging consists of three steps:

1. Discovering project changes (differences) . The ancestor (in a 3-way merge only) and source projects are loaded (if they are not loaded yet) for comparing.
2. Accepting / rejecting changes . All target changes that do not conflict source changes are applied (source changes conflicting with target changes are rejected). A 2-way merge produces source changes only.
3. Applying accepted changes . Changes are applied on the ancestor project (target and ancestor projects are the same in a 2-way merge).

There are APIs for standard and advanced project merging. The standard API encapsulates all three steps into a single call, while the advanced merge API allows for accessing the project differences and controlling what changes are accepted or rejected.

The following figure shows the domain model of the merging procedure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****You can find the code examples:

- for Merge API (requires the Project Merge plugin to be installed first): <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\merge
- for Compare API: < installation_direc tory>\openapi\examples\compare

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The modeling tools provide APIs for project merging and differencing. There are two separate APIs:</p><ul><li><strong>Merge</strong> - allows for project merging. The Merge API comes together with the Project Merge plugin, which requires a license. You can access the Merge API javadoc at <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\docs\merge_javadoc.zip</em> once you acquire and install the Project Merge plugin.</li><li><strong>Compare</strong> - allows for project differences comparison. The Compare API comes together with the internal Merge Core plugin, which is available pre-installed on the modeling tool. You can access the Compare API javadoc at <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\docs\javadoc.zip</em> once you have the modeling tool installed.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>Project merging consists of three steps:</p><ol><li><strong>Discovering project changes (differences)</strong>. The ancestor (in a 3-way merge only) and source projects are loaded (if they are not loaded yet) for comparing.</li><li><strong>Accepting / rejecting changes</strong>. All target changes that do not conflict source changes are applied (source changes conflicting with target changes are rejected). A 2-way merge produces source changes only.</li><li><strong>Applying accepted changes</strong>. Changes are applied on the ancestor project (target and ancestor projects are the same in a 2-way merge).</li></ol><p>There are APIs for standard and advanced project merging. The standard API encapsulates all three steps into a single call, while the advanced merge API allows for accessing the project differences and controlling what changes are accepted or rejected.</p><p>The following figure shows the domain model of the merging procedure.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="MergeDomain.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Merging and differencing" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples:</p><ul><li>for Merge API (requires the Project Merge plugin to be installed first): <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\merge</em></li><li>for Compare API: &lt;<em>installation_direc</em><em>tory&gt;\openapi\examples\compare</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989553 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00151: (2026x Refresh1) Metadata Utility Class

- page_id: `303989553`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989553/2026x+Refresh1+Metadata+Utility+Class
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:41.588+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 > (2026x Refresh1) Working With Model > (2026x Refresh1) Utilities and Helpers
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Metadata](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Metadata.html)* utility class provides a comprehensive API for working with **[metadata features](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/MetadataFeature.html)**, **[metaclasses](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Metaclass.html)**, and **[annotations](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Annotation.html)** in the KerML modeling environment.

It supports:

- Retrieving metadata (owned, external, semantic, self)
- Creating and applying metadata
- Idempotent metadata management
- Working with metaclasses and EMF EClass
- Inspecting applicability and type relationships
- Resolving base types and annotated element types

This class centralizes all common metadata operations and should be the primary entry point when working with metadata in Open API.

#### **Retrieving Metadata**

##### Get Owned Semantic Metadata

Returns only **semantic metadata owned by the element**.

Use this when metadata contributes directly to the element’s semantics.

```text
semantic = Metadata.getOwnedSemanticMetadata(element);
]]>
```

##### Get All Metadata

Returns all metadata applied to the element:

- Owned metadata
- Externally defined metadata

```text
metadata = Metadata.getMetadata(element);
]]>
```

##### Get Metadata Including Self

Includes:

- Owned metadata
- External metadata
- Self metadata (defined by element syntax)

```text
metadata = Metadata.getMetadataWithSelf(element);
]]>
```

##### Get Owned Metadata Only

```text
owned = Metadata.getOwnedMetadata(element);
]]>
```

##### Find Metadata by Metaclass

Retrieve metadata matching a specific [metaclass](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Metaclass.html).

```text

```

#### **Creating Metadata**

##### Create Owned Metadata

Creates metadata directly owned by the element.

```text

```

##### Create Metadata in Namespace

Creates metadata in a shared [namespace](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Namespace.html) and annotates the element.

```text

```

##### Get or Create Owned Metadata (Idempotent)

Ensures metadata exists without duplication.

```text

```

##### Set Owned Metadata in Bulk

Ensures [element](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html)has exactly the given metadata set.

Optionally removes non-matching metadata.

```text
metaclasses = ...;

Metadata.setOwnedMetadata(element, metaclasses, true);
]]>
```

#### **Working with Metaclasses**

##### Get [Metaclass](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Metaclass.html)of Element

```text

```

##### Resolve EClass → [Metaclass](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Metaclass.html)

```text

```

##### Resolve Metaclass → EClass

```text

```

#### **Metadata Inspection & Utilities**

##### Check Applicability

Determines if metadata can be applied to an element.

```text

```

##### Check Annotation Presence

```text

```

##### Get Metaclasses from Metadata

```text
metaclasses = Metadata.getMetaclassesOfMetadata(element);
]]>
```

##### Get Metaclass Name

```text

```

#### **Self Metadata**

Self metadata represents metadata defined by the element’s own syntax.

##### Get Self Metadata

```text

```

##### Check if Metadata is Self Metadata

```text

```

##### Get EClass of Self Metadata

```text

```

#### **Base Types and Classifiers**

##### Get Base Types from Metadata

Used primarily for semantic metadata such as type hierarchies.

```text
baseTypes = Metadata.getBaseTypes(type);
]]>
```

##### Convert to Classifiers

Expands feature types into classifier types.

```text
classifiers = Metadata.toClassifiers(baseTypes);
]]>
```

##### Filter Only Feature Types

```text
features = Metadata.toFeatures(baseTypes);
]]>
```

#### **Advanced Type Checks**

##### Check if Element Matches Metamodel Metaclass

```text

```

##### Check Multiple Metaclasses

```text

```

##### Check Metamodel Feature

```text

```

#### **Summary**

The *[Metadata](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Metadata.html)*utility class is the central API for:

- Managing metadata lifecycle
- Working with metaclasses and annotations
- Performing semantic introspection
- Bridging KerML and EMF metamodel layers

It should be used whenever metadata needs to be **retrieved, created, inspected, or validated** in SysML v2 Open API.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Metadata.html">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Metadata</a></em> utility class provides a comprehensive API for working with <strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/MetadataFeature.html">metadata features</a></strong>, <strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Metaclass.html">metaclasses</a></strong>, and <strong><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Annotation.html">annotations</a></strong> in the KerML modeling environment.</p><p>It supports:</p><ul><li>Retrieving metadata (owned, external, semantic, self)</li><li>Creating and applying metadata</li><li>Idempotent metadata management</li><li>Working with <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Metaclass.html">metaclasses</a> and EMF <em>EClass</em></li><li>Inspecting applicability and type relationships</li><li>Resolving base types and annotated element types</li></ul><p>This class centralizes all common metadata operations and should be the primary entry point when working with metadata in Open API.</p><h2><strong>Retrieving Metadata</strong></h2><h3>Get Owned Semantic Metadata</h3><p>Returns only <strong>semantic metadata owned by the element</strong>.</p><p>Use this when metadata contributes directly to the element’s semantics.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d925d9a-242a-4902-a4d7-5ad450a69a53"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Element element = ...;

List<MetadataFeature> semantic = Metadata.getOwnedSemanticMetadata(element);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Get All Metadata</h3><p>Returns all metadata applied to the element:</p><ul><li>Owned metadata</li><li>Externally defined metadata</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cbd89097-fed9-4a9e-9dd2-7104b7e14819"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Element element = ...;

List<MetadataFeature> metadata = Metadata.getMetadata(element);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Get Metadata Including Self</h3><p>Includes:</p><ul><li>Owned metadata</li><li>External metadata</li><li>Self metadata (defined by element syntax)</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b3c7c39e-aca6-4e33-9250-c06853198c9a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Element element = ...;

List<MetadataFeature> metadata = Metadata.getMetadataWithSelf(element);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Get Owned Metadata Only</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c9cc635f-423d-4482-a631-ddd6471c9d60"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[List<MetadataFeature> owned = Metadata.getOwnedMetadata(element);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Find Metadata by Metaclass</h3><p>Retrieve metadata matching a specific <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Metaclass.html">metaclass</a>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="959ec38d-8137-4f99-92e2-44c657d09a6b"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Metaclass metaclass = ...;

MetadataFeature feature = Metadata.getMetadata(element, metaclass);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Creating Metadata</strong></h2><h3>Create Owned Metadata</h3><p>Creates metadata directly owned by the element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03e3c3b1-a2b5-4b14-942a-48387e215070"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Element element = ...;
Metaclass metaclass = ...;

MetadataFeature feature = Metadata.createOwnedMetadata(element, metaclass);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Create Metadata in Namespace</h3><p>Creates metadata in a shared <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Namespace.html">namespace</a> and annotates the element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="04591114-1126-4091-a3b3-6c58a6485147"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Element element = ...;
Namespace namespace = ...;
Metaclass metaclass = ...;

MetadataFeature feature = Metadata.createMetadata(element, namespace, metaclass);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Get or Create Owned Metadata (Idempotent)</h3><p>Ensures metadata exists without duplication.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="714f7744-50cd-442d-8c9e-3c0f6a71ea86"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[MetadataFeature feature = Metadata.getOrCreateOwnedMetadata(element, metaclass);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Set Owned Metadata in Bulk</h3><p>Ensures <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Element.html"> element </a>has exactly the given metadata set.</p><p>Optionally removes non-matching metadata.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8a505a5-29af-49a3-b3c5-f66deae57fe3"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Collection<Metaclass> metaclasses = ...;

Metadata.setOwnedMetadata(element, metaclasses, true);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Working with Metaclasses</strong></h2><h3>Get <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Metaclass.html"> Metaclass </a>of Element</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="feee8688-7f90-4074-b0ab-9e6069dbf677"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Metaclass mc = Metadata.getMetaClassOf(element);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Resolve EClass → <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Metaclass.html">Metaclass</a></h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11b621f9-d049-4b8e-a4ca-7f646bc5e2ce"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[EClass eClass = element.eClass();

Metaclass mc =
    Metadata.getMetaClassFor(
        ModelElementProject.getProject(element),
        eClass
    );
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Resolve Metaclass → EClass</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8a1c75c-5c78-4045-9d5a-bc565c7dd8b1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[EClass eClass = Metadata.getEClassFor(metaclass);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Metadata Inspection &amp; Utilities</strong></h2><h3>Check Applicability</h3><p>Determines if metadata can be applied to an element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="14112330-7a1b-4c9f-b7ac-26b18456b7a3"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[boolean applicable = Metadata.isApplicable(element);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Check Annotation Presence</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0baf29b-56d9-4530-b7fe-52612e935816"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[boolean hasMetadata = Metadata.isAnnotatedWithOwnedMetadata(element, metaclass);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Get Metaclasses from Metadata</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="80fb5684-e117-4500-bf10-7431803b02b4"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[List<Metaclass> metaclasses = Metadata.getMetaclassesOfMetadata(element);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Get Metaclass Name</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8808bfef-3ae2-457b-b77b-59e892b17c1f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[String name = Metadata.getMetaclassName(feature);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Self Metadata</strong></h2><p>Self metadata represents metadata defined by the element’s own syntax.</p><h3>Get Self Metadata</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bae0a94f-c561-4808-8d00-29285a2bf3ba"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[MetadataFeature self = Metadata.getSelfMetadata(element);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Check if Metadata is Self Metadata</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b4c16f0e-9481-44d8-b018-bec95432b76f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[boolean isSelf = Metadata.isSelfMetadata(feature);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Get EClass of Self Metadata</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="39accedb-8310-417d-b98c-4d0beb691dd2"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[EClass eClass = Metadata.getEClassIfSelfMetadata(feature);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Base Types and Classifiers</strong></h2><h3>Get Base Types from Metadata</h3><p>Used primarily for semantic metadata such as type hierarchies.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="08bb9ad6-5ffc-473f-916a-c7a0b6100bf1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Type type = ...;

List<Type> baseTypes = Metadata.getBaseTypes(type);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Convert to Classifiers</h3><p>Expands feature types into classifier types.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4e50eb2-d75d-44c5-b219-cd054228a7d7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[List<Type> classifiers = Metadata.toClassifiers(baseTypes);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Filter Only Feature Types</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01bfae0e-e4ab-438f-879f-1d35b6d4f46e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[List<Type> features = Metadata.toFeatures(baseTypes);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Advanced Type Checks</strong></h2><h3>Check if Element Matches Metamodel Metaclass</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93108256-4a1d-4a68-b912-26de26a26f65"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[boolean matches = Metadata.isInstanceOfMetamodelMetaclass(element, metaclass);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Check Multiple Metaclasses</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51b26332-9b73-4611-ad73-8b6a72034db7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[boolean matchesAny = Metadata.isInstanceOfMetamodelMetaclass(element, metaclasses);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Check Metamodel Feature</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62ee6991-0a40-46bb-931b-1cdc386caf99"><ac:parameter ac:name="">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[boolean isMetamodel = Metadata.isMetamodelFeature(feature);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Summary</strong></h2><p>The <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Metadata.html"> Metadata </a></em>utility class is the central API for:</p><ul><li>Managing metadata lifecycle</li><li>Working with metaclasses and annotations</li><li>Performing semantic introspection</li><li>Bridging KerML and EMF metamodel layers</li></ul><p>It should be used whenever metadata needs to be <strong>retrieved, created, inspected, or validated</strong> in SysML v2 Open API.</p>
````

## PAGE 00152: (2026x Refresh1) Model manipulation

- page_id: `303989495`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989495/2026x+Refresh1+Model+manipulation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:39.098+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) REST APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1043546914

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1043546918

INLINE

1043546912

INLINE

**On this page**

33

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1043546917

INLINE

Teamwork Cloud operates at the EMF level, while MagicDraw operates at the UML level, which is on top of EMF. REST API, which is the Teamwork Cloud API, also operates at the EMF level.

Therefore, Teamwork Cloud is not affected by UML-specific implementation, and all derived properties may not be saved in the Teamwork Cloud database. The best-known derived property is *owner*. This is the result when there is no such “*setOwner”*in REST API.

Even though all non-derived properties are saved in the Teamwork Cloud database, they are saved as raw EMF data. The user is required to have knowledge about UML models to manipulate data. For example, an applied stereotype is saved as a hierarchy of *InstanceSpecification*, *Slot*, and *ValueSpecification*.

This section describes how to traverse into a UML model and create some elements in it.

##### MagicDraw project

In Teamwork Cloud terminology, a MagicDraw project is referred to as a *resource*. Although REST API can be used to create a Teamwork Cloud resource, it is only a bare project MagicDraw cannot read. A MagicDraw project is composed of many MagicDraw-specific meta-data such as project options, primary project, and used projects configuration. The best way to create a MagicDraw project is to create it by MagicDraw and submit it to the Teamwork Cloud server.

##### MagicDraw primary project

To traverse through a UML model, the primary project must be identified. The model data starts at the revision level. Issuing GET to */revisions/{revisionId}* shows the first-level object in the revision. UUIDs of the first-level object are listed in **rootObjectIDs**.

```text

```

Among these entries, the first element with the **esiproject:EsiProject** type specifies the primary project named **main decomposition project**. Other **esiproject:EsiProject** objects are the models (used projects) in a MagicDraw project. The main decomposition project is element **429f969a**. You need to load element **429f969a** and all their children. An element is represented in the JSONLD format. The attributes of the elements are in **kerml:esiData**. There will be an element named **UML Model**. The below code fragment shows the attributes of this element.

```text

```

Take the first **esiproject:EsiDataSection** from the feature's sections list, which is **b3e68e8e**. The excerpt of the **b3e68e8e** data is shown below.

```text

```

The first ID in the **rootElements** list of the data section will be the ID of the UML model root element in the resource. The root element should always be of the **uml:Package** type or derived from it.

##### Traversing to a MagicDraw model

Once a root project is retrieved, the whole hierarchy can be retrieved layer by layer. An element can be retrieved from */elements/{elementId}*. Child UUIDs of the resulting element are in **kerml:ownedElement**.

Multiple elements can be retrieved by POST to */elements*. In this case, you need to put UUID in the **uuid.txt****file to load elements.

```text

```

The command to load those elements is:

```bash

```

In most cases, loading multiple elements at once is substantially faster than loading them one by one.

##### Creating elements

To use REST API to create elements, you are required to have knowledge about UML models, especially how to set the owner. A combination of parent-child type yields differences in the owner attribute. For example, if you want to create a class under a class, you need to set the **UMLClass** attribute in the child class. For a class under a package, you need to set **owningPackage** in the child class. The easiest way to know which attribute is needed is to try creating it in MagicDraw and get it in REST API.

There are two URLs to create element(s), */resources/{resourceId}/elements* and */resources/{resourceId}/elements{elementId}*. Please note that the *elementId* in the latter form is not the parent. An Ecore is needed to create an element. It can be specified in the following ways:

- Specified from the *elementId* in */resources/{resourceId}/elements/*{elementId}**. This form is used in most cases, except from creating the first element in the project. The new element data type will be in the same namespace of this specified element. text
- Specified from kerml:nsURI .
- Specifying the whole Ecore in **kerml:ecore**. This mode is rarely used. text\r\n\r\n 
 <\/eClassifiers>\r\n<\/ecore:EPackage>\r\n"
}]]>

The following script creates an element:

```text

```

Multiple elements can be created at once by specifying the query parameter *batch=true* in the URL. The script is shown below:

```text

```

##### Applied stereotypes

Applied stereotypes stored in the **appliedStereotype** attribute are the arrays containing reference objects to stereotype objects. The code below shows an example of an applied stereotype.

```text

```

To apply the stereotype, set PATCH to the element you are working on, as shown below.

```text

```

The UUID **8dbdc804** is an ID of the applied stereotype whose content is applied via the following command where the UUID 8df9f306 is an ID of the element to which the stereotype is applied:

```text

```

##### Tagged values

Tagged values stored in the **taggedValue** attribute are the arrays containing reference objects to tagged value objects. The code below shows an example of an applied stereotype and tagged values.

```text

```

To add a tagged value, apply the stereotype to the target element first.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The model is incorrect if tagged values do not have their corresponding stereotypes.

To apply the tagged value, use the POST request with the following code:

```text

```

The command will be:

```bash

```

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d9535d3-0233-43b8-bc0a-8ee73b3a9802"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1043546914</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33f95720-5b7e-4e64-9806-bc2622df0c6f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1043546918</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b3b9f99-c035-42be-b34f-999c3f79cd72"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1043546912</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="652e211b-4665-4e43-97d3-36532c4f4f4b"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65a1429d-8504-4f53-98fa-311107f97241"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1043546917</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Teamwork Cloud operates at the EMF level, while MagicDraw operates at the UML level, which is on top of EMF. The best-known derived property is <em>owner</em>. The user is required to have knowledge about UML models to manipulate data. For example, an applied stereotype is saved as a hierarchy of <em>InstanceSpecification</em>, <em>Slot</em>, and <em>ValueSpecification</em>.</p><p>This section describes how to traverse into a UML model and create some elements in it.</p><h3>MagicDraw project</h3><p>In Teamwork Cloud terminology, a MagicDraw project is referred to as a <em>resource</em>. Although REST API can be used to create a Teamwork Cloud resource, it is only a bare project MagicDraw cannot read. A MagicDraw project is composed of many MagicDraw-specific meta-data such as project options, primary project, and used projects configuration. The best way to create a MagicDraw project is to create it by MagicDraw and submit it to the Teamwork Cloud server.</p><h3>MagicDraw primary project</h3><p>To traverse through a UML model, the primary project must be identified. The model data starts at the revision level. Issuing GET to <em>/revisions/{revisionId}</em> shows the first-level object in the revision. UUIDs of the first-level object are listed in <strong>rootObjectIDs</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56ba1f1d-55d3-42ed-8c75-d9773deaf9ca"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
	"commitType": "NORMAL",
    "branchID": "../..",
    "resourceID": "../../..",
    "@base": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/resources/4615e8fa-81e5-40e0-a51b-8496a48caf18/revisions/5/elements",
    "author": "Administrator",
    "@type": 
	[
    	"RDFSource",
    	"kerml:Revision"
    ],
    "pickedRevision": -1,
    "description": "Branch \"xx\" created",
    "@context": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/schemas/revision",
    "directParent": 3,
    "dependencies": [],
    "rootObjectIDs": 
	[
      	"429f969a-5c81-45f4-94af-8cf983f22950",
		"ec6060a3-f3d9-482b-93a3-32af9e19202c",
		"ca9a0235-f0f7-46b7-a142-e79a67c2d00d",     
		"f7c3ae92-af44-4dab-8163-a199ca05c006",
		"ba3d0700-1062-4baf-a1df-a55a4f31ce54",
		"0f14cd2d-2fd0-4523-950c-627d59e1a43d",
      	"7cd22dea-aaf8-4e08-bd67-5bd975c3f06a",
     	"af1042fa-8b1b-4cf2-bb7d-98dd1b881da3",
		"6d24e5e7-cdff-4e9e-85b8-28b3088f85b6",
		"243020e5-da6c-4896-b32a-fcba0e93ac8d",
		"f7449238-5cd1-41eb-9025-040210b02d93",
      	"4d2459a1-49dc-4eb7-aa82-9bbb4a76b038",
		"b242613d-957e-4aec-9333-e5938f50b2ab",  
		"9b953064-e422-4391-b7d9-43a2d4f14a32",
		"fc997cfd-23c5-4d0b-9953-06667dcde0dd",
		"29d9416b-ead5-4a9d-b530-b23de836f1b8",
		"7af3f24b-2da9-4b31-94b3-a87f15747296"
	],
	"createdDate": "1533051367",
	"ID": "",
    "artifacts": "artifacts"
},]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Among these entries, the first element with the <strong>esiproject:EsiProject</strong> type specifies the primary project named <strong>main decomposition project</strong>. Other <strong>esiproject:EsiProject</strong> objects are the models (used projects) in a MagicDraw project. The main decomposition project is element <strong>429f969a</strong>. You need to load element <strong>429f969a</strong> and all their children. An element is represented in the JSONLD format. The attributes of the elements are in <strong>kerml:esiData</strong>. There will be an element named <strong>UML Model</strong>. The below code fragment shows the attributes of this element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a231d404-e71f-45b7-961f-0acc426c1902"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA["kerml:esiData":
{
	"name": "UML Model",
    "namespace": "com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml_model",
	"project": 
	{
		"@id": "429f969a-5c81-45f4-94af-8cf983f22950"
	},
	"internalVersion": "1",
	"version": "17.0",
	"sections": 
	[
		{
			"@id": "b3e68e8e-2253-4726-8d05-d8f74fd0ba5a"
        }
	]
},]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Take the first <strong>esiproject:EsiDataSection</strong> from the feature's sections list, which is <strong>b3e68e8e</strong>. The excerpt of the <strong>b3e68e8e</strong> data is shown below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60584186-0d26-423e-a1e2-d829ed92b6d2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
	"kerml:name": "model",
	"@base": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/resources/4615e8fa-81e5-40e0-a51b-8496a48caf18/revisions/5/elements",
	"kerml:nsURI": "http://www.nomagic.com/ns/magicdraw/esiproject/1.0",
	"@type": "esiproject:EsiDataSection",
    "kerml:owner": 
	{
		"@id": "429f969a-5c81-45f4-94af-8cf983f22950"
    },
    "kerml:revision": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/resources/4615e8fa-81e5-40e0-a51b-8496a48caf18/revisions/5",
    "@context": 
	{
		"esiproject:EsiDataSection": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/schema/esiproject/2014345/EsiDataSection",
		"kerml": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/schema/kerml/20140325"
    },
    "kerml:ownedElement": [],
    "kerml:modifiedTime": "20180731223607ICT",
    "kerml:esiData": 
	{
		"featuredBy": 
		{
			"@id": "c9256728-4617-4097-8e9e-dc63e2823bf8"
		},
		"rootElements":
		[
			"ca9a0235-f0f7-46b7-a142-e79a67c2d00d"
		],
		"name": "model",
      	"project":
		{
			"@id": "429f969a-5c81-45f4-94af-8cf983f22950"
      	},
		"properties": []
	},
	"kerml:resource": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/resources/4615e8fa-81e5-40e0-a51b-8496a48caf18",
    "kerml:esiID": "b3e68e8e-2253-4726-8d05-d8f74fd0ba5a",
	"@id": "#b3e68e8e-2253-4726-8d05-d8f74fd0ba5a"
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The first ID in the <strong>rootElements</strong> list of the data section will be the ID of the UML model root element in the resource. The root element should always be of the <strong>uml:Package</strong> type or derived from it.</p><h3>Traversing to a MagicDraw model</h3><p>Once a root project is retrieved, the whole hierarchy can be retrieved layer by layer. An element can be retrieved from <em>/elements/{elementId}</em>. Child UUIDs of the resulting element are in <strong>kerml:ownedElement</strong>.</p><p>Multiple elements can be retrieved by POST to <em>/elements</em>. In this case, you need to put UUID in the <strong>uuid.txt</strong><em> </em>file to load elements.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33153f09-c2b7-48f5-a3ed-31c4b49e6b7e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[4eeb2e6d-9cbc-4f59-9d74-69263e52ba54,520bc74d-b5b4-40d5-87cb-9d0b8aba5d2e,7a057ae7-6281-462d-9dcd-de9931633f5c,a6212656-5ad1-4de6-a47f-db656c2c25fd]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The command to load those elements is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a018be71-340d-4466-a56e-6e1bb98c8511"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[curl -v -H "Content-Type: text/plain" -X POST -s --cookie cookie.txt --insecure -d @uuid.txt --insecure "https://server:8111/osmc/resources/${projectId}/elements"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In most cases, loading multiple elements at once is substantially faster than loading them one by one.</p><h3>Creating elements</h3><p>To use REST API to create elements, you are required to have knowledge about UML models, especially how to set the owner. A combination of parent-child type yields differences in the owner attribute. For example, if you want to create a class under a class, you need to set the <strong>UMLClass</strong> attribute in the child class. For a class under a package, you need to set <strong>owningPackage</strong> in the child class. The easiest way to know which attribute is needed is to try creating it in MagicDraw and get it in REST API.</p><p>There are two URLs to create element(s), <em>/resources/{resourceId}/elements</em> and <em>/resources/{resourceId}/elements{elementId}</em>. Please note that the <em>elementId</em> in the latter form is not the parent. An Ecore is needed to create an element. It can be specified in the following ways:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specified from the <em>elementId</em> in <em>/resources/{resourceId}/elements/<em>{elementId}</em></em>. This form is used in most cases, except from creating the first element in the project. The new element data type will be in the same namespace of this specified element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cda4bcb3-e1df-42bb-b58f-96a53a031c9f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
	"@type": "uml:Class",
	"kerml:nsURI": "http://www.nomagic.com/magicdraw/UML/2.5.1",
	"kerml:esiData": 
	{
		"owningPackage": 
		{
			"@id": "757be712-f397-404d-a5ff-b97567eb240f"
        },
        "name": "c3"
    }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Specified from <strong>kerml:nsURI</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specifying the whole Ecore in <strong>kerml:ecore</strong>. This mode is rarely used.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="352b1dcd-d2c6-4040-970f-2dde4c18d7ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
	"@type": "ikml:Container",
	"kerml:esiData": 
	{
		"name": "txdhdhdhd",
		"uri": "http://www.chula.ac.th",
    },
	"kerml:ecore": "<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\r\n<ecore:EPackage xmi:version=\"2.0\" xmlns:xmi=\"http://www.omg.org/XMI\" 
	xmlns:xsi=\"http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance\"\r\n
	xmlns:ecore=\"http://www.eclipse.org/emf/2002/Ecore\"
	name=\"ikml\" nsURI=\"http://www.nomagic.com/ikml/1.0\"
	nsPrefix=\"ikml\">\r\n 
	<eClassifiers xsi:type=\"ecore:EClass\" name=\"Element\" abstract=\"true\"><\/eClassifiers>\r\n<\/ecore:EPackage>\r\n"
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target">The following script creates an element:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abf6e33b-2609-416e-b360-e1c3d88c33f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cat file.txt | curl -v -H "Content-Type: application/ld+json" -X POST -s --cookie cookie.txt --insecure -d @-https://server:8111/osmc/resources/${projectId}/elements]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Multiple elements can be created at once by specifying the query parameter <em>batch=true</em> in the URL. The script is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b654223-c3a5-495c-baa9-8180037d07db"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cat file.txt | curl -v -H "Content-Type: application/ld+json" -X POST -s --cookie cookie.txt --insecure -d @-"https://server:8111/osmc/resources/${projectId}/elements/${elementId}?batch=true"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Applied stereotypes</h3><p>Applied stereotypes stored in the <strong>appliedStereotype</strong> attribute are the arrays containing reference objects to stereotype objects. The code below shows an example of an applied stereotype.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c58e346-18a9-4370-b75d-d3dac640b749"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "@id": "#bdeffbc9-daae-407c-8663-d3b1a0ed6077",
	"kerml:esiID": "bdeffbc9-daae-407c-8663-d3b1a0ed6077",
	"@type": "uml:Class",
	"kerml:esiData": {
		"ID": "_2021x_2_3360135_1630482378802_586147_17",
		"mdExtensions": [],
		"_representationText": null,
		"taggedValue": [
			{
				"@id": "edaa1dd8-583d-4a8b-8974-3111aef8d47b"
            }
		],
		"appliedStereotype": [
			{
				"@id": "8dbdc804-5eed-424d-9812-28e183a8dfdc"
            }
		]
	}
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To apply the stereotype, set PATCH to the element you are working on, as shown below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e88402a-1b6d-42d0-8124-a5c56c4611c9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
	"@context": {
		"@base": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/workspaces/59068af0-a7ae-4663-
b972-3c58a2fde23f/resources/b0585059-c4e3-4d82-90d9-c5386045bb2e/branches/
5dfb978f-0984-47ea-aaec-bdbb5a945687/elements/",
		"kerml": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/schema/kerml/20140325",
		"uml:Class": "http://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/schema/uml/2014345/Class"
	},
	"kerml:esiData": {
		"appliedStereotype": [
			{
				"@id": "8dbdc804-5eed-424d-9812-28e183a8dfdc"
            }
		]
    }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The UUID <strong>8dbdc804</strong> is an ID of the applied stereotype whose content is applied via the following command where the UUID 8df9f306 is an ID of the element to which the stereotype is applied:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a519b65-4594-4d3b-a2f9-5bf201fa4a7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$ curl -v -H "Content-Type: application/ld+json" -X POST -s -c ..\get\cookie.txt -b
..\get\cookie.txt --insecure -d @- https://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/resources/2663059e-8ef5-4f13-97b6-864a0353d2d0/elements/8df9f306-c4dc-41fb-ac9f-4a87fe71610a]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Tagged values</h3><p>Tagged values stored in the <strong>taggedValue</strong> attribute are the arrays containing reference objects to tagged value objects. The code below shows an example of an applied stereotype and tagged values.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4790701f-dcda-46eb-9c21-43ede212c123"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
	"@id": "#bdeffbc9-daae-407c-8663-d3b1a0ed6077",
	"kerml:esiID": "bdeffbc9-daae-407c-8663-d3b1a0ed6077",
	"@type": "uml:Class",
	"kerml:esiData": {
		"ID": "_2021x_2_3360135_1630482378802_586147_17",
        "mdExtensions": [],
		"_representationText": null,
		"taggedValue": [
			{
				"@id": "d9388ec6-ddca-4c18-9a5c-409d8649731f"
            },
            {
				"@id": "edaa1dd8-583d-4a8b-8974-3111aef8d47b"
            }
		],
		"appliedStereotype": [
			{
				"@id": "8dbdc804-5eed-424d-9812-28e183a8dfdc"
            }
        ]
    }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To add a tagged value, apply the stereotype to the target element first.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="048b897c-21c3-47d3-a4d2-a1796364e739"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The model is incorrect if tagged values do not have their corresponding stereotypes.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To apply the tagged value, use the POST request with the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f2f7a78-453b-49dc-a35d-26fccf8dadc9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
	"@type": "uml:StringTaggedValue",
	"kerml:esiData": {
		"tagDefinition": {
			"@id": "657a3511-d012-4134-af5d-411a6df78745"
        },
		"taggedValueOwner": {
			"@id": "515073dd-fe25-4327-bfc2-6466375a50f7"
        },
		"value": [
			"somevaluefromrest"
        ]
    }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The command will be:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9e2fd526-78f4-4ca3-bc53-f94b7925d50e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[curl -v -H "Content-Type: application/ld+json" -X POST -s -c ..\get\cookie.txt -b ..\get\cookie.txt
--insecure -d @- https://127.0.0.1:8111/osmc/resources/2663059e-8ef5-4f13-97b6-864a0353d2d0/elements/8df9f306-c4dc-41fb-ac9f-4a87fe71610a]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9dbb4036-6351-4e7a-a701-2b65f4490680"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1043575942</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) REST APIs" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) General convention" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Authentication" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) MagicDraw-specific extensions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989230 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00153: (2026x Refresh1) Model Modification

- page_id: `303989230`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989230/2026x+Refresh1+Model+Modification
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:26.666+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section describes how to safely modify using the Open API.

All model changes must be executed within a controlled context to ensure:

- Consistency of the model
- Proper command tracking
- Support for undo/redo operations

The platform provides two approaches for performing model modifications:

- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Editing API' space=''] (recommended)
- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API' space=''] (lower-level control)

#### **Model Modification Concepts**

Before modifying a model, it is important to understand the core concepts:

- Commands Each modification is recorded as a command for undo/redo
- Command History Tracks executed changes and enables rollback
- Sessions Define the scope in which model changes are applied

These mechanisms ensure that all changes are safe, traceable, and reversible.

#### **Choosing an API**

##### Use the Editing API (Recommended)

- Automatically manages commands and command history
- Simpler and safer to use
- Best for most plugin and automation scenarios

See: **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Editing API' space='']**

##### Use SessionManager (Advanced)

- Provides manual control over sessions
- Requires explicit session lifecycle management
- Useful for legacy integrations or specialized workflows

See: **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API' space='']**

#### **Example Comparison**

```java
{
    // model changes here
});
 
// Manual approach (SessionManager)
SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Edit");
try {
    // model changes here
} finally {
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);
}]]>
```

#### **Key Takeaways**

- All model changes must occur within a controlled execution context
- Prefer the Editing API for most use cases
- Use SessionManager only when you need explicit control

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This section describes how to safely modify using the Open API.</p><p>All model changes must be executed within a controlled context to ensure:</p><ul><li data-uuid="204a1b0d-8f5c-42e1-8ab3-47826cb1ee86">Consistency of the model</li><li data-uuid="e7ea88ff-edf8-4e19-a0cf-78879216885d">Proper command tracking</li><li data-uuid="78b9ebd0-2208-4725-bd5e-d8084b7f9f4c">Support for undo/redo operations</li></ul><p>The platform provides two approaches for performing model modifications:</p><ul><li data-uuid="555f0f75-7ede-4448-b5c9-293353161ff8">The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Editing API" /></ac:link></strong> (recommended)</li><li data-uuid="8a6faa8f-8170-41f3-a288-eff55e5fcfd7">The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API" /></ac:link></strong> (lower-level control)</li></ul><h2><strong>Model Modification Concepts</strong></h2><p>Before modifying a model, it is important to understand the core concepts:</p><ul><li data-uuid="6e52e3ef-58a9-4c4a-8acf-ddbb556d04c9"><strong>Commands</strong><br />Each modification is recorded as a command for undo/redo</li><li data-uuid="f8a6fe49-bb66-4abe-8686-40f2bfb09d2e"><strong>Command History</strong><br />Tracks executed changes and enables rollback</li><li data-uuid="aa6f542a-fc4a-44d3-80a8-a51b427a1272"><strong>Sessions</strong><br />Define the scope in which model changes are applied</li></ul><p>These mechanisms ensure that all changes are safe, traceable, and reversible.</p><h2><strong>Choosing an API</strong></h2><h3>Use the Editing API (Recommended)</h3><ul><li data-uuid="a3809323-e0af-43dc-bcc0-3921e79ce177">Automatically manages commands and command history</li><li data-uuid="87ae7100-1125-437c-b308-9033bea55216">Simpler and safer to use</li><li data-uuid="d637413b-5c77-449f-a66d-93d601d6e759">Best for most plugin and automation scenarios</li></ul><p>See: <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Editing API" /></ac:link></strong></p><h3>Use SessionManager (Advanced)</h3><ul><li data-uuid="30b5f33d-d8ed-4d95-8773-761edab7bcc2">Provides manual control over sessions</li><li data-uuid="3124b350-1d7b-4727-94c6-1be7503a42a4">Requires explicit session lifecycle management</li><li data-uuid="967ab4d7-c272-4bef-83de-ab7bdfa68e49">Useful for legacy integrations or specialized workflows</li></ul><p>See: <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API" /></ac:link></strong></p><h2><strong>Example Comparison</strong></h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb723b17-8711-4e6f-955d-d4733302f21a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.editing.Editing;
import com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.SessionManager;

Editing editing = Editing.getInstance(project);

// Recommended approach (Editing API)
editing.execute("Create element", () -> {
    // model changes here
});
 
// Manual approach (SessionManager)
SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Edit");
try {
    // model changes here
} finally {
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Key Takeaways</strong></h2><ul><li data-uuid="c665fedb-401c-4c70-9371-0ca2697abe01">All model changes must occur within a controlled execution context</li><li data-uuid="ec42f09d-49c7-421b-9d10-546ef63a3862">Prefer the <strong>Editing API</strong> for most use cases</li><li data-uuid="996c2c82-75f9-46fb-831a-965763a01d11">Use <strong>SessionManager</strong> only when you need explicit control</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989544 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00154: (2026x Refresh1) Model Traversal and Visitor Pattern

- page_id: `303989544`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989544/2026x+Refresh1+Model+Traversal+and+Visitor+Pattern
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:41.107+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 > (2026x Refresh1) Working With Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor provides a structured, extensible mechanism for traversing SysML model hierarchies. It implements the classic Visitor Pattern, allowing developers to perform:

- Model validation
- Structural and semantic analysis
- Automated transformations
- Metrics extraction
- Reporting and documentation generation

Traversal is driven by the model elements themselves through their accept(visitor) method, ensuring type‑safe dispatch to the correct visit<ClassName> method.

### **1. How Visitor Dispatch Works**

Every SysML model element implements an accept method:

This means:

- The element chooses the correct visit<ClassName> method.
- You never manually dispatch based on type.
- Traversal is consistent and extensible.

### **2. Using SysMLVisitorContext**

SysMLVisitorContext is the standard context object used during traversal. It provides: boolean isAlreadyVisited(int classID); void addToVisited(int classId);

Each model element type provides its own integer identifier. Open API users should not work with the visitor context. All tracking is done in internal implementation.

### **3. Creating a Custom Visitor**

To implement custom traversal logic, extend: SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor<SysMLVisitorContext>

```java
{
	@Override
	public void visitAcceptActionUsage(AcceptActionUsage element, SysMLVisitorContext context)
	{
		System.out.println("Visiting Package: " + element.getName());
		SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor.super.visitAcceptActionUsage(element, context);

	}

	@Override
	public void visitActionDefinition(ActionDefinition element, SysMLVisitorContext context)
	{
		System.out.println("Visiting ActionDefinition : " + element.getName());
		SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor.super.visitActionDefinition(element, context);
	}
}]]>
```

### **4. Traversing a Model**

To traverse a SysML model:

1. Create your visitor.
2. Call accept(visitor) on the element. java

```text

```

How Visitor Dispatch Works</strong></h1><p>Every SysML model element implements an accept method:</p><p>This means:</p><ul><li data-uuid="6e46aa88-4dda-4ca1-a1c3-d453ae2af865"><p>The element chooses the correct visit&lt;ClassName&gt; method.</p></li><li data-uuid="01901653-e7e5-4364-80a0-617b76d6c590"><p>You never manually dispatch based on type.</p></li><li data-uuid="6ea98b10-8246-42a8-a0b5-248c099f0d01"><p>Traversal is consistent and extensible.</p></li></ul><h1><strong>2. Using SysMLVisitorContext</strong></h1><p>SysMLVisitorContext is the standard context object used during traversal. It provides: boolean isAlreadyVisited(int classID); void addToVisited(int classId);</p><p>Each model element type provides its own integer identifier. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Open API users should not work with the visitor context. All tracking is done in internal implementation.</span></p><h1><strong>3. Creating a Custom Visitor</strong></h1><p>To implement custom traversal logic, extend: SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor&lt;SysMLVisitorContext&gt;</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8bb3cf6-587c-4b51-b32c-a84add037676"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor;
````html
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.SysMLVisitorContext;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.AcceptActionUsage;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.ActionDefinition;
public class MyCustomVisitor implements SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor<SysMLVisitorContext>
{
	@Override
	public void visitAcceptActionUsage(AcceptActionUsage element, SysMLVisitorContext context)
	{
		System.out.println("Visiting Package: " + element.getName());
		SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor.super.visitAcceptActionUsage(element, context);

	}

	@Override
	public void visitActionDefinition(ActionDefinition element, SysMLVisitorContext context)
	{
		System.out.println("Visiting ActionDefinition : " + element.getName());
		SysMLModelHierarchyVisitor.super.visitActionDefinition(element, context);
	}
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h1><strong>4. Traversing a Model</strong></h1><p>To traverse a SysML model:</p><ol><li data-uuid="b7376704-e2ed-40ff-a2ae-b816d51fd37b"><p>Create your visitor.</p></li><li data-uuid="14cb2649-2706-46a7-9a3b-5a83092bd0de"><p>Call accept(visitor) on the element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="273611a8-2554-419d-a49b-91e6e23e19b6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Package;

public class Main
{
	public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception
	{
		MyCustomVisitor myVisitor = new MyCustomVisitor();
		Package root = ..;
		for (Element element : root.getOwnedElement())
		{
			element.accept(myVisitor);
		}
		System.out.println("Traversal complete.");
	}
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><pre><br /></pre><p><br /></p><p><span> </span></p>
````

## PAGE 00155: (2026x Refresh1) Model traversing, Visitor pattern

- page_id: `303989008`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989008/2026x+Refresh1+Model+traversing+Visitor+pattern
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:12.501+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Here is an example of how to collect all children from the *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element* and avoid recursion using a simple *for*cycle:

```java
children = new ArrayList(); 
    Element current = ... 
    children.add(current);
    // if the current element has children, list will be increased. 
    for (int i = 0; i < children.size(); i++)
    {
        current = children.get(i);
        // add all children into end of this list, so it emulates a recursion. 
        children.addAll(current.getOwnedElement());
    }]]>
```

It is highly recommended to use more advanced collecting methods from *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Finder* utility:

- byScope()
- byType()
- byTypeRecursively()

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989024 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00156: (2026x Refresh1) ModelHierarchyVisitor

- page_id: `303989024`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989024/2026x+Refresh1+ModelHierarchyVisitor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:13.382+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with UML model > (2026x Refresh1) Model traversing, Visitor pattern
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The *com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelHierarchyVisitor* is an enhanced implementation of the *Visitor*pattern and a subclass of *com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelVisitor*, used for visiting model elements.

Every *visit<element_metatype>*method calls a *visit<element_metatype>*method for a super type.

Model elements *visit…*methods have an additional context parameter of the *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.reflect.VisitorContext* type. The visitor context is used to track what super type *visit<element_metatype> m*ethods were already visited (to avoid multiple visits because some model elements have a multiple inheritance). Open API users should not work with the visitor context. All tracking is done in internal implementation.

For example, you can put some code into *com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelHierarchyVisitor.visitClassifier(**Classifier)***and it will be executed for all subclasses of a *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Classifier*.

```java
it = root.getOwnedElement().iterator();
    while (it.hasNext())
    {
        it.next().accept(myVisitor);
    }]]>
```

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>\openapi\examples\statistics*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelHierarchyVisitor</em> is an enhanced implementation of the <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Visitor </em>pattern and a subclass of <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelVisitor</em>, used for visiting model elements. </p><p>Every <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">visit&lt;element_metatype&gt; </em>method calls a <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">visit&lt;element_metatype&gt; </em>method for a super type.</p><p>Model elements <em>visit… </em>methods have an additional context parameter of the <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.reflect.VisitorContext</em> type. The visitor context is used to track what super type <em>visit&lt;element_metatype&gt; m</em>ethods were already visited (to avoid multiple visits because some model elements have a multiple inheritance). Open API users should not work with the visitor context. All tracking is done in internal implementation.</p><p><br /></p><p>For example, you can put some code into <em>com.nomagic.uml2.impl.ModelHierarchyVisitor.visitClassifier(</em><em>Classifier)</em><em> </em>and it will be executed for all subclasses of a <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Classifier</em>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7085da27-f3ae-415f-9197-30427949c8f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ModelVisitor myVisitor = new ModelHierarchyVisitor()
    {
        public void visitClassifier(Classifier element, VisitorContext context)
        {
          	//this is my classifier
        }
    };
    Project project = ...;
    Package root = project.getPrimaryModel(); 
    Iterator<Element> it = root.getOwnedElement().iterator();
    while (it.hasNext())
    {
        it.next().accept(myVisitor);
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\statistics</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303988912 space=DEVG version=2 -->
## PAGE 00157: (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide

- page_id: `303988912`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303988912/2026x+Refresh1+Modeling+Tools+Developer+Guide
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T14:54:37.838+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the generated javadoc ([https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/)), you will find a detailed description of classes, their attributes, and operations. JavaDocs are located in *<modeling tool installation directory>\openapi\docs*.

This guide describes the Open Java API of MagicDraw or a Cameo Suite product, such as Cameo Systems Modeler, and provides instructions on how to implement custom plugins, add actions to the menus or toolbars, change UML model elements as well as create new patterns. Though MagicDraw is used in examples mainly, other modeling tools, since they are based on the MagicDraw modeling platform, perform the same.

The program core code and used libraries are packaged in jars files that are located in *<modeling tool installation directory>\lib* (and its subdirectories).

Some plugins also exposes Open API. Plugins libraries can be located in *<modeling tool installation directory>\plugins\<plugin_name>*

The code can be divided into the following three API scope groups:

- OpenAPI – a code for public usage, stable through builds and versions.
- InternalAPI – a code for the internal NoMagic usage only, may change through builds and versions without any restrictions.
- NotAPI – not an API code, it may change in each build.

Java annotations are used to distinguish which API scope in the provided code belongs to. Annotations of the type (class, interface, enum) declaration:

| Annotation | API Scope | Scope applies to |
| --- | --- | --- |
| @OpenApiAll | OpenAPI | The annotated type declaration and all declared members, constructors. |
| @OpenApi | OpenAPI | The annotated type declaration only. Each declared member, constructor is annotated explicitly with the @OpenApi or @InternalApi annotation (see the following table). |
| @InternalApi | InternalAPI | The annotated type declaration and all declared members, constructors. |
| @NotApi | NotAPI | The annotated type declaration and all declared members, constructors. |

Annotations of the member, constructor declaration of the @OpenApi type:

| Annotation | API Scope | Scope applies to |
| --- | --- | --- |
| @OpenApi | OpenAPI | The annotated declared member or constructor. |
| @InternalApi | InternalAPI | The annotated declared member or constructor. |

Only code from the OpenAPI scope should be used to extend the modeling tool. All the InternalAPI and NotAPI scope code are additionally annotated with the *@Deprecated* annotation. This allows seeing the illegal API usage in all modern IDEs without an additional configuration.

We provide a set of plugin samples in *<*modeling tool installation directory*>\openapi\examples*. By using these examples, you can find out on how to use an OpenAPI.

In the generated javadoc, you will find the detailed description of classes, their attributes, and operations. JavaDocs are located in *<*modeling tool installation directory*>\openapi\docs*.

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To move a symbol on the diagram to another location, you can use the following method:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a56154ac-0627-4845-9653-ee18d51a0f3a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    PresentationElementManager.getInstance().movePresentationElement(PresentationElement view, Point location)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17822cd1-f81b-4e4a-953a-f7e105178d09"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    PresentationElement view = ...; 
    SessionsManager.getInstance().createSession("Move symbol"); 
    PresentationsElementsManager.getInstance().movePresentationElement(view, new Point(50, 50));
    SessionsManager.getInstance().closeSession();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Similarly, a group of symbols can be moved by using the following method:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab01b1f8-334a-42da-9009-3d61172a088e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    movePresentationElements(List<PresentationElement> views, Point location)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\symbolmove</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b483f3d-c1a6-45f7-9612-a04919e10de5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To move a symbol on the diagram to another location and to a different parent, you can use the following method:

```java

```

```java

```

Similarly, a group of symbols can be moved by using the following method:

```java
views, PresentationElement parent, Point location)]]>
```
