````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To move a symbol on the diagram to another location and to a different parent, you can use the following method:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ef2ca9ed-13bd-4877-b0df-6e281fe267d6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	movePresentationElement(PresentationElement view, PresentationElement parent, Point location)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93a80233-ca8f-4fcc-8cee-f6f03b668e49"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    PresentationElement view = ...; 
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession("Move symbol"); 
    PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().movePresentationElement(view, newParent, new Point(50, 50)); 
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Similarly, a group of symbols can be moved by using the following method:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d4f9e9de-480e-405f-998a-9c3f94b2c46d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    movePresentationElements(List<PresentationElement> views, PresentationElement parent, Point location)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\symbolmove</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="299ed20a-ddb8-4bab-a2d1-10891a4c1360"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SessionManager API" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989149 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00160: (2026x Refresh1) Multi-threading

- page_id: `303989149`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989149/2026x+Refresh1+Multi-threading
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:23.060+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) General
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The majority of the application code (including OpenAPI) is not thread safe. Only the UML model is thread safe for reading (the UML model can be queried safely by concurrent threads), but still the UML model writing (modification) is not thread safe.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Running action with progress' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The majority of the application code (including OpenAPI) is not thread safe. Only the UML model is thread safe for reading (the UML model can be queried safely by concurrent threads), but still the UML model writing (modification) is not thread safe.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="484fea0b-5751-4dfb-9861-690ecfc262e8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="830e6398-0ff5-46dd-b2fb-15df811ffd40" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Running action with progress" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989559 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00161: (2026x Refresh1) Multiplicities Utility Class

- page_id: `303989559`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989559/2026x+Refresh1+Multiplicities+Utility+Class
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:41.989+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 > (2026x Refresh1) Working With Model > (2026x Refresh1) Utilities and Helpers
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In KerML, `[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Multiplicity](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Multiplicity.html)` is a subtype of `[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Type.html)` that constrains how many values a `[Feature](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Feature.html)` or `[Type](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Type.html)` may hold.

The most commonly used concrete subtype is `[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.MultiplicityRange](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/MultiplicityRange.html)`, which defines explicit lower and upper bounds corresponding to standard SysML v2 notation such as:

- 0..1
- 1
- 1..*
- 0..* (default)

#### **Overview**

`[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Multiplicities](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Multiplicities.html)` is the central utility class for all programmatic multiplicity operations.

Use this class exclusively when:

- Creating multiplicities
- Reading multiplicities
- Modifying multiplicities
- Validating multiplicity expressions

#### **Setting Multiplicity on a Type**

The `[setMultiplicity](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Multiplicities.html#setMultiplicity(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type,java.lang.String))` method:

- Parses the provided string representation
- Creates or replaces the owned [Multiplicity](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Multiplicity.html)
- Sets both lower and upper bounds

```java

```

#### **Getting Multiplicity from a Type**

The [*getOwnedOrInheritedMultiplicity*](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Multiplicities.html#getOwnedOrInheritedMultiplicity(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type))method:

- Returns the multiplicity defined on the type
- Falls back to inherited multiplicity through the specialization chain
- Returns null only if no multiplicity is defined anywhere

**Important:**A null value indicates the implicit default multiplicity 0..*.

```java

```

#### **Reading and Modifying an Existing Multiplicity**

When working with multiplicities, it is important to distinguish between:

- Explicit multiplicity (authored on the element)
- Implicit multiplicity (derived default)

```java

```

#### **Regex Constants**

The `[Multiplicities](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Multiplicities.html)` class exposes reusable regex patterns for validating multiplicity strings:

- `[LOWER_UPPER_MULTIPLICITY_REGEX](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Multiplicities.html#LOWER_UPPER_MULTIPLICITY_REGEX)` Matches range expressions such as:
  - 0..1
  - 1..10
  - 0..*
- `[SINGLE_MULTIPLICITY_REGEX](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Multiplicities.html#SINGLE_MULTIPLICITY_REGEX)` Matches scalar values:
  - 1
  - 0
  - *

(interpreted as 1..1, 0..0, or 0..* depending on context)

These constants should be reused for UI validation and parsing. Do not reimplement them — edge cases (e.g., escaped dots, *) are already handled.

```java

```

#### **Key Takeaways**

- Always use [Multiplicities](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Multiplicities.html) utility class — do not manipulate multiplicities manually
- null multiplicity means implicit 0..*
- Never modify implicit multiplicities in place — create explicit overrides
- Use the provided regex constants for validation instead of custom patterns

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Type.html" rel="nofollow">Type</a></code> may hold.</p><p>The most commonly used concrete subtype is <code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/MultiplicityRange.html" rel="nofollow">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.MultiplicityRange</a></code>, which defines explicit lower and upper bounds corresponding to standard SysML v2 notation such as:</p><ul><li>0..1</li><li>1</li><li>1..*</li><li>0..* (default)</li></ul><h2><strong>Overview</strong></h2><p><code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Multiplicities.html" rel="nofollow">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Multiplicities</a></code> is the central utility class for all programmatic multiplicity operations.</p><p>Use this class exclusively when:</p><ul><li>Creating multiplicities</li><li>Reading multiplicities</li><li>Modifying multiplicities</li><li>Validating multiplicity expressions</li></ul><h2><strong>Setting Multiplicity on a Type</strong></h2><p>The <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Multiplicities.html#setMultiplicity(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type,java.lang.String)">setMultiplicity</a></code> method:</p><ul><li>Parses the provided string representation</li><li>Creates or replaces the owned <code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Multiplicity.html" rel="nofollow">Multiplicity</a></code></li><li>Sets both lower and upper bounds</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1986f193-a18a-4232-8954-55b47f810df0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Multiplicities;

Multiplicities.setMultiplicity(tirePart, "1..*");]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Getting Multiplicity from a Type</strong></h2><p>The <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Multiplicities.html#getOwnedOrInheritedMultiplicity(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Type)"><em>getOwnedOrInheritedMultiplicity</em> </a>method:</p><ul><li data-uuid="5aec249a-e24f-4a37-a988-871be5b5e0c1">Returns the multiplicity defined on the type</li><li data-uuid="d6683f38-95c6-4aee-8fa0-d59c67d29f72">Falls back to inherited multiplicity through the specialization chain</li><li data-uuid="4cb98fa1-6c5b-4891-b287-d6d807eb1de7">Returns null only if no multiplicity is defined anywhere</li></ul><p><strong>Important: </strong>A null value indicates the implicit default multiplicity 0..*.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de117bf7-b93e-49b3-adb9-dc177749c56c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Multiplicities;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Multiplicity;

Multiplicity m = Multiplicities.getOwnedOrInheritedMultiplicity(wheelFeature);

if (m == null) {
    // No explicit multiplicity anywhere in the hierarchy — implicit 0..*
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Reading and Modifying an Existing Multiplicity</strong></h2><p>When working with multiplicities, it is important to distinguish between:</p><ul><li data-uuid="7215bae3-2949-480b-9f51-433f96bf0bc2">Explicit multiplicity (authored on the element)</li><li data-uuid="57399d20-dfde-4e64-867b-aceb2a9e226a">Implicit multiplicity (derived default)</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88eb9452-f0e9-4a8e-8475-27af31ba7bdc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Multiplicities;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Multiplicity;

Feature feature = ...;

Multiplicity m = Multiplicities.getOwnedOrInheritedMultiplicity(feature);

if (m == null) {
	// No authored multiplicity — implicit 0..* applies
    // Create an explicit multiplicity if modification is needed
    Multiplicities.setMultiplicity(feature, "1..*");

} else if (Multiplicities.isImplicitMultiplicity(m)) {
    // Derived default — do not modify in place
    // Override by creating an explicit multiplicity
    Multiplicities.setMultiplicity(feature, "1..*");
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Regex Constants</strong></h2><p>The <code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Multiplicities.html" rel="nofollow">Multiplicities</a></code> class exposes reusable regex patterns for validating multiplicity strings:</p><ul><li data-uuid="265b1950-c779-4c78-a180-19b7dd160cde"><p><code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Multiplicities.html#LOWER_UPPER_MULTIPLICITY_REGEX" rel="nofollow">LOWER_UPPER_MULTIPLICITY_REGEX</a></code> Matches range expressions such as:</p><ul><li>0..1</li><li>1..10</li><li>0..*</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="32b653b1-1fbd-4afe-b547-656985c066f2"><p><code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Multiplicities.html#SINGLE_MULTIPLICITY_REGEX" rel="nofollow">SINGLE_MULTIPLICITY_REGEX</a></code> Matches scalar values:</p><ul><li>1</li><li>0</li><li>*</li></ul></li></ul><p>(interpreted as 1..1, 0..0, or 0..* depending on context)</p><p>These constants should be reused for UI validation and parsing. Do not reimplement them — edge cases (e.g., escaped dots, *) are already handled.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4fe98b70-c91d-424c-b5c8-6273fcc5c92e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Multiplicities;

if (userInput.matches(Multiplicities.LOWER_UPPER_MULTIPLICITY_REGEX)) {
    Multiplicities.setMultiplicity(feature, userInput);
} else if (userInput.matches(Multiplicities.SINGLE_MULTIPLICITY_REGEX)) {
    Multiplicities.setMultiplicity(feature, userInput);
} else {
    //report Invalid Multiplicity
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Key Takeaways</strong></h2><ul><li data-uuid="f92ba82a-807c-4bdb-bde5-a3e7f3b8b151">Always use <code><a class="external-link" href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Multiplicities.html" rel="nofollow">Multiplicities</a></code> utility class — do not manipulate multiplicities manually</li><li data-uuid="0ae25320-753b-4d20-bfff-4aeb9fd3ea14"><code>null</code> multiplicity means implicit 0..*</li><li data-uuid="14fc8879-46f3-4257-871c-85e20566b23b">Never modify implicit multiplicities in place — create explicit overrides</li><li data-uuid="f96e07aa-d49b-478b-8d6b-d517cd7ea6e5">Use the provided regex constants for validation instead of custom patterns</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989528 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00162: (2026x Refresh1) New Model Element Creation

- page_id: `303989528`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989528/2026x+Refresh1+New+Model+Element+Creation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:40.320+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 > (2026x Refresh1) Working With Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the `[com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.ElementsFactory](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/ElementsFactory.html)` class to create new SysML v2 model elements.

The factory provides type-specific creation methods in the form: *elementsFactory.create<ModelElementType>()*

Each call instantiates a new element of the given type. Newly created elements are not automatically placed in the model—you must explicitly assign ownership to integrate them into the model structure.

#### **Obtaining the Factory**

Before creating elements, obtain an instance of `[ElementsFactory](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/ElementsFactory.html)` for the current project:

```java

```

Alternatively, if you already have an existing model element, you can resolve the factory from it:

```java

```

#### **Example: Creating Elements**

```java

```

#### **Key Points**

- The factory is project-scoped — always obtain it from the correct project or element.
- create<Type>() methods only instantiate elements ; they do not place them in the model.
- To make an element part of the model, you must assign ownership (for example, using setOwner(...) ).
- Ownership operations may create underlying relationships automatically (for example, memberships), depending on the metamodel semantics.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Use the <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/ElementsFactory.html">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.ElementsFactory</a></code> class to create new SysML v2 model elements.</p><p>The factory provides type-specific creation methods in the form: <em>elementsFactory.create&lt;ModelElementType&gt;()</em></p><p>Each call instantiates a new element of the given type. Newly created elements are not automatically placed in the model—you must explicitly assign ownership to integrate them into the model structure.</p><h2><strong>Obtaining the Factory</strong></h2><p>Before creating elements, obtain an instance of <code><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/ElementsFactory.html">ElementsFactory</a></code> for the current project:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8159ac9a-4e8f-4454-a49f-9be593cdcc63"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.ElementsFactory;

Project project = ...;
ElementsFactory elementsFactory = ElementsFactory.get(project);  ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />Alternatively, if you already have an existing model element, you can resolve the factory from it:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="471f21f2-2c66-4f2d-8315-5fa527522d44"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.ElementsFactory;

Element existingElement = ...;
ElementsFactory elementsFactory = ElementsFactory.get(existingElement);  ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Example: Creating Elements</strong></h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aedc3d65-d3fe-4e84-859f-d2693ed1ba74"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.ElementsFactory;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Namespace;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Package;

Project project = ...;

// Obtain factory
ElementsFactory elementsFactory = ElementsFactory.get(project);

// Create elements
Namespace namespace = elementsFactory.createNamespace();
Package pkg = elementsFactory.createPackage();

// Attach elements to the model
pkg.setOwner(namespace); ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2><strong>Key Points</strong></h2><ul><li data-uuid="64eec9db-d031-4696-8cfc-9e4c7398689c">The factory is <strong>project-scoped</strong> — always obtain it from the correct project or element.</li><li data-uuid="75017e4f-349e-451e-9d8c-bde07c69253a"><code>create&lt;Type&gt;()</code> methods only <strong>instantiate elements</strong>; they do not place them in the model.</li><li data-uuid="c4b53933-99c5-4b9f-9e29-1a0240bd83cb">To make an element part of the model, you must assign ownership (for example, using <code>setOwner(...)</code>).</li><li data-uuid="3f335940-66ea-4cbb-8a68-43bb303cc6e2">Ownership operations may create underlying relationships automatically (for example, memberships), depending on the metamodel semantics.</li></ul>
````

## PAGE 00163: (2026x Refresh1) New Relationship Creation

- page_id: `303989524`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989524/2026x+Refresh1+New+Relationship+Creation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:40.139+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 > (2026x Refresh1) Working With Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Relationships such as a [Dependency](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Dependency.html) are created using the *[ElementsFactory](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/ElementsFactory.html)*, then added to an appropriate owner to ensure proper containment in the model. Once created, the relationship is configured by linking its ends—setting one element as the client and another as the supplier—thereby establishing a connection between two model elements.

The client and supplier properties are accessed using getter methods like *[Relationships.getSource](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Relationships.html#getSource(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Relationship))(relationship)* and *Relationships.**[getTarget](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Relationships.html#getTarget(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Relationship))(relationship)*. The client and supplier properties can be set using the methods in Relationships class: *[Relationships.setSource](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Relationships.html#setSource(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Relationship,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element))(relationship,client)*and *[Relationships.setTarget](https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Relationships.html#setTarget(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Relationship,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element))(relationship,supplier)*.

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Relationships such as a <a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/kerml/Dependency.html">Dependency</a> are created using the <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/sysml/model/ElementsFactory.html">ElementsFactory</a></em>, then added to an appropriate owner to ensure proper containment in the model. Once created, the relationship is configured by linking its ends—setting one element as the client and another as the supplier—thereby establishing a connection between two model elements.</p><p>The client and supplier properties are accessed using getter methods like <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Relationships.html#getSource(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Relationship)">Relationships.getSource</a>(relationship)</em> and <em>Relationships.</em><em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Relationships.html#getTarget(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Relationship)">getTarget</a>(relationship)</em>. The client and supplier properties can be set using the methods in Relationships class: <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Relationships.html#setSource(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Relationship,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element)">Relationships.setSource</a>(relationship,client) </em>and <em><a href="https://jdocs.nomagic.com/2026xRefresh1/com/dassault_systemes/modeler/kerml/model/Relationships.html#setTarget(com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Relationship,com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Element)">Relationships.setTarget</a>(relationship,supplier)</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ca1051f-6bbd-43d5-938c-93b4e039780d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.Relationships;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Dependency;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Namespace;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.model.kerml.Package;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.ElementsFactory;
import com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.sysml.PartDefinition;


// Create elements
ElementsFactory elementsFactory = ElementsFactory.get(project);

Package dronePkg = elementsFactory.createPackage();
dronePkg.setDeclaredName("Drone System");

PartDefinition flightController = elementsFactory.createPartDefinition();
flightController.setDeclaredName("Flight Controller");

PartDefinition gpsModule = elementsFactory.createPartDefinition();
gpsModule.setDeclaredName("GPS Module");

// Add parts to package
flightController.setOwner(dronePkg);
gpsModule.setOwner(dronePkg);

// Create a generic Dependency
Dependency fcDependsOnGps = elementsFactory.createDependency();
fcDependsOnGps.setDeclaredName("FC depends on GPS");

// Add dependency to package (owner)
fcDependsOnGps.setOwner(dronePkg);

// Connect the two PartDefinitions
Relationships.setSource(fcDependsOnGps,flightController);
Relationships.setTarget(fcDependsOnGps,gpsModule);
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00164: (2026x Refresh1) Notification of presentation element draw

- page_id: `303988984`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303988984/2026x+Refresh1+Notification+of+presentation+element+draw
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:11.349+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Diagram events
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

*com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.SymbolDrawNotification* notifies when the presentation element is drawn (added to a diagram):

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.SymbolDrawNotification</em> notifies when the presentation element is drawn (added to a diagram):</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="efd26006-93c1-44dc-b100-15ef466ace73"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    // An element removal listener
    final PropertyChangeListener removeListener = new PropertyChangeListener()
    {
        public void propertyChange(PropertyChangeEvent evt)
        { 
            if (ExtendedPropertyNames.REMOVE.equals(evt.getPropertyName()))
            {
                // symbol removed
            }
        }
    };
 
    // An element drawing listener
    SymbolDrawListener symbolDrawListener = new SymbolDrawListener()
    {
        public void symbolAdded(DiagramPresentationElement diagram, PresentationElement symbol, String actionID)
        {
            // A symbol added
            // A register listener
            symbol.addPropertyChangeListener(removeListener);
        }
    };
    
    // A register draw listener
    SymbolDrawNotification symbolDrawNotification =SymbolDrawNotification.getSymbolDrawNotification(project);
    symbolDrawNotification.addSymbolDrawListener(symbolDrawListener);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989515 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00165: (2026x Refresh1) OAuth 2.0 authentication

- page_id: `303989515`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989515/2026x+Refresh1+OAuth+2.0+authentication
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:39.792+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) REST APIs > (2026x Refresh1) Authentication
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

5

Other web applications can integrate with Authentication server through OAuth 2.0 protocol to authenticate users and access Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of those users.

Authentication server behaves as OAuth 2.0 Authorization Server. Web applications that need access to Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of an authenticated user should implement OAuth 2.0 Client functionality according to OAuth 2.0 protocol specification.

### OAuth 2.0 integration

OAuth 2.0 specification

Authentication server provides a JSON with all endpoints required for the integration. This JSON can be retrieved using *https://<server_host>:8443/authentication/.well-known/oauth2.0-configuration* endpoint. This JSON also returns supported response types and grant types.

Example JSON:

```bash

```

Authentication server supports the following authorization grants:

- Authorization code
- Implicit
- Resource Owner Password Credentials
- Device Code (OAuth 2.0 extension, see OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant for more information)

### OAuth 2.0 client registration

To register OAuth 2.0 clients:

1. 
  1. **Use the web UI:** go to the **Settings**app of Web Application Platform and search for **OAuth 2.0**tab in the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing OAuth client keys' space='MCS']**OAuth clients******section.
  2. **Use *registration_endpoint* API:** (see the example JSON). 
Example client registration request when using API:

```bash

```

Limitations of the client registration endpoint:

- You cannot provide supported grant types or response types – default options are set.
- Authentication server generates the client secret automatically.

However, you can update all of these attributes any time in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing OAuth client keys' space='MCS']******section of the **Settings**app (i.e. using the web UI).

### Teamwork Cloud REST API access

After the OAuth 2.0 client gets an access token after user authentication, it can call Teamwork Cloud REST API endpoints by providing the following header:

```text
]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4deecac1-fe49-4c9f-84b0-27c064f2168c"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">5</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span>Other web applications can integrate with Authentication server through OAuth 2.0 protocol to authenticate users and access Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of those users.</span></p><p><span>Authentication server behaves as OAuth 2.0 Authorization Server. Web applications that need access to Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of an authenticated user should implement OAuth 2.0 Client functionality according to OAuth 2.0 protocol specification.</span></p><h1><span style="font-size: 24.0px;letter-spacing: -0.01em;">OAuth 2.0 integration</span></h1><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7784a74b-75b6-4997-9a8b-3c40f14c492f"><ac:rich-text-body>See <a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749">OAuth 2.0 specification</a> for more details.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>Authentication server provides a JSON with all endpoints required for the integration. This JSON can be retrieved using <em>https://&lt;server_host&gt;:8443/authentication/.well-known/oauth2.0-configuration</em> endpoint. This JSON also returns supported response types and grant types.</span></p><p>Example JSON:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf15d67d-779a-4d39-8131-3351b1520d4b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{

  "response_types_supported": [

    "token",

    "code"

  ],

  "device_authorization_endpoint": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/api/oauth2/device_code",

  "jwks_uri": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/jwks.json",

  "grant_types_supported": [

    "urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code",

    "refresh_token",

    "password",

    "authorization_code"

  ],

  "registration_endpoint": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/api/oauth2/register",

  "token_endpoint_auth_methods_supported": [

    "client_secret_basic"

  ],

  "scopes_supported": [],

  "issuer": "https://localhost:8443/authentication",

  "authorization_endpoint": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/oauth2/authorize",

  "token_endpoint": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/api/oauth2/token"

}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span>Authentication server supports the following authorization grants:</span></p><ul><li><span>Authorization code </span></li><li><span>Implicit</span></li><li><span>Resource Owner Password Credentials</span></li><li><span>Device Code (OAuth 2.0 extension, see </span><a href="https://oauth.net/2/grant-types/device-code/">OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant</a><span> for more information)</span></li></ul><p><span> </span></p><h1>OAuth 2.0 client registration</h1><p><span>To register OAuth 2.0 clients:</span></p><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ol><li><span><strong>Use the web UI:</strong> go to the <strong>Settings </strong>app of Web Application Platform and search for <strong>OAuth 2.0 </strong>tab in the<em> </em><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Managing OAuth client keys" /><ac:link-body><strong>OAuth clients</strong><strong> </strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>section.</span></li><li><span><strong>Use <em>registration_endpoint</em> API:</strong> (see the example JSON).<br />Example client registration request when using API:</span></li></ol></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c55467c-6f7f-40d2-b3d7-f9cb12e1a9ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[POST https://localhost:8443/authentication/api/oauth2/register

BODY:

{

           client_name: “Client name”,

           redirect_uris: [“redirect uri”] // comma separated URIs

}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e29a054-c385-40f2-8c3d-5088c5c7cd68"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Limitations of the client registration endpoint:</p><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">You cannot provide supported grant types or response types – default options are set.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Authentication server generates the client secret automatically.</span></li></ul><p>However, you can update all of these attributes any time in the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Managing OAuth client keys" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OAuth clients]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong><strong> </strong>section of the <strong>Settings </strong>app (i.e. using the web UI).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h1><span style="font-size: 24.0px;letter-spacing: -0.01em;">Teamwork Cloud REST API access</span></h1><p><span>After the OAuth 2.0 client gets an access token after user authentication, it can call Teamwork Cloud REST API endpoints by providing the following header:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f58c24d-34d3-4d65-9691-930fbebf89d4"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Authorization: Bearer <access token>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989520 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00166: (2026x Refresh1) Open API Deprecation Policy

- page_id: `303989520`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989520/2026x+Refresh1+Open+API+Deprecation+Policy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:39.982+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Overview:** This policy outlines the procedures and guidelines for the deprecation of APIs within our system.

**Deprecation Process:**

1. **Notification:** APIs that are deprecated will be clearly marked as such in the API javadoc.
2. **Timeline:** Deprecated APIs can be removed in the next major version following their deprecation. For instance, if an API is marked as deprecated in version 2024x (or any 2024x RefreshX), it can be removed in version 2026x.
3. **Replacement APIs:** Deprecated APIs will have replacement APIs provided by the development team. Users are encouraged to migrate to the replacement APIs to ensure continued compatibility and support.

**Exceptional Cases:** In exceptional cases during major releases, Open APIs may be removed without prior deprecation. This may occur due to significant changes in data structures or profile modifications. While such instances are rare, users should be aware that, in exceptional cases, Open APIs may be subject to removal without prior notice.

**Review:** This policy will be periodically reviewed and updated to align with evolving technological and business requirements. Any changes to the policy will be communicated to relevant stakeholders in a timely manner.

**Effective Version:** This API deprecation policy is effective as of version 2024x Refresh1.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

For instance, if an API is marked as deprecated <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="833d9cd5-af83-4d17-a89f-47dc8eb0c173">in version 2024x <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(or any 2024x RefreshX)</span>, it can be removed in version 2026x.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></li><li><p><strong>Replacement APIs:</strong> Deprecated APIs will have replacement APIs provided by the development team. Users are encouraged to migrate to the replacement APIs to ensure continued compatibility and support.</p></li></ol><p><strong>Exceptional Cases:</strong> In exceptional cases during major releases, Open APIs may be removed without prior deprecation. This may occur due to significant changes in data structures or profile modifications. While such instances are rare, users should be aware that, in exceptional cases, Open APIs may be subject to removal without prior notice.</p><p><strong>Review:</strong> This policy will be periodically reviewed and updated to align with evolving technological and business requirements. Any changes to the policy will be communicated to relevant stakeholders in a timely manner.</p><p><strong>Effective Version:</strong> This API deprecation policy is effective as of version 2024x Refresh1.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989504 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00167: (2026x Refresh1) OpenID Connect authentication

- page_id: `303989504`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989504/2026x+Refresh1+OpenID+Connect+authentication
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:39.423+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) REST APIs > (2026x Refresh1) Authentication
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

5

Other web applications can integrate with Authentication server through OpenID Connect (further referred to as OIDC) protocol to authenticate users and access Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of these users.

Authentication server behaves as the OIDC Identity Provider. Web applications that need to access Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of an authenticated user should implement OIDC Service Provider functionality according to OIDC protocol specification.

### OpenID Connect integration

OpenID Connect specification

Authentication server provides a JSON with all endpoints required for the integration. This JSON can be retrieved using *https://<server_host>:8443/authentication/.well-known/oidc-configuration* endpoint. This JSON also returns supported response types and grant types.

Example JSON:

```bash

```

Authentication server supports the following authorization flows:

- Authorization code flow
- Implicit flow
- Hybrid flow
- Device code flow (extension, see [Device Code Flow - Authorization](https://developers.onelogin.com/openid-connect/api/device-code-auth) for more information)

### OIDC client registration

To register OIDC clients:

1. 
  1. **Use the web UI:** go to the **Settings**app of Web Application Platform and search for **OpenID Connect**tab in the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing OAuth client keys' space='MCS']**OAuth clients******section.
  2. **Use *registration_endpoint* API:** (see the example JSON). Example client registration request when using API:

```bash

```

Limitations of the client registration endpoint:

- You cannot provide supported grant types or response types – default options are set.
- Authentication server generates the client secret automatically.

However, you can update all of these attributes any time in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing OAuth client keys' space='MCS']******section of the **Settings**app (i.e. using the web UI).

### Teamwork Cloud REST API access

After the OIDC client gets ID token after user authentication, it can call Teamwork Cloud REST API endpoints by providing the following header:

```bash
]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fcd0e526-e8d6-48ce-820a-b72fe5ab9e9f"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">5</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span>Other web applications can integrate with Authentication server through OpenID Connect (further referred to as OIDC) protocol to authenticate users and access Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of these users.</span></p><p><span>Authentication server behaves as the OIDC Identity Provider. Web applications that need to access Teamwork Cloud REST API on behalf of an authenticated user should implement OIDC Service Provider functionality according to OIDC protocol specification.</span></p><h1><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">OpenID Connect integration</span></h1><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0fd1d489-dd1c-459e-8130-59146fd3c2b7"><ac:rich-text-body>See <a href="https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-basic-1_0.html">OpenID Connect specification</a> for more details.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>Authentication server provides a JSON with all endpoints required for the integration. This JSON can be retrieved using <em>https://&lt;server_host&gt;:8443/authentication/.well-known/oidc-configuration</em> endpoint. This JSON also returns supported response types and grant types.</span></p><p><span>Example JSON:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6549693e-eb4b-400e-9f3c-45d90018e297"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
  "response_types_supported": [
    "code token",
    "code id_token",
    "code",
    "id_token",
    "id_token token",
    "code id_token token"
  ],
  "device_authorization_endpoint": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/api/oidc/device_code",
  "jwks_uri": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/jwks.json",
  "grant_types_supported": [
    "urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code",
    "refresh_token",
    "password",
    "authorization_code"
  ],
  "subject_types_supported": [
    "public"
  ],
  "id_token_signing_alg_values_supported": [
    "RS256"
  ],
  "registration_endpoint": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/api/oidc/register",
  "token_endpoint_auth_methods_supported": [
    "client_secret_basic"
  ],
  "scopes_supported": [
    "openid"
  ],
  "issuer": "https://localhost:8443/authentication",
  "authorization_endpoint": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/oidc/authorize",
  "token_endpoint": "https://localhost:8443/authentication/api/oidc/token"
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span> </span></p><p><span>Authentication server supports the following authorization flows:</span></p><ul><li><span>Authorization code flow</span></li><li><span>Implicit flow</span></li><li><span>Hybrid flow</span></li><li><span>Device code flow (extension, see <span style="color: rgb(28,31,42);"><a href="https://developers.onelogin.com/openid-connect/api/device-code-auth">Device Code Flow - Authorization</a></span> for more information)</span></li></ul><h1><span style="font-size: 24.0px;letter-spacing: -0.01em;">OIDC client registration</span></h1><p><span>To register OIDC clients:</span></p><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ol><li><span><strong>Use the web UI:</strong> go to the <strong>Settings </strong>app of Web Application Platform and search for <strong>OpenID Connect </strong>tab in the<em> </em><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Managing OAuth client keys" /><ac:link-body><strong>OAuth clients</strong><strong> </strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>section.</span></li><li><span><strong>Use <em>registration_endpoint</em> API:</strong> (see the example JSON).<br /></span>Example client registration request when using API:</li></ol></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21093e5c-a719-472e-8bc9-b59774e0bbc0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[POST https://localhost:8443/authentication/api/oidc/register

BODY:

{

           client_name: “Client name”,

           redirect_uris: [“redirect uri”] // comma separated URIs

}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e1fe551d-0a65-4ad7-9275-0e44721b67a9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Limitations of the client registration endpoint:</p><ul><li><span>You cannot provide supported grant types or response types – default options are set.</span></li><li><span>Authentication server generates the client secret automatically.</span></li></ul><p>However, you can update all of these attributes any time in the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Managing OAuth client keys" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OAuth clients]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong><strong> </strong>section of the <strong>Settings </strong>app (i.e. using the web UI).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h1><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Teamwork Cloud REST API access</span></h1><p><span>After the OIDC client gets ID token after user authentication, it can call Teamwork Cloud REST API endpoints by providing the following header:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d73af696-184e-4ad8-ade6-123651fb8611"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Authorization: Token <ID token>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989282 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00168: (2026x Refresh1) Oracle DDL generation and customization

- page_id: `303989282`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989282/2026x+Refresh1+Oracle+DDL+generation+and+customization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:28.751+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Oracle DDL script generation is based on the Velocity engine. It provides an ability to change the generated DDL script by changing the velocity template. We explain how the Oracle DDL generation works in our modeling tools and how to change the template for specific elements.

Knowledge of the Velocity Template Language is necessary for understanding, editing, or creating templates. The Velocity documentation can be downloaded from [http://click.apache.org/docs/velocity/VelocityUsersGuide.pdf](http://click.apache.org/docs/velocity/VelocityUsersGuide.pdf).

For more information about Oracle DDL 11g, see [http://docs.oracle.com/cd/E25054_01/server.1111/e25789/sqllangu.htm](http://docs.oracle.com/cd/E25054_01/server.1111/e25789/sqllangu.htm).

The modeling tool Oracle DDL generation consists of the following three components:

- The Velocity engine. Oracle DDL generation is performed by the Velocity engine. The engine collects context variables and mergers them into a template.
- The template. The template is a user-defined document that provides the Velocity Template Language (VTL) syntax. The VTL syntax is used to manipulate the context variables for generating the text script. The template file can be changed in the CG Properties Editor dialog box (in the Oracle DDL set shortcut menu, choose the Properties command). The default Oracle DDL template is stored in < program installation folder>\ data\DB_engineering\Oracle_template folder.
- Context variables. Context variables are models (retrieved from OpenAPI), code engineering set information, and user-defined variables.

To identify the Oracle object, check applied Stereotypes and Tagged Values.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

The Velocity documentation can be downloaded from <a href="http://click.apache.org/docs/velocity/VelocityUsersGuide.pdf">http://click.apache.org/docs/velocity/VelocityUsersGuide.pdf</a>. </p><p>For more information about Oracle DDL 11g, see <a href="http://docs.oracle.com/cd/E25054_01/server.1111/e25789/sqllangu.htm">http://docs.oracle.com/cd/E25054_01/server.1111/e25789/sqllangu.htm</a>.</p><p>The modeling tool Oracle DDL generation consists of the following three components:</p><ul><li> The Velocity engine. Oracle DDL generation is performed by the Velocity engine. The engine collects context variables and mergers them into a template.</li><li>The template. The template is a user-defined document that provides the Velocity Template Language (VTL) syntax. The VTL syntax is used to manipulate the context variables for generating the text script. The template file can be changed in the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">CG Properties Editor </strong>dialog box (in the Oracle DDL set shortcut menu, choose the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Properties </strong>command). The default Oracle DDL template is stored in &lt;<em>program </em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">installation folder&gt;\</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">data\DB_engineering\Oracle_template </em>folder.</li><li>Context variables. Context variables are models (retrieved from OpenAPI), code engineering set information, and user-defined variables.</li></ul><p>To identify the Oracle object, check applied Stereotypes and Tagged Values.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25b2885a-064a-4fc0-bac0-35a022134e4b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="03a84b5e-cf4a-4758-b490-c4d3fa411bf6"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989214 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00169: (2026x Refresh1) OSLC API

- page_id: `303989214`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989214/2026x+Refresh1+OSLC+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:26.305+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Since version 2022x Refresh2, Teamwork Cloud OSLC API implementation has been moved to the Web Application Platform, resulting in a change of port and API URI patterns:

- OSLC API no longer uses port 8111 but relies on port 8443, as well as other Web Application Platform applications.
- OSLC root services document URI can be found using the following pattern - http(s)://WEBAPP_IP:PORT/oslc/api/rootservices .
- Each of the model elements is exposed using the following URI pattern - http(s)://WEBAPP_IP:PORT/oslc/api/oslc/am/{projectID}/{elementID} . Note that the elementID here is an Element Server ID.

**Note regarding migration**

The old pre-2022x Refresh2 root services URI and element URIs will be automatically redirected to the new URIs via HTTP 301 redirect. After migrating to 2022x Refresh2, you must set the WAP URI value in the *server*field of the Teamwork Cloud *esi.oslc* configuration block in the *application.conf* file.

Teamwork Cloud has OSLC support and exposes model element data following [OSLC Architecture Management (AM)](http://open-services.net/ns/am) and [OSLC Configuration Management](https://archive.open-services.net/wiki/configuration-management/index.html)vocabularies. Along with core OSLC services provided in Teamwork Cloud, this enables smooth integration with other OSLC-compatible tools by linking resources in Linked Data fashion.

We expose the following model element properties:

- rdf:type ( http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type ) - represents architecture resource type, which according to OSLC AM vocabulary is always a Resource ( http://open-services.net/ns/am#Resource ).
- dcterms:modified ( http://purl.org/dc/terms/modified ) - stands for the last element modification date.
- dcterms:identifier ( http://purl.org/dc/terms/identifier ) - the Element Server ID as used in element's URI pattern.
- dcterms:title ( http://purl.org/dc/terms/title ) - the name of the element

```xml
May 16, 2018 2:08:52 PM
    50775427-bce2-4de4-b747-8ab82d294237
    Engine
  
]]>
```

- Currently, querying services are not supported.
- Model data can only be read through OSLC services, and editing capabilities are not supported.
- We offer OSLC UI previews through integration with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud' space='MCS'] . For this, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing from graphical user interface' space='MCS'] .
- We provide OSLC delegated dialog support via Selection Dialog service for navigating the model tree and linking to AM resources from external tools.

##### OSLC Configuration Management Support

OSLC Configuration Management support makes Teamwork Cloud OSLC services configuration-aware. This enables Teamwork Cloud resource discovery at the branch or tagged commit levels. The entry point for discovery of OSLC Configuration Management services is via the *<oslc_config:cmServiceProviders>* property. Teamwork Cloud concepts map to OSLC Configuration Management concepts in the following way:

| Teamwork Cloud concept | OSLC Configuration Management concept |
| --- | --- |
| Cameo Model (.Master Resource) | Component |
| Branch | Stream |
| Tagged Commit | Baseline |
| Used Project (Branch) | Contribution of a Stream to a Stream |

##### **OAuth 1.0a authentication**

To ensure secure access to server resources via OSLC, OAuth 1.0a authentication protocol is used.

OAuth 1.0a requires consumer key and secret to be known before starting the authentication process flow. These can be created through Teamwork CloudAdmin as described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing OAuth client keys' space='MCS'].

Alternatively, a pair of consumer key and secret can be generated manually via a service exposed in the root services document. The following HTTP POST request should be made to a consumer key generation service (jfs:oauthRequestConsumerKeyUrl):

```js

```

```js

```

Note, that keys generated following this approach will still need to be[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing OAuth client keys' space='MCS']approved by an administrator through Teamwork CloudAdmin.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29e5060b-9718-47f8-8cd7-1427d5129831"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Since version 2022x Refresh2, Teamwork Cloud OSLC API implementation has been moved to the Web Application Platform, resulting in a change of port and API URI patterns:</span></p><ul><li>OSLC API no longer uses port 8111 but relies on port 8443, as well as other Web Application Platform applications.</li><li>OSLC root services document URI can be found using the following pattern - <em>http(s)://WEBAPP_IP:PORT/oslc/api/rootservices</em>.</li><li>Each of the model elements is exposed using the following URI pattern - <em>http(s)://WEBAPP_IP:PORT/oslc/api/oslc/am/{projectID}/{elementID}</em>.<br />Note that the <em>elementID</em> here is an Element Server ID.</li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Note regarding migration</strong></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The old pre-2022x Refresh2 root services URI and element URIs will be automatically redirected to the new URIs via HTTP 301 redirect. After migrating to 2022x Refresh2, you must set the WAP URI value in the <em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f3ee46c6-ba1f-49b4-957f-b64ea1c9fea5">server</ac:inline-comment-marker> </em>field of the Teamwork Cloud <em>esi.oslc</em> configuration block in the <em>application.conf</em> file.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1fc8fdab-1c2c-4ca3-9015-33b7f61ad136">Teamwork</ac:inline-comment-marker> Cloud has OSLC support and exposes model element data following <a href="http://open-services.net/ns/am">OSLC Architecture Management (AM)</a> and <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><a href="https://archive.open-services.net/wiki/configuration-management/index.html">OSLC Configuration Management</a><span> </span></span>vocabularies. Along with core OSLC services provided in Teamwork Cloud, this enables smooth integration with other OSLC-compatible tools by linking resources in Linked Data fashion.</span></p><p>We expose the following model element properties:</p><ul><li>rdf:type (<a href="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type">http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type</a>) - represents architecture resource type, which according to OSLC AM vocabulary is always a Resource (<a href="http://open-services.net/ns/am#Resource">http://open-services.net/ns/am#Resource</a>).</li><li>dcterms:modified (<a href="http://purl.org/dc/terms/modified">http://purl.org/dc/terms/modified</a>) - stands for the last element modification date.</li><li>dcterms:identifier (<a href="http://purl.org/dc/terms/identifier">http://purl.org/dc/terms/identifier</a>) - the Element Server ID as used in element's URI pattern.</li><li>dcterms:title (<a href="http://purl.org/dc/terms/title">http://purl.org/dc/terms/title</a>) - the name of the element<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1f154bf0-5a5c-4311-9f23-091bc174fbe7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">xml</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample RDF/XML representation of element data</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<rdf:RDF
    xmlns:oslc="http://open-services.net/ns/core#"    
    xmlns:oslc_am="http://open-services.net/ns/am#" 
    xmlns:dc="http://purl.org/dc/terms/"
    xmlns:dcterms="http://purl.org/dc/terms/"  
    xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
    xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#">
  <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://localhost:8443/oslc/api/oslc/am/b1acff2d-4396-4314-9dba-477d573ede16/50775427-bce2-4de4-b747-8ab82d294237">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://open-services.net/ns/am#Resource"/>
    <oslc:serviceProvider rdf:resource="https://localhost:8443/oslc/api/oslc/am/b1acff2d-4396-4314-9dba-477d573ede16/services"/>
    <dcterms:modified rdf:datatype="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime">May 16, 2018 2:08:52 PM</dcterms:modified>
    <dcterms:identifier rdf:datatype="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string">50775427-bce2-4de4-b747-8ab82d294237</dcterms:identifier>
    <dcterms:title rdf:parseType="Literal">Engine</dcterms:title>
  </rdf:Description>
</rdf:RDF>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ul><li><p>Currently, querying services are not supported.</p></li><li><p>Model data can only be read through OSLC services, and editing capabilities are not supported.</p></li><li>We offer OSLC UI previews through integration with <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud" /></ac:link>. For this, you need to <ac:link ac:anchor="Publishing other types of resources"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Publishing from graphical user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[publish an OSLC resource]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">We provide OSLC delegated dialog support via Selection Dialog service for navigating the model tree and linking to AM resources from external tools.</span></li></ul><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">OSLC <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Configuration Management Support</span></span></h3><p>OSLC Configuration Management support makes Teamwork Cloud OSLC services configuration-aware. This enables Teamwork Cloud resource discovery at the branch or tagged commit levels. The entry point for discovery of <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="bb2c04dc-ef20-41c5-9764-299b17fab63d">OSLC Configuration Management services</ac:inline-comment-marker> is via the <em>&lt;oslc_config:cmServiceProviders&gt;</em> property. Teamwork Cloud concepts map to OSLC Configuration Management concepts in the following way:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 47.1682%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 46.5384%;" /><col style="width: 53.5495%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: center;">Teamwork Cloud concept</th><th style="text-align: center;">OSLC Configuration Management concept</th></tr><tr><td>Cameo Model (.Master Resource)</td><td>Component</td></tr><tr><td>Branch</td><td>Stream</td></tr><tr><td>Tagged Commit</td><td>Baseline</td></tr><tr><td>Used Project (Branch)</td><td>Contribution of a Stream to a Stream</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4d1be4db-c9ac-49ef-8221-e21e5639b4b7">OAuth 1.0a authentication</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><br /></strong></h3><p>To ensure secure access to server resources via OSLC, OAuth 1.0a authentication protocol is used.</p><p>OAuth 1.0a requires consumer key and secret to be known before starting the authentication process flow. These can be created through <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Teamwork Cloud </span>Admin as described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Managing OAuth client keys" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Alternatively, a pair of consumer key and secret can be generated manually via a service exposed in the root services document. The following HTTP POST request should be made to a consumer key generation service (jfs:oauthRequestConsumerKeyUrl):<br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81dd2474-b1c0-4b12-9130-db9c5401d9dd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">js</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
  "name": "consumerNameGoesHere",
  "secret": "OAuthSecretGoesHere"
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a2e2db9-97a7-4b9d-98f6-073474ff885b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">js</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
  "key": "generatedConsumerKeyShouldBeHere"
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Note, that keys generated following this approach will <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="bd78a089-e091-4aa8-8802-67266a59924c">still need to be </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Managing OAuth client keys" /><ac:link-body><span class="inline-comment-marker">approved</span> by an administrator</ac:link-body></ac:link> through <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Teamwork Cloud </span>Admin.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989311 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00170: (2026x Refresh1) Parametric Helper as executing parametric simulation from an Activity

- page_id: `303989311`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989311/2026x+Refresh1+Parametric+Helper+as+executing+parametric+simulation+from+an+Activity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:30.204+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 > (2026x Refresh1) Alf Plugin Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x Refresh1) Java APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Parametric engine provides an API for a parametric execution with a runtime object of a Classifier. The runtime object of the Classifier will be passed to the API as an argument and the engine will execute the given object. With this API, you can use a scripting language to execute the parametric simulation, shown below:

```text

```

An argument object is a runtime object of a classifier to be executed. To obtain this particular runtime object, you can use some UML actions, e.g., ReadSelfAction, ReadStructuralFeatureValueAction, ValueSpecificationAction, or the Cameo Simulation Toolkit Open API. The following figure shows the Parametric Activity diagram in the *CylinderPipe.mdzip* sample. The **action : ExecuteParametric** is used to run the parametric execution. The runtime object, which will be executed, is obtained from the value Specification Action rawMaterialSupplier.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Parametric Activity diagram in the CylinderPipe.mdzip sample.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Parametric engine provides an API for a parametric execution with a runtime object of a Classifier. The runtime object of the Classifier will be passed to the API as an argument and the engine will execute the given object. With this API, you can use a scripting language to execute the parametric simulation, shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149034b5-c6af-4ed6-a2ae-39a65ae247a4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.parametrics.utility.ParametricsHelper.executeObject(Object object);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>An argument object is a runtime object of a classifier to be executed. To obtain this particular runtime object, you can use some UML actions, e.g., ReadSelfAction, ReadStructuralFeatureValueAction, ValueSpecificationAction, or the Cameo Simulation Toolkit Open API. The following figure shows the Parametric Activity diagram in the <em>CylinderPipe.mdzip</em> sample. The <strong>action : ExecuteParametric</strong> is used to run the parametric execution. The runtime object, which will be executed, is obtained from the value Specification Action rawMaterialSupplier.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="567" ac:width="594"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddavfe88aed5c24cf68dcde9f46c1f9f6e1a.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Parametric Helper as executing parametric simulation from an Activity" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"> The Parametric Activity diagram in the CylinderPipe.mdzip sample.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989095 space=DEVG version=1 -->
## PAGE 00171: (2026x Refresh1) Paths

- page_id: `303989095`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989095/2026x+Refresh1+Paths
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T09:44:18.389+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) UML-based > (2026x Refresh1) Working with diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Presentation elements (symbols)
- labels: ['supplier', 'client', 'suplier-piont', 'client-point', 'break-point']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

706205016

INLINE

706205019

INLINE

706205017

INLINE

Paths are presentation elements created for such model elements as Relationships. Path has the following attributes:

- Supplier – the presentation element of a Relationship supplier. Use method com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.paths.PathElement.getSupplier() to access this element.
- Client – the presentation element of a Relationship client. Use method PathElement.getClient() to access this element.
- Supplier point – the connection point of a path and supplier element. Use method PathElement.getSupplierPoint() to access this point.
- Client point – the connection point of a path and client element. Use method PathElement.getClientPoint() to access this point.
- Break points – a list of path breaking points between supplier and client points. This list can be empty if path is straight. Use method PathElement.getBreakPoints() to access this list.

706205015

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with paths and relationships' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying paths' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd12a4b0-5699-4f8c-ae5a-d3d977d4e690"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">706205016</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb925210-d1f1-45de-aea8-4fb77133e2ae"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">706205019</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d663fe2c-b2ad-4913-8213-d8906aa70e09"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">706205017</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Paths are presentation elements created for such model elements as Relationships. Path has the following attributes:</p><ul><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Supplier </strong>– the presentation element of a Relationship supplier. Use method <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.paths.PathElement.getSupplier()</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </em>to access this element.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Client </strong>– the presentation element of a Relationship client. Use method <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">PathElement.getClient()</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </em>to access this element.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Supplier point </strong>– the connection point of a path and supplier element. Use method <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">PathElement.getSupplierPoint()</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </em>to access this point.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Client point </strong>– the connection point of a path and client element. Use method <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">PathElement.getClientPoint()</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </em>to access this point.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Break points </strong>– a list of path breaking points between supplier and client points. This list can be empty if path is straight. Use method <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">PathElement.getBreakPoints()</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </em>to access this list.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="115d9bc9-c9eb-4e34-87df-77ee281760c5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">706205015</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Working with paths and relationships" /></ac:link></li><li class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Displaying paths" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989466 space=DEVG version=2 -->
## PAGE 00172: (2026x Refresh1) Personal access token authentication sample for Teamwork Cloud REST API

- page_id: `303989466`
- space_key: `DEVG`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG/pages/303989466/2026x+Refresh1+Personal+access+token+authentication+sample+for+Teamwork+Cloud+REST+API
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-06-22T12:18:32.312+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Developer Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Developer Guides Home > (2026x Refresh1) Teamwork Cloud Developer Guide > (2026x Refresh1) REST APIs > (2026x Refresh1) Authentication
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To use Teamwork Cloud REST API withpersonal access token authentication

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating a personal access token' space='MCS'].
2. Copy the token and use it in the HTTP header attribute to perform REST API calls. The token is used in an Authorization header with the **Token** Type.

```text

```

#### NOTE: Token expiration and validation

Token expiration and validation

The personal access token does not have an expiration date, so use it with caution and ensure it is adequately protected.

To invalidate the personal access token, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating a personal access token' space='MCS'].

The personal access token should be regenerated after the SSL certificate is updated for Web Application Platform. This is because the authentication server uses the certificate for personal access token signing and later for token signature validation.
